# Accounts and pricing

# Accounts and pricing

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

---

# Am I able to set a cap for monthly spending? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/can-i-set-up-a-cap-for-monthly-spending/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Mapbox does not provide the ability to cap your monthly billing. For now, you can create predictable monthly API usage is by monitoring your [account statistics](https://www.mapbox.com/account/statistics)
 and your [invoices](https://www.mapbox.com/account/invoices)
.

Once your account statistics and/or invoices reach a level where you'd like to be capped, you can:

1.  [Delete](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/dive-deeper/access-tokens/#deleting-tokens)
     your [access tokens](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
     from your tokens page (deactivating any mapping implementations that were authenticated by that access token). Or,
2.  Remove your access tokens from your code. Where you see our map initialization code block occurs depends on your implementation. You can temporarily remove your access tokens so that no traffic authenticates.
3.  When you're ready to enable map traffic again, you can add the access token back into your map code will reactivate the maps.

For more information about your account statistics or invoices, you can explore our [Accounts Documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/)
.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# Does Mapbox provide refunds? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/does-mapbox-provide-refunds/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Yes, Mapbox does provide refunds under certain conditions.

If Mapbox has issued a refund, the funds will be returned to the account's original payment method 5-10 business days after the refund has been processed. Individual Mapbox invoices will not be reflected to show refunds. If you require additional receipts for the refund, you can reach out to Mapbox Support team [with the necessary details about your invoice](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request)
.

Additionally, if sales tax has been applied to your invoice, but you can provide valid exemption documentation covering the purchase period, you can get a refund on the sales tax part of your payment if you contact **[accountsreceivable@mapbox.com](mailto:accountsreceivable@mapbox.com)
 within 30 days of the invoice date**.

After 30 days from the invoice date, you will need to contact your local state tax authority to process a refund.

Note that prepaid credit added to your account for future Mapbox services cannot be refunded in case it's no longer needed. The prepaid credit will be applied to future invoices on a recurring basis until the credit is $0.00. Mapbox won't charge the credit card on file until the account's prepaid balance is $0.00.

Refer to our documentation for more details:

*   [Request a sales tax refund](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#request-a-sales-tax-refund)
    
*   [Prepaid credit](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#prepaid-credit)
    

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# How can I add an alternate/secondary billing address? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-add-an-alternate-billing-address/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

A [secondary / alternate billing email address](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#secondary-billing-email-addresses)
 is only available for customers with the [Monthly Invoicing](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#monthly-invoicing)
 payment setting. You can request participation in the Monthly Invoicing beta by contacting our Sales team through this form: [https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/)
.

For customers with [Automatic Payments](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#automatic-payments)
 as their payment setting, we recommend the following workarounds to help make sure multiple stakeholders within your organization have access to billing notifications:

1.  Consider creating a group email address like "[company+mapbox@company.com](mailto:company+mapbox@company.com)
    " and adding members to that group to receive notifications.
2.  Consider setting up automatic forwarding rules in your email client to forward any emails with the title **\[Mapbox Billing\] Successful payment receipt** to the recipients of your choosing (like a billing/finance team). They will be able to download the PDF receipt right from the body of the email.

For more information, you can refer to the [Mapbox documentation on invoices](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#monthly-invoicing)
.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# Does Mapbox support Two-factor Authentication (2FA)? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/does-mapbox-support-two-factor-authentication/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Two-factor authentication (2FA), also known as multi-factor authentication (MFA) or two-step authentication, is an optional but recommended security feature for Mapbox accounts. Once enabled, users will be prompted to enter the account's password and a security code generated on registered mobile devices whenever they log in to the account. For more information, refer to the documentation available at [/guides/settings/#two-factor-authentication](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#two-factor-authentication)
.

[To enable 2FA](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#enable-two-factor-authentication)
, you need to be logged into your account and navigate to the Security page. You'll be prompted to scan a barcode with your mobile device or type a code into password management services like 1Password. The recommended authenticator app is Google Authenticator, which is free and available for iOS and Android. For a Windows phone, use the Authenticator app. Your mobile device will display a 6-digit code, which you need to type into the field below the barcode to complete the process.

If you lose your two-factor device, you can use a recovery code to access your account. This code is a single-use code that lets you sign in without your two-factor device. To use the code, you’ll need your username or email and your password.

To use a recovery code, navigate to the Sign-in page, enter your account ID and password, then click Sign in. Click the Lost your mobile device? Link below the Sign-in button. A new field for the recovery code will appear. Enter your recovery code in the new field and click Sign in. Find instructions for using a recovery code in our [Account documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#security)
.

Note that enabling 2FA for an account raises the likelihood that you or other users of the account may have trouble logging into the account in the future. Thus it is recommended to store 2FA recovery codes in a safe place and distribute them to new account owners when transferring ownership of an account.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# How can I delete an account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-delete-an-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

To delete your Mapbox account, follow these steps:

1.  Log into your account and go to your [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
     page.
2.  Scroll to the bottom of the settings page.
3.  Click the **Delete account** button.

Note that account deletion is a permanent action that cannot be undone. Mapbox will not be able to recover your account, and the same account ID (username) cannot be re-used to create another account.

After clicking the button to delete an account, there will be a modal that requires additional confirmation that you do intend to fully delete the account. When an account is deleted, the following are effective immediately:

*   All styles, projects, and data associated with the account are no longer available.
*   The account cannot be logged into.
*   Access tokens tied to that account will be immediately terminated, and map implementations using them will no longer be able to effectively access Mapbox services.

Mapbox will not automatically prorate for any unused services or subscriptions.

If you experience issues while deleting your account or would like to discuss prorated refunds for unused services, contact the Mapbox support team by following [these instructions](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#if-i-cannot-resolve-my-issue-what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request)
.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# How can I change the organization name on an invoice? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-change-the-organization-name-on-an-invoice/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

To change the organization name on your Mapbox account, follow these steps:

*   Log in and go to the [billing tab of your Account's settings page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing)
    .
*   Enter the new or updated organization name in the memo field and click Save changes.

If you have any questions about updating the profile information for an account, visit the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 for recommendations and next steps to contact support.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# How can I reactivate a deactivated account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-reactivate-a-deactivated-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

To reactivate a deactivated Mapbox account, follow these steps:

1.  Log into your account and go to your [billing settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing)
     page.
2.  Scroll down to the bottom of the page, click add a payment method and enter valid payment information to be used for past due and upcoming invoices.
3.  Navigate to the account's [invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
    , and click the **Pay** button to pay any Past Due invoices.

Past Due notices must be paid before reactivation

You cannot reactivate your account until you have paid your Past Due invoices as stated in our [Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos/)
. You can find more information in our [Account Deactivation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#account-deactivation)
.

Other forms of deactivation

If your account was deactivated, but you do not have a set of outstanding payments, you account was likely deactivated due to fraudulent behavior, which is a breach of our [Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos/)
.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# How can I remove a credit card from my account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-remove-a-credit-card-from-my-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

It is not possible to remove a credit card from an account, though you are able to [add or update a payment method](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#add-or-update-payment-method)
.

Most Mapbox services include a free tier of services available to all accounts during each billing period. It's important to be aware of the [limitations of our free tier services](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing)
, as the number of requests, elements, users, sessions, or other units included in the free tier varies by Mapbox product.

To prevent account deactivation and to keep payments prompt, a valid card must be added to each Mapbox account. The card added to your account will be automatically charged when the **Upcoming** invoice is finalized based on that period of service usage.

Note that **if an account does not have a credit card on file, Mapbox will not be able to verify account ownership**. In this case, the only next step will be to create a new Mapbox account and add access tokens from your new account into your map implementations.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# How can I troubleshoot email verification issues for my account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-troubleshoot-email-verification-issues/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

To make sure that you can receive important notifications about your account, Mapbox requires all accounts to verify their registered email address. You can do this by clicking the link that Mapbox sends immediately upon account creation. The verification process must be completed to gain full access to the Mapbox applications. Verification links are valid for 24 hours and can be re-requested when logged into your account. If an account's email address is updated, you will be prompted to verify the new address.

If you're having trouble verifying your account's email address, you can visit the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 for recommendations.

If you're not receiving the verification email, there are a few things you can try:

1.  Check for invalid email addresses. Emails cannot be delivered if the address contains a spelling error, the mailbox is full, the inbox does not exist, or for many other reasons. If the address is invalid, follow the prompts on the [email confirmation page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/check-email/)
     to update spelling errors or change the account's registered email. This begins the verification process again with the new address.
2.  If you confirm that the email address is spelled properly and that the emails are not in your Spam folder or Promotions tab, allow for up to 60 minutes of latency throughout your email system. It's possible that your IT team maintains a filter above your inbox that requires additional processing and is delaying the email's arrival. Consider opening an internal ticket letting your IT team know about the problem, recommending they run a global search for emails from [no-reply@mapbox.com](mailto:no-reply@mapbox.com)
     or with the subject "verify your email address".

If the email address has not received an email after 60 minutes and is configured for receiving vendor email,[contact Mapbox Support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 so they can troubleshoot with you based on the specific error codes being surfaced by your email provider.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# How can I change my account id/username? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-change-my-account-id/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

It is not possible to [change an account's ID](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-username-not-possible)
 (username). But you can [add or change the account's organization name,](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#change-an-account-holders-name)
 which will populate on all invoices.

Or, you can create a new account, [transfer your map styles,](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/transfer-styles-between-accounts/)
 and update your web and mobile applications to reference the new account's tokens and styles. For more information about setting up an account for a team or preparing for an ownership transition, see [our guide for collaboration](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/#best-practices-for-setting-up-an-account)
.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# How does Pay-as-you-go billing work? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-does-pay-as-you-go-billing-work/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Mapbox's Pay-as-you-go billing works as follows:

1.  **Free Tier**: All customers with Pay-as-you-go billing receive a monthly free tier of services that resets on the first day of the account's monthly billing period. We send an email notification to the account's email address the first time a free tier is exceeded during each billing period.
2.  **Invoicing**: Every month, a new invoice is generated on the first day of a new billing period. This upcoming invoice is generated even if an account has not yet incurred overages for that billing period. Usage data is reflected on the upcoming invoice within 24 hours, and charges are always in USD. Invoices are finalized on the last day of the account's billing period. When your invoice is finalized, your payment method on file is automatically charged. If the charge fails, the account will be at risk of deactivation and service interruptions.

3.  **Billing Period**: The invoices page provides insight into an account's monthly billing period. Billing periods start on the first day of the month and end on the last day of the month. The account's billing period will be shortened during the first month of account creation or account reactivation.
    
4.  **Automatic Payments**: The default payment setting for all customers is automatic payments. With automatic payments, the account's credit card on file (or prepaid credit) will be automatically charged when the Upcoming invoice is finalized.
    
5.  **Monthly Invoicing**: With Monthly Invoicing, the account's invoice is not automatically charged to a credit card on file. Instead, an invoice is emailed to the account's billing email address. Monthly Invoicing is not available for all customers.
    

Note that accounts may enter a deactivated state if invoices are not paid, so we do recommend adding a credit card for recurring charges or a lump sum of [prepaid credit](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#prepaid-credit)
 that can be used for invoices - especially if you're using Mapbox in production - to avoid any service outages.

For a general overview of how Pay-as-you-go billing works, you can reference the [What to Expect](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#what-to-expect)
 guide and the [Billing emails](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#exceeded-free-tier)
 documentation.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# How can I regain access to my account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Mapbox serves as critical infrastructure for our customers and we work hard to protect the security of your accounts and data. Maintaining access to an account starts by setting up or updating your account with the recommendations listed in our [best practices guide for collaboration](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/)
. If you are unable to login to your account, or unsure how to begin the steps for regaining account access, this guide outlines common account lockout scenarios and step-by-step instructions for confirming ownership of an account, regaining access, and updating the credentials. For general information about Mapbox accounts, see our [Account documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/)
.

If you’re still experiencing difficulties after working through this guide, [contact support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

Verifying ownership of an account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#verifying-ownership-of-an-account "Direct link to Verifying ownership of an account")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are unable to regain access to an account on your own and require help from our team, you will need to begin an account ownership security review.

To verify ownership of a personal account, there must be a credit card on file in the account's billing settings. To verify ownership of an account that belongs to your team, contact your Account Manager, Technical Account Manager, or our [support team](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to get started. In your request, include as many of these details as possible:

*   Mapbox account ID (username)
*   Registered email address
*   Invoice ID or invoice number
*   Access token, Style URL, or Tileset ID
*   Last 4 digits of a CC on file
*   Full URL of an API request
*   Link to a live map

We try to respond to all account ownership verification and lockout troubleshooting requests within 2 business days of submission.

If you know an account's registered email address and have access to the inbox for that email address, you can [reset the password](https://account.mapbox.com/reset-password)
 and login to the account with the new password + the registered email address. If you would like to [update the account's registered email address](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-email-address)
, you can do so from the **Profile page** once logged into the account.

### I’m not receiving the password reset email[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#im-not-receiving-the-password-reset-email "Direct link to I’m not receiving the password reset email")

If you are entering the account's registered email address into the [password reset form](https://account.mapbox.com/reset-password)
 but not receiving an email, check your spam folder to make sure our email hasn't been routed there. Not seeing it there, either? Sometimes password reset emails are undeliverable - maybe emails from Mapbox to the email address have bounced in the past or there have been other problems preventing the email from being delivered (especially for inactive email addresses). If you are not receiving the password reset email, let us know by [submitting the support form](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

### I know the password, but I cannot authenticate with two-factor authentication (2FA)[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#i-know-the-password-but-i-cannot-authenticate-with-two-factor-authentication-2fa "Direct link to I know the password, but I cannot authenticate with two-factor authentication (2FA)")

If you are inheriting an account and know the account's password or have reset the account's password successfully, but do not have 2FA codes or [recovery codes](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#use-a-recovery-code-to-access-an-account-without-the-registered-two-factor-device)
, you will need to [verify ownership of the account](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#verifying-ownership-of-an-account)
 with our team. If ownership of the account can be verified, our team can remove 2FA from the account so you can login with the password. Contact your Account Manager, Technical Account Manager, or our [support team](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to get started.

### I lost my two-factor authentication (2FA) backup codes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#i-lost-my-two-factor-authentication-2fa-backup-codes "Direct link to I lost my two-factor authentication (2FA) backup codes")

*   **How to regain access:** If you enabled 2FA on your own account but no longer have access to the device used for 2FA or the [recovery codes](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#use-a-recovery-code-to-access-an-account-without-the-registered-two-factor-device)
    , our team will need to verify that you’re the owner of the account. This verification [requires having a payment method](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#add-or-update-payment-method)
     on file. You can [contact support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
     to begin the verification process. If your account doesn’t have a payment method on file and we’re unable to verify ownership, consider creating a new account.
*   **Prevention:** When you enable 2FA, a single-use recovery code is generated. **Write down this code and keep it in a safe place**, like a password manager.

### I don’t know the username or password for our Mapbox account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#i-dont-know-the-username-or-password-for-our-mapbox-account "Direct link to I don’t know the username or password for our Mapbox account")

If you've inherited a project that uses Mapbox location services, but you don't have access to the login credentials, the information below will help you work with support team to confirm ownership of the account.

*   **How to regain access:** Verify ownership of your account by [contacting support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
    .
*   **Prevention:** Save the username & password for your Mapbox account in a shared vault like a [1Password](https://1password.com/)
     vault and/or [Okta](https://www.okta.com/)
     and use an email address that is accessible for all your stakeholders.

### My colleague left the company with their email address still registered on our account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#my-colleague-left-the-company-with-their-email-address-still-registered-on-our-account "Direct link to My colleague left the company with their email address still registered on our account")

*   **How to regain access:** If a former colleague's email address is tied to your company's Mapbox account, try contacting your IT department requesting the email address be reactivated or forwarded automatically to an email of your choosing. Once you have access to that email address or forwarding rules are in place, you can [reset your password](https://account.mapbox.com/recover-password)
     to regain access to that account. Once you’ve regained access to your account, you can [change the email](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#profile)
     to any of your choosing.

If you need our Security team to change the registered email on your account, we’ll need to verify that you are the owner of the account first. [Send us a note](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 and we will be happy to look into it for you. We try and respond to all account verification and lockout troubleshooting requests within 1-2 business days of submission.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# How can I transfer ownership of my account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-can-i-transfer-ownership-of-my-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Transferring ownership of a Mapbox account involves several steps:

1.  **Ownership Management**: Share the account credentials with the new owner. This will allow them to [change the email](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#profile)
     and [change the password](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#password)
     to assume full ownership of the account. It's recommended to use an email address that multiple people have access to and to save the new password in a shared password manager, like [1Password](https://1password.com/)
    .
2.  **Account Credentials**: Encourage the outgoing owner to [change the registered email address](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-email-address)
     to a valid, shared email. This will allow stakeholders to log in to the account to change the password as well as receive invoices, notifications, and password reset emails.
3.  **Authentication Methods**: Consider enabling [SAML Single-sign on](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
     as the primary authentication method if your organization uses an identity provider that supports the SAML2.0 protocol. With SSO, your users can also be assigned [user roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     with specific permissions for each role. With SSO enabled, your team will not need to share a password for the account.
4.  **Password Management**: Store the Mapbox account credentials in a shared-secure location, like a [1Password](https://1password.com/)
     vault and/or [Okta](https://www.okta.com/)
    . This will help keep your password safe and available when you or your teammates need it. Even if your account has SAML SSO enabled, there will be a password associated with the account. Save this password in a secure location with limited access.

Note that it is not possible to change an account's ID (username). If you need to change the username, you can create a new account, [transfer your map styles](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/transfer-styles-between-accounts/)
 and update your web and mobile applications to reference the new account's tokens and styles. Once this transition is complete, you can [delete](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#delete-your-account)
 the original account without breaking any links to your maps.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# How do I contact Mapbox? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-do-i-contact-mapbox/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

If you have questions about your account, billing or email verification issues, you can create a [support ticket](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

If you would like to contact sales to ask about our product offerings, you can contact the sales team through the [following form](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales?page=/contact)
.

If you are experiencing technical issues while implementing Mapbox, we offer [paid plans](https://www.mapbox.com/support#support-plans)
 which allow you to create tickets and contact our technical support team directly.

You can also find helpful resources, tutorials, and FAQs in our [help docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/)
. You can post questions on [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/ask)
 or in our [Mapbox Developer Discord](https://discord.com/invite/uMpcC5RmJh)
 to seek help from the broader community of Mapbox developers.

# Why is my credit card being declined by Mapbox? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/why-is-my-credit-card-being-declined-by-mapbox/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Mapbox accepts the following credit, debit, prepaid, and gift cards:

*   Visa
*   MasterCard
*   American Express
*   JCB
*   Discover
*   Diner's club

If you do not have access to a credit or debit card, consider using a prepaid card available from several vendors, including:

*   [American Express](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/prepaid/view-all-cards/)
    
*   [Visa](https://usa.visa.com/pay-with-visa/cards/card-finder/prepaid-finder-page.html)
    
*   [MasterCard](https://www.mastercard.com/us/en/personal/find-a-card/prepaid-card.html)
    

To protect your privacy, Banks do not provide detailed information to Mapbox when credit card payments fail. If your card information seems correct, it's recommended to contact your bank for more information or to allow payments to Mapbox.com. If payments are still being declined after contacting your bank, you could try adding a different credit card for the payment.

If you're working with your bank to resolve this issue, it's important to let Mapbox know to help prevent account deactivation and keep your account's Mapbox services live. You can [contact Mapbox Support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 for this purpose.

For more information, you can refer to the [Mapbox documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#payments)
.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# Am I able to set a cap for monthly spending? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/can-i-set-up-a-cap-for-monthly-spending/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Mapbox does not provide the ability to cap your monthly billing. For now, you can create predictable monthly API usage is by monitoring your [account statistics](https://www.mapbox.com/account/statistics)
 and your [invoices](https://www.mapbox.com/account/invoices)
.

Once your account statistics and/or invoices reach a level where you'd like to be capped, you can:

1.  [Delete](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/dive-deeper/access-tokens/#deleting-tokens)
     your [access tokens](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
     from your tokens page (deactivating any mapping implementations that were authenticated by that access token). Or,
2.  Remove your access tokens from your code. Where you see our map initialization code block occurs depends on your implementation. You can temporarily remove your access tokens so that no traffic authenticates.
3.  When you're ready to enable map traffic again, you can add the access token back into your map code will reactivate the maps.

For more information about your account statistics or invoices, you can explore our [Accounts Documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/)
.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

---

# Does Mapbox provide refunds? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/does-mapbox-provide-refunds/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Yes, Mapbox does provide refunds under certain conditions.

If Mapbox has issued a refund, the funds will be returned to the account's original payment method 5-10 business days after the refund has been processed. Individual Mapbox invoices will not be reflected to show refunds. If you require additional receipts for the refund, you can reach out to Mapbox Support team [with the necessary details about your invoice](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request)
.

Additionally, if sales tax has been applied to your invoice, but you can provide valid exemption documentation covering the purchase period, you can get a refund on the sales tax part of your payment if you contact **[accountsreceivable@mapbox.com](mailto:accountsreceivable@mapbox.com)
 within 30 days of the invoice date**.

After 30 days from the invoice date, you will need to contact your local state tax authority to process a refund.

Note that prepaid credit added to your account for future Mapbox services cannot be refunded in case it's no longer needed. The prepaid credit will be applied to future invoices on a recurring basis until the credit is $0.00. Mapbox won't charge the credit card on file until the account's prepaid balance is $0.00.

Refer to our documentation for more details:

*   [Request a sales tax refund](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#request-a-sales-tax-refund)
    
*   [Prepaid credit](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#prepaid-credit)
    

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# Does Mapbox support Two-factor Authentication (2FA)? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/does-mapbox-support-two-factor-authentication/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Two-factor authentication (2FA), also known as multi-factor authentication (MFA) or two-step authentication, is an optional but recommended security feature for Mapbox accounts. Once enabled, users will be prompted to enter the account's password and a security code generated on registered mobile devices whenever they log in to the account. For more information, refer to the documentation available at [/guides/settings/#two-factor-authentication](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#two-factor-authentication)
.

[To enable 2FA](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#enable-two-factor-authentication)
, you need to be logged into your account and navigate to the Security page. You'll be prompted to scan a barcode with your mobile device or type a code into password management services like 1Password. The recommended authenticator app is Google Authenticator, which is free and available for iOS and Android. For a Windows phone, use the Authenticator app. Your mobile device will display a 6-digit code, which you need to type into the field below the barcode to complete the process.

If you lose your two-factor device, you can use a recovery code to access your account. This code is a single-use code that lets you sign in without your two-factor device. To use the code, you’ll need your username or email and your password.

To use a recovery code, navigate to the Sign-in page, enter your account ID and password, then click Sign in. Click the Lost your mobile device? Link below the Sign-in button. A new field for the recovery code will appear. Enter your recovery code in the new field and click Sign in. Find instructions for using a recovery code in our [Account documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#security)
.

Note that enabling 2FA for an account raises the likelihood that you or other users of the account may have trouble logging into the account in the future. Thus it is recommended to store 2FA recovery codes in a safe place and distribute them to new account owners when transferring ownership of an account.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# How can I add an alternate/secondary billing address? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-add-an-alternate-billing-address/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

A [secondary / alternate billing email address](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#secondary-billing-email-addresses)
 is only available for customers with the [Monthly Invoicing](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#monthly-invoicing)
 payment setting. You can request participation in the Monthly Invoicing beta by contacting our Sales team through this form: [https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/)
.

For customers with [Automatic Payments](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#automatic-payments)
 as their payment setting, we recommend the following workarounds to help make sure multiple stakeholders within your organization have access to billing notifications:

1.  Consider creating a group email address like "[company+mapbox@company.com](mailto:company+mapbox@company.com)
    " and adding members to that group to receive notifications.
2.  Consider setting up automatic forwarding rules in your email client to forward any emails with the title **\[Mapbox Billing\] Successful payment receipt** to the recipients of your choosing (like a billing/finance team). They will be able to download the PDF receipt right from the body of the email.

For more information, you can refer to the [Mapbox documentation on invoices](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#monthly-invoicing)
.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# How can I troubleshoot email verification issues for my account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-troubleshoot-email-verification-issues/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

To make sure that you can receive important notifications about your account, Mapbox requires all accounts to verify their registered email address. You can do this by clicking the link that Mapbox sends immediately upon account creation. The verification process must be completed to gain full access to the Mapbox applications. Verification links are valid for 24 hours and can be re-requested when logged into your account. If an account's email address is updated, you will be prompted to verify the new address.

If you're having trouble verifying your account's email address, you can visit the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 for recommendations.

If you're not receiving the verification email, there are a few things you can try:

1.  Check for invalid email addresses. Emails cannot be delivered if the address contains a spelling error, the mailbox is full, the inbox does not exist, or for many other reasons. If the address is invalid, follow the prompts on the [email confirmation page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/check-email/)
     to update spelling errors or change the account's registered email. This begins the verification process again with the new address.
2.  If you confirm that the email address is spelled properly and that the emails are not in your Spam folder or Promotions tab, allow for up to 60 minutes of latency throughout your email system. It's possible that your IT team maintains a filter above your inbox that requires additional processing and is delaying the email's arrival. Consider opening an internal ticket letting your IT team know about the problem, recommending they run a global search for emails from [no-reply@mapbox.com](mailto:no-reply@mapbox.com)
     or with the subject "verify your email address".

If the email address has not received an email after 60 minutes and is configured for receiving vendor email,[contact Mapbox Support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 so they can troubleshoot with you based on the specific error codes being surfaced by your email provider.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# How does Pay-as-you-go billing work? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-does-pay-as-you-go-billing-work/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Mapbox's Pay-as-you-go billing works as follows:

1.  **Free Tier**: All customers with Pay-as-you-go billing receive a monthly free tier of services that resets on the first day of the account's monthly billing period. We send an email notification to the account's email address the first time a free tier is exceeded during each billing period.
2.  **Invoicing**: Every month, a new invoice is generated on the first day of a new billing period. This upcoming invoice is generated even if an account has not yet incurred overages for that billing period. Usage data is reflected on the upcoming invoice within 24 hours, and charges are always in USD. Invoices are finalized on the last day of the account's billing period. When your invoice is finalized, your payment method on file is automatically charged. If the charge fails, the account will be at risk of deactivation and service interruptions.

3.  **Billing Period**: The invoices page provides insight into an account's monthly billing period. Billing periods start on the first day of the month and end on the last day of the month. The account's billing period will be shortened during the first month of account creation or account reactivation.
    
4.  **Automatic Payments**: The default payment setting for all customers is automatic payments. With automatic payments, the account's credit card on file (or prepaid credit) will be automatically charged when the Upcoming invoice is finalized.
    
5.  **Monthly Invoicing**: With Monthly Invoicing, the account's invoice is not automatically charged to a credit card on file. Instead, an invoice is emailed to the account's billing email address. Monthly Invoicing is not available for all customers.
    

Note that accounts may enter a deactivated state if invoices are not paid, so we do recommend adding a credit card for recurring charges or a lump sum of [prepaid credit](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#prepaid-credit)
 that can be used for invoices - especially if you're using Mapbox in production - to avoid any service outages.

For a general overview of how Pay-as-you-go billing works, you can reference the [What to Expect](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#what-to-expect)
 guide and the [Billing emails](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#exceeded-free-tier)
 documentation.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# How do I pay an outstanding invoice? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-do-i-pay-an-outstanding-invoice/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

To pay unpaid invoices with `Past Due` status on Mapbox, follow these steps:

1.  Log into your account and navigate to your account's [invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
    .
2.  Click the **Pay** button next to any invoice with a `Past Due` status.

Note

Paying via the **Pay** button will not save or update the credit card on the account. Also, prepaid credit cannot be used to pay `Past Due` invoices. Mapbox reserves the right to retry charges for `Past Due` invoices based on the Mapbox Terms of Service. For more details, you can refer to the [Mapbox Documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/)
.

# What can I do if I'm having trouble signing into my Mapbox account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/what-can-i-do-if-im-having-trouble-signing-into-my-mapbox-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

If you're having trouble signing in to your Mapbox account, here are some common issues and their solutions:

1.  **Account access issues**: If you're unable to access your account, make sure you have the correct account ID (username) and registered email address. If applicable, also check your access token.
    
2.  **Failed login issues**: If you're having trouble logging in, check the following:
    
    *   Web browser(s) and versions
    *   Any enabled web browser extensions you're using
    *   Any enabled experimental flags (chrome://flags/) if using Google Chrome
    *   Whether you have tested this issue while in incognito mode (if applicable)
    *   Whether you have tested this issue with no browser extensions (if applicable)
    
    If you do not know your browser information, you can go to the browser settings or the About page to find these details.
    
3.  **Not receiving the password reset email**: If you're not receiving the password reset email, first check your spam folder. If it's not there, it's possible that emails from Mapbox have bounced in the past or there have been other problems preventing the email from being delivered.
    
4.  **Locked out of your account**: If you're locked out of your account and need to regain access, you can follow [the account lockout troubleshooting guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/account-lockout/)
    . This guide provides recommendations for common scenarios.
    

If you still have a problem, you can submit a [support request](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# What statistics are available for my Mapbox account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/what-statistics-are-available-for-my-mapbox-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Your Mapbox account's Statistics page provides usage information for all billable products found on the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/)
. You can access these statistics by logging into your account and navigating to the [Statistics page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/statistics/)
 of your Developer Console.

The Statistics page provides insight into an account's usage data for the billable products listed on the pricing page. Information about API rate limits, API performance, and HTTP status codes of requests to the Mapbox APIs are not displayed on the Statistics page.

You can filter the Mapbox usage data on your Statistics page in two ways:

*   **By date**: Choose a standard period (such as week or month) or enter a custom date range. All dates are in UTC (Greenwich Mean Time). Usage data will be available on the Statistics page within 24 hours.
*   **By access token**: See usage for all access tokens or one specific, active [access token](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/access-token/)
    . Legacy SDKs for Mobile statistics are not available when filtering by access token. To see Legacy SDKs for Mobile statistics, filter by **All tokens and connected apps**.

Your account usage is organized by product on your Statistics page. For each product, you'll see the number of metered units for the token and time period selected. You will only see statistics for the products you’ve used within the specified time period and with the specified token.

For many products, you can load additional metrics about the _Countries_, _Browsers_, _Domains_, and _Tileset IDs_ associated with your requests. These additional metrics are presented as percentages of the total number of metered units for the specified time period and token. Additional metrics are not available for products that are metered by user count (such as Maps SDK for Mobile). They are also not available for time periods exceeding 31 days.

Note that when an access token is deleted, it is not possible to isolate statistics for a token after it has been deleted. Deleted tokens do not show up in the drop-down filter list, rather, usage data tied to a token before and after its deletion will be aggregated in the **All tokens and connected apps** view.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

 for recommendations and next steps to contact support.

# How can I delete an account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-delete-an-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

To delete your Mapbox account, follow these steps:

1.  Log into your account and go to your [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
     page.
2.  Scroll to the bottom of the settings page.
3.  Click the **Delete account** button.

Note that account deletion is a permanent action that cannot be undone. Mapbox will not be able to recover your account, and the same account ID (username) cannot be re-used to create another account.

After clicking the button to delete an account, there will be a modal that requires additional confirmation that you do intend to fully delete the account. When an account is deleted, the following are effective immediately:

*   All styles, projects, and data associated with the account are no longer available.
*   The account cannot be logged into.
*   Access tokens tied to that account will be immediately terminated, and map implementations using them will no longer be able to effectively access Mapbox services.

Mapbox will not automatically prorate for any unused services or subscriptions.

If you experience issues while deleting your account or would like to discuss prorated refunds for unused services, contact the Mapbox support team by following [these instructions](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#if-i-cannot-resolve-my-issue-what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request)
.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# How can I remove a credit card from my account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-remove-a-credit-card-from-my-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

It is not possible to remove a credit card from an account, though you are able to [add or update a payment method](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#add-or-update-payment-method)
.

Most Mapbox services include a free tier of services available to all accounts during each billing period. It's important to be aware of the [limitations of our free tier services](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing)
, as the number of requests, elements, users, sessions, or other units included in the free tier varies by Mapbox product.

To prevent account deactivation and to keep payments prompt, a valid card must be added to each Mapbox account. The card added to your account will be automatically charged when the **Upcoming** invoice is finalized based on that period of service usage.

Note that **if an account does not have a credit card on file, Mapbox will not be able to verify account ownership**. In this case, the only next step will be to create a new Mapbox account and add access tokens from your new account into your map implementations.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# How can I reactivate a deactivated account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-reactivate-a-deactivated-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

To reactivate a deactivated Mapbox account, follow these steps:

1.  Log into your account and go to your [billing settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing)
     page.
2.  Scroll down to the bottom of the page, click add a payment method and enter valid payment information to be used for past due and upcoming invoices.
3.  Navigate to the account's [invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
    , and click the **Pay** button to pay any Past Due invoices.

Past Due notices must be paid before reactivation

You cannot reactivate your account until you have paid your Past Due invoices as stated in our [Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos/)
. You can find more information in our [Account Deactivation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#account-deactivation)
.

Other forms of deactivation

If your account was deactivated, but you do not have a set of outstanding payments, you account was likely deactivated due to fraudulent behavior, which is a breach of our [Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos/)
.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# Why is my credit card being declined by Mapbox? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/why-is-my-credit-card-being-declined-by-mapbox/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Mapbox accepts the following credit, debit, prepaid, and gift cards:

*   Visa
*   MasterCard
*   American Express
*   JCB
*   Discover
*   Diner's club

If you do not have access to a credit or debit card, consider using a prepaid card available from several vendors, including:

*   [American Express](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/prepaid/view-all-cards/)
    
*   [Visa](https://usa.visa.com/pay-with-visa/cards/card-finder/prepaid-finder-page.html)
    
*   [MasterCard](https://www.mastercard.com/us/en/personal/find-a-card/prepaid-card.html)
    

To protect your privacy, Banks do not provide detailed information to Mapbox when credit card payments fail. If your card information seems correct, it's recommended to contact your bank for more information or to allow payments to Mapbox.com. If payments are still being declined after contacting your bank, you could try adding a different credit card for the payment.

If you're working with your bank to resolve this issue, it's important to let Mapbox know to help prevent account deactivation and keep your account's Mapbox services live. You can [contact Mapbox Support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 for this purpose.

For more information, you can refer to the [Mapbox documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#payments)
.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

---

# How do I pay for Mapbox services? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/how-do-i-pay-for-mapbox-services/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Automatic payments are the default payment setting for all customers to pay for Mapbox services. With this setting, the account's credit card on file (or [prepaid credit](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#prepaid-credit)
) will be automatically charged when the upcoming invoice is finalized.

Mapbox accepts the following credit, debit, prepaid, and gift cards: Visa, MasterCard, American Express, JCB, Discover, and Diner's club. If you do not have access to a credit or debit card, you can consider using a prepaid card available from several vendors, including American Express, Visa, and MasterCard.

To add or update a credit card on an account, you can visit the [billing settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing)
 and click the **Add payment method** button or the **Edit** button if you are updating the credit card.

You can also add prepaid credit to be used for future Mapbox services. Prepaid credit can be added any time and cannot be refunded in the case it's no longer needed. Account credit will be applied to future invoices on a recurring basis until the credit is $0.00. Mapbox won't charge the credit card on file until the account's prepaid balance is $0.00.

**To add prepaid credit to the account, follow these instructions:**

1.  Navigate to the [billing settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing)
    .
2.  Make sure you have a valid credit card on file.
3.  Click the **Add credit** button.
4.  Enter the amount of credit you want to buy.
5.  The credit card will be charged immediately, and the account's email address will receive a receipt for the payment.
6.  After buying credit, a record will be available in the billing settings and on the `Upcoming` invoice.

If your organization is unable to make payments with a credit card, you can [contact the Mapbox Sales team](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/)
 to learn more about the requirements for the Monthly Invoicing beta program.

For additional information, refer to:

*   [Mapbox Documentation: Invoices and billing](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/)
    
*   [Mapbox Documentation: Getting started](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/getting-started/)
    

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# How can I change my account id/username? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-change-my-account-id/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

It is not possible to [change an account's ID](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-username-not-possible)
 (username). But you can [add or change the account's organization name,](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#change-an-account-holders-name)
 which will populate on all invoices.

Or, you can create a new account, [transfer your map styles,](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/transfer-styles-between-accounts/)
 and update your web and mobile applications to reference the new account's tokens and styles. For more information about setting up an account for a team or preparing for an ownership transition, see [our guide for collaboration](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/#best-practices-for-setting-up-an-account)
.

# How can I transfer ownership of my account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-transfer-ownership-of-my-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Transferring ownership of a Mapbox account involves several steps:

1.  **Ownership Management**: Share the account credentials with the new owner. This will allow them to [change the email](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#profile)
     and [change the password](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#password)
     to assume full ownership of the account. It's recommended to use an email address that multiple people have access to and to save the new password in a shared password manager, like [1Password](https://1password.com/)
    .
2.  **Account Credentials**: Encourage the outgoing owner to [change the registered email address](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-email-address)
     to a valid, shared email. This will allow stakeholders to log in to the account to change the password as well as receive invoices, notifications, and password reset emails.
3.  **Authentication Methods**: Consider enabling [SAML Single-sign on](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
     as the primary authentication method if your organization uses an identity provider that supports the SAML2.0 protocol. With SSO, your users can also be assigned [user roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     with specific permissions for each role. With SSO enabled, your team will not need to share a password for the account.
4.  **Password Management**: Store the Mapbox account credentials in a shared-secure location, like a [1Password](https://1password.com/)
     vault and/or [Okta](https://www.okta.com/)
    . This will help keep your password safe and available when you or your teammates need it. Even if your account has SAML SSO enabled, there will be a password associated with the account. Save this password in a secure location with limited access.

Note that it is not possible to change an account's ID (username). If you need to change the username, you can create a new account, [transfer your map styles](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/transfer-styles-between-accounts/)
 and update your web and mobile applications to reference the new account's tokens and styles. Once this transition is complete, you can [delete](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#delete-your-account)
 the original account without breaking any links to your maps.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

---

# How can I regain access to my account? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Mapbox serves as critical infrastructure for our customers and we work hard to protect the security of your accounts and data. Maintaining access to an account starts by setting up or updating your account with the recommendations listed in our [best practices guide for collaboration](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/)
. If you are unable to login to your account, or unsure how to begin the steps for regaining account access, this guide outlines common account lockout scenarios and step-by-step instructions for confirming ownership of an account, regaining access, and updating the credentials. For general information about Mapbox accounts, see our [Account documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/)
.

If you’re still experiencing difficulties after working through this guide, [contact support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

Verifying ownership of an account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#verifying-ownership-of-an-account "Verifying ownership of an account への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are unable to regain access to an account on your own and require help from our team, you will need to begin an account ownership security review.

To verify ownership of a personal account, there must be a credit card on file in the account's billing settings. To verify ownership of an account that belongs to your team, contact your Account Manager, Technical Account Manager, or our [support team](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to get started. In your request, include as many of these details as possible:

*   Mapbox account ID (username)
*   Registered email address
*   Invoice ID or invoice number
*   Access token, Style URL, or Tileset ID
*   Last 4 digits of a CC on file
*   Full URL of an API request
*   Link to a live map

We try to respond to all account ownership verification and lockout troubleshooting requests within 2 business days of submission.

If you know an account's registered email address and have access to the inbox for that email address, you can [reset the password](https://account.mapbox.com/reset-password)
 and login to the account with the new password + the registered email address. If you would like to [update the account's registered email address](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-email-address)
, you can do so from the **Profile page** once logged into the account.

### I’m not receiving the password reset email[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#im-not-receiving-the-password-reset-email "I’m not receiving the password reset email への直接リンク")

If you are entering the account's registered email address into the [password reset form](https://account.mapbox.com/reset-password)
 but not receiving an email, check your spam folder to make sure our email hasn't been routed there. Not seeing it there, either? Sometimes password reset emails are undeliverable - maybe emails from Mapbox to the email address have bounced in the past or there have been other problems preventing the email from being delivered (especially for inactive email addresses). If you are not receiving the password reset email, let us know by [submitting the support form](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

### I know the password, but I cannot authenticate with two-factor authentication (2FA)[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#i-know-the-password-but-i-cannot-authenticate-with-two-factor-authentication-2fa "I know the password, but I cannot authenticate with two-factor authentication (2FA) への直接リンク")

If you are inheriting an account and know the account's password or have reset the account's password successfully, but do not have 2FA codes or [recovery codes](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#use-a-recovery-code-to-access-an-account-without-the-registered-two-factor-device)
, you will need to [verify ownership of the account](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#verifying-ownership-of-an-account)
 with our team. If ownership of the account can be verified, our team can remove 2FA from the account so you can login with the password. Contact your Account Manager, Technical Account Manager, or our [support team](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to get started.

### I lost my two-factor authentication (2FA) backup codes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#i-lost-my-two-factor-authentication-2fa-backup-codes "I lost my two-factor authentication (2FA) backup codes への直接リンク")

*   **How to regain access:** If you enabled 2FA on your own account but no longer have access to the device used for 2FA or the [recovery codes](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#use-a-recovery-code-to-access-an-account-without-the-registered-two-factor-device)
    , our team will need to verify that you’re the owner of the account. This verification [requires having a payment method](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#add-or-update-payment-method)
     on file. You can [contact support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
     to begin the verification process. If your account doesn’t have a payment method on file and we’re unable to verify ownership, consider creating a new account.
*   **Prevention:** When you enable 2FA, a single-use recovery code is generated. **Write down this code and keep it in a safe place**, like a password manager.

### I don’t know the username or password for our Mapbox account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#i-dont-know-the-username-or-password-for-our-mapbox-account "I don’t know the username or password for our Mapbox account への直接リンク")

If you've inherited a project that uses Mapbox location services, but you don't have access to the login credentials, the information below will help you work with support team to confirm ownership of the account.

*   **How to regain access:** Verify ownership of your account by [contacting support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
    .
*   **Prevention:** Save the username & password for your Mapbox account in a shared vault like a [1Password](https://1password.com/)
     vault and/or [Okta](https://www.okta.com/)
     and use an email address that is accessible for all your stakeholders.

### My colleague left the company with their email address still registered on our account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-can-i-regain-access-to-my-account/#my-colleague-left-the-company-with-their-email-address-still-registered-on-our-account "My colleague left the company with their email address still registered on our account への直接リンク")

*   **How to regain access:** If a former colleague's email address is tied to your company's Mapbox account, try contacting your IT department requesting the email address be reactivated or forwarded automatically to an email of your choosing. Once you have access to that email address or forwarding rules are in place, you can [reset your password](https://account.mapbox.com/recover-password)
     to regain access to that account. Once you’ve regained access to your account, you can [change the email](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#profile)
     to any of your choosing.

If you need our Security team to change the registered email on your account, we’ll need to verify that you are the owner of the account first. [Send us a note](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 and we will be happy to look into it for you. We try and respond to all account verification and lockout troubleshooting requests within 1-2 business days of submission.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

---

# How do I contact Mapbox? | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/faq/how-do-i-contact-mapbox/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

If you have questions about your account, billing or email verification issues, you can create a [support ticket](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

If you would like to contact sales to ask about our product offerings, you can contact the sales team through the [following form](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales?page=/contact)
.

If you are experiencing technical issues while implementing Mapbox, we offer [paid plans](https://www.mapbox.com/support#support-plans)
 which allow you to create tickets and contact our technical support team directly.

You can also find helpful resources, tutorials, and FAQs in our [help docs](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/)
. You can post questions on [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/ask)
 or in our [Mapbox Developer Discord](https://discord.com/invite/uMpcC5RmJh)
 to seek help from the broader community of Mapbox developers.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ
