# Settings and account access | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

The **Settings page** is where you can [delete an account](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#delete-your-account)
 or manage the account's profile information, billing and authentication methods, and connected apps. To access the Settings page, log into the account and navigate to the [settings section](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
 of your account. Once on the page, scroll to the bottom and you'll see the blue delete account button.

Note that for accounts using [SAML Single-sign on](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
 authentication, only users with the `Root` role assigned in their identity provider can access and update settings.

Profile[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#profile "Direct link to Profile")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All users who login to an account with the account's password assume `Root` user privileges by default, and can access and update the account's settings. Or, if the account has enabled [SAML Single sign-on authentication](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
, there are two user roles that are available: `Root` and `Admin`, which are assigned to users in an identity provider (like Okta). For accounts with SSO enabled, only users with the `Root` role will be able to access and update settings as described below. [Learn more about user roles in the SAML SSO documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
.

### Verify an account's email address[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#verify-an-accounts-email-address "Direct link to Verify an account's email address")

To be sure that you can receive important notifications about your account, all accounts must verify the registered email address by clicking a link that we send immediately upon account creation. The verification process must be completed to gain full access to the Mapbox applications. Verification links are valid for 24 hours and can be re-requested when logged into your account. If an account's email address is updated, you will be prompted to verify the new address.

Having trouble verifying your account's email address? Visit the [troubleshooting](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 section for recommendations.

### Change an account's email address[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-email-address "Direct link to Change an account's email address")

Each Mapbox account supports one registered email address. To update an account's email address, follow these steps:

1.  Log in and navigate to your [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
     page, and click the `Profile` tab.
2.  Enter the new email address and click **Save changes**.

An account's registered email address is the primary email address to which we send important account and [billing notifications](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#billing-emails)
. If you are [inheriting an account](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/#inheriting-an-account-from-a-developer-designer-or-consultant)
 from a team member, consultant, or client, update the primary email address to be one that multiple members of your organization have access to, like `developers@mapbox.com`. Similarly, if you are transferring an account to a team, consultant, or client, changing the registered email address on the account from your own to one they have access to make sure important notifications are not missed.

### Change an account's username (not possible)[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-username-not-possible "Direct link to Change an account's username (not possible)")

It is not possible to change an account's ID (username). Doing so would break links to maps tied to that account and could cause other unanticipated problems with implementations that depend on that account ID.

Instead, you can [change the account holder's name](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#change-an-account-holders-name)
 or create a new account, [transfer your map styles](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/transfer-styles-between-accounts/)
 and update your web and mobile applications to reference the new account's tokens and styles. For more information about setting up an account for collaboration or preparing for an ownership transition, see [our guide for collaboration](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/#best-practices-for-setting-up-an-account)
.

Once this transition is complete, you can [delete](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#delete-your-account)
 the original account without breaking any links to your maps.

### Change an account holder's name[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#change-an-account-holders-name "Direct link to Change an account holder's name")

To change the account holder's name, follow these steps:

1.  Log in and navigate to your [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
     page, and click the `Profile` tab.
2.  Enter the updated name in the "Name" field and click **Save changes**.

If your account's type is "Business", you can also update your organization's name on the same `Profile` tab of the [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
 page.

To update the details about your organization that are included on your invoice, see the documentation for [adding custom information to an invoice](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#adding-custom-information-to-an-invoice)
.

**Questions about updating the profile information for an account? Visit the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 for recommendations and next steps to contact support.**

Password[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#password "Direct link to Password")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Change your password[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#change-your-password "Direct link to Change your password")

1.  Log in and navigate to your [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/change-password/)
    .
2.  Enter your current password and new password in the appropriate fields.
3.  If you have two-factor authentication enabled, enter your two-factor authentication code.
4.  Click **Save new password**.

### Reset your password[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#reset-your-password "Direct link to Reset your password")

If you're having trouble logging in to an account, try [resetting the account's password](https://account.mapbox.com/reset-password)
 by providing the email address that's associated with the account. We'll send the registered email on file for that account an email with further instructions to reset the password.

The link that we send in the password reset email is only valid for 24 hours. If you receive an `Invalid token` error message or have waited more than 24 hours, you'll need to request another password reset email.

**Questions about changing or resetting an account's password? Not receiving the reset password email? Visit the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 for recommendations and next steps to contact support.**

Security[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#security "Direct link to Security")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mapbox is critical infrastructure for our customers. We go to significant lengths to protect the security of your account, your data, and your users. Visit our [Security page](https://www.mapbox.com/platform/security)
 for more information about how we [process payments](https://www.mapbox.com/platform/security/#payment-processing)
, [store data](https://www.mapbox.com/platform/security/#data-security)
, and [conduct regular audits](https://www.mapbox.com/platform/security/#regular-audits)
.

An account's [**Security Settings**](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security)
 page is where you can enable and manage SAML Single sign-on and/or two-factor authentication for an account.

### Single Sign-on authentication (SSO)[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso "Direct link to Single Sign-on authentication (SSO)")

Manage your organization's access to Mapbox accounts while adding another level of security with SAML Single sign-on (SSO). SSO enables members of your organization to authenticate into a Mapbox account through any trusted, third-party identity provider that supports the [SAML2.0 protocol](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SAML_2.0)
.

#### SAML SSO capabilities[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#saml-sso-capabilities "Direct link to SAML SSO capabilities")

**Supports:**

*   SAML2.0 protocol
*   Identity provider (IdP) initiated login
*   Shared accounts that multiple users can access
*   [User roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     of `Root` and `Admin`

**Does not support:**

*   JIT (Just in Time) provisioning
*   SCIM provisioning / de-provisioning
*   Domain control or domain lockout
*   Service provider (SP) initiated login
*   Identity provider (IdP) initiated single logout
*   OAuth, OpenID Connect, Kerberos, other protocols
*   Multiple identity providers for a single account
*   SAML authentication for [Atlas on-premises](https://www.mapbox.com/atlas)
    
*   Individual user accounts, a nested sub-account hierarchy, or separate, connected accounts

#### Setting up SAML SSO for your Mapbox account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#setting-up-saml-sso-for-your-mapbox-account "Direct link to Setting up SAML SSO for your Mapbox account")

SAML Single sign-on for Mapbox can be configured with any identity provider that supports the SAML 2.0 protocol. Integrate the Mapbox SAML applications offered by [Okta](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#okta)
, [Azure AD](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#azure-active-directory)
, and [OneLogin](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#onelogin)
 for streamlined setup and IdP specific documentation, or [learn more about the general steps for setting up SSO](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#identity-providers-that-support-saml20)
 with any identity provider below.

##### Okta[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#okta "Direct link to Okta")

*   Okta Integration Network (OIN): [Mapbox Okta verified SAML app](https://www.okta.com/integrations/mapbox/)
    
*   Tutorial: [How to configure SAML 2.0 with Mapbox](https://saml-doc.okta.com/SAML_Docs/How-to-Configure-SAML-2.0-for-Mapbox.html)
    

##### Azure Active Directory[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#azure-active-directory "Direct link to Azure Active Directory")

*   Tutorial: [Azure Active Directory SSO integration with Mapbox](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/saas-apps/mapbox-tutorial)
    

##### OneLogin[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#onelogin "Direct link to OneLogin")

*   OneLogin App Store: A Mapbox SAML app is available. Login to your [OneLogin portal](https://app.onelogin.com/login)
    , navigate to the Administration panel, click "Browse app catalog", and search for "Mapbox".

How to set up SAML 2.0 SSO Authentication for your Mapbox account

Learn how to enable SAML Single Sign-on (SSO) to connect Mapbox accounts to Okta and other enterprise-grade identity solutions.

#### Identity providers that support SAML2.0[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#identity-providers-that-support-saml20 "Direct link to Identity providers that support SAML2.0")

You can create a custom SAML app for Mapbox if there is not a Mapbox SAML application available in your IdP. The setup workflow and terminology used by each identity provider can be unique, but there are general themes:

1.  Login to the Mapbox account you want to set up with SSO authentication
2.  Navigate to the account's [SSO setup page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/sso)
    
3.  In your identity provider (IdP), create a new SAML application
4.  Copy and paste the required details in the **Configure your identity provider** section of the Mapbox [SSO setup page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/sso)
     into your IdP’s configuration workflow
5.  Create a custom attribute within your app that will pass the required [user roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     from your IdP to Mapbox in the SAML assertion.
6.  Copy and paste the required details from your SAML app within your IdP into the **Setup SAML single sign-on for Mapbox** section of the [SSO setup page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/sso)
    
7.  Click **Enable single sign-on** to save the integration
8.  [Validate the integration](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#validating-the-saml-sso-integration)
     is working as expected by assigning yourself one of the required [user roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     for the Mapbox SAML app. If required, assign yourself the application within your IdP.
9.  Assign users to the Mapbox application in your IdP, and assign a required Mapbox [user roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     to each user (or user groups)
10.  Follow the recommendations for [enforcing SAML authentication](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#enforcing-saml-sso-authentication)
    ,then flip the **Activate Mapbox SSO** toggle from `Off` to `On` to immediately terminate all sessions.

#### Configuring your identity provider[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#configuring-your-identity-provider "Direct link to Configuring your identity provider")

Log into your identity provider with the required administrative privileges, then create a custom SAML2.0 application for Mapbox. See the following external links for IdP specific instructions:

*   [AWS SSO](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/singlesignon/latest/userguide/samlapps.html)
    
*   [GSuite](https://support.google.com/a/answer/6087519?hl=en)
    
*   [Auth0](https://auth0.com/docs/authenticate/single-sign-on/outbound-single-sign-on/configure-auth0-saml-identity-provider)
    

In this new application, enter the following values from the [Mapbox SSO setup page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/sso)
:

*   `Single sign-on URL`, also could be referred to as the `SSO URL`, `Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) URL`, `Application ACS URL`, `Reply URL`, `Callback URL`, or `Post-back URL` in your IdP.
*   `Audience Restriction`, also could be referred to as `Audience URI`, `SP Entity ID`, `Identifier`, or `Application SAML Audience` in your IdP.

**Configuration notes**

*   The application username must be in **email format**
*   The **SHA256** encryption algorithm is required
*   One of the supported [user roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     must be present in the SAML assertion for a successful SAML login.

#### Configuring user roles in your identity provider[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider "Direct link to Configuring user roles in your identity provider")

Through SAML SSO you can assign users roles that provide certain permissions in the Mapbox Account and Studio apps that are also enforced by all Mapbox APIs. **User roles are assigned in the identity provider and transferred to Mapbox in the SAML assertion.** The available user roles are:

| Role | Permissions | Typical users |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Root` | Users with the `Root` role can access invoices, read and write to account settings, and read and write to all resources and APIs. | IT Admins, Product Owners, CTOs |
| `Admin` | Users with the `Admin` role can read and write to all resources and APIs. They **cannot** access invoices, nor can they read or write to account settings. | Developers, Designers, Project contributors |

Many identity providers use **custom attributes** and **attribute statements** for roles. The attribute key name and role values are case-insensitive. Typically, roles can be assigned to individuals or groups. Consult the documentation for your specific IdP, such as the [Okta documentation for Mapbox SAML apps](https://saml-doc.okta.com/SAML_Docs/How-to-Configure-SAML-2.0-for-Mapbox.html)
 for details.

#### Enabling SAML SSO for the Mapbox account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#enabling-saml-sso-for-the-mapbox-account "Direct link to Enabling SAML SSO for the Mapbox account")

To complete the initial connection between your identity provider and mapbox.com, enter the required information in the [Mapbox account's SSO setup page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/sso)
:

*   `Identity Provider sign-on URL`, also could be referred to as `SSO URL`, `SAML endpoint`, or `SSO sign-in URL` in your IdP
*   `Issuer ID`, also could be referred to as `Entity ID`, `Issuer`, or `Issuer URL` in your IdP
*   `X.509 Certificate`, pasted as text into the field.

Be sure to include `— BEGIN CERTIFICATE —` and `— END CERTIFICATE —` when pasting your X.509 certificate into the Mapbox form. You may need to open the X.509 certificate in a text editor to accurately copy the full body.

Click **Enable single sign-on** to submit the form. You will be prompted to re-authenticate.

#### Validating the SAML SSO integration[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#validating-the-saml-sso-integration "Direct link to Validating the SAML SSO integration")

Once you have saved the integration, return to your identity provider to assign the application to yourself and/or another member of the organization for testing. When assigned, try clicking the tile, "chiclet", or link for your Mapbox app to login from your identity provider's portal. If you receive an error when attempting to login with SAML, you can adjust the settings by clicking the `edit single sign on`.

#### Enforcing SAML SSO authentication[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#enforcing-saml-sso-authentication "Direct link to Enforcing SAML SSO authentication")

Enabling SSO for an account does not invalidate password authentication. The account's password is still a valid authentication method to make sure your organization's transition period to SAML authentication is seamless, and that direct password access to the account is available in the case of your identity provider has a service outage. Note that any users logging in with the account's password (and optional 2FA) will assume the `Root` user role.

To encourage your users transition from password based authentication to SAML login through your IdP, we recommend the following once the integration has been tested successfully:

*   Assign yourself the `Root` user role in your IdP so you will have access to settings
*   Assign most other users the `Admin` user role so they will not have access to settings
*   [Change the email](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-email-address)
     for the account to an email address that the `Root` user(s) can access
*   Save the new password in a safe location with least-privilege access (identity provider, shared password manager, IT vault)
*   If 2FA is enabled for the account, either turn it off or save the recovery codes with the new password
*   Announce to your organization that SAML SSO will be the primary mechanism for Mapbox access as of a future date. Encourage all users to test that they can access Mapbox with SAML before that date.
*   On the date of enforcement, toggle the `Activate single sign on` button on the account's [Security Settings page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/)
     to **immediately terminate all the account's active sessions**.
*   [Change the password](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#change-your-password)
     for the account, making the previous password invalid. As with activating Mapbox SSO, when the account's password is changed, all active sessions will be terminated. We recommend taking these two steps at the same time to prevent confusion for your users.

Once SSO is activated and the password has been changed, these updates should effectively push all application users to authentication with Single sign-on, as they'll no longer have the password.

**Questions about setting up SAML SSO? Visit the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 for recommendations and next steps to contact support.**

#### Deleting SAML SSO integration[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#deleting-saml-sso-integration "Direct link to Deleting SAML SSO integration")

You have the option to delete your SAML SSO integration. This is something you would need to do only in a rare situation -- generally, only if your organization has setup Single sign-on with a test account. Some identity providers can only be associated with a single Mapbox account. If you or your IT team has associated an IdP with a test account, we recommend deleting that integration before beginning setup for the organization's main account.

### Two-factor authentication[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#two-factor-authentication "Direct link to Two-factor authentication")

**Two-factor authentication (2FA)**, also known as multi-factor authentication (MFA) or two-step authentication, provides an optional, but recommended, layer of security for Mapbox accounts. Once enabled, all users will be prompted to enter the account's password as well as a security code generated on registered mobile devices whenever they log in to the account.

#### Enable two-factor authentication[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#enable-two-factor-authentication "Direct link to Enable two-factor authentication")

When logged in to an account, you can enable two-factor authentication from the [Security page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security)
. The Security page will include a barcode which you'll be prompted to scan with your mobile device, or a code that can by typed into password management services like 1Password.

Scan the generated barcode using an authenticator app on your mobile device. We recommend using Google Authenticator - it's free and available for [iOS](https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/google-authenticator/id388497605?mt=8)
 and [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.google.android.apps.authenticator2)
.

Your mobile device will display a 6-digit code. Type this code into the field below the barcode to complete the process.

#### Save your recovery code in a safe place so you can access the account if you lose your device[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#save-your-recovery-code-in-a-safe-place-so-you-can-access-the-account-if-you-lose-your-device "Direct link to Save your recovery code in a safe place so you can access the account if you lose your device")

Note that enabling 2FA for an account raises the likelihood that you or other users of the account may have trouble logging into the account in the future. Mapbox verifies ownership of an account by asking users to confirm the credit card number on file for that account. When 2FA is enabled on an account without a credit card, there is no way that the Support team can help users regain access to that account. Exercise care by storing 2FA recovery codes in a safe place and distributing them to new account owners when transferring ownership of an account. Consider enabling [SAML SSO](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
 for team accounts or accounts with multiple users, since many SSO providers require 2FA for logging into the IdP.

#### Use a recovery code to access an account without the registered two-factor device[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#use-a-recovery-code-to-access-an-account-without-the-registered-two-factor-device "Direct link to Use a recovery code to access an account without the registered two-factor device")

After you've set up two-factor authentication on an account, you will be redirected to a page with a **recovery code**. A recovery code is a single-use code that lets you sign in without your two-factor device.

**Write down this code and keep it in a safe place.** Treat your recovery code like a password to your account. If you lose your mobile device, you will need this code to log in to your account.

If you have already set up two-factor authentication on your account and do not have a recovery code, but can access the account with your 2FA device, go to your [Security page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security)
 to generate and retrieve a new recovery code for the account. Immediately store this code is a safe place, like a 1Password vault for individuals or teams.

To use the recovery code to login to an account, you'll need the account's account ID (username) or email, and the account's password. Follow the steps below to use your recovery code:

1.  Navigate to the [Sign in page](https://account.mapbox.com/auth/signin/)
    .
2.  Enter the account ID (username) and password, then click **Sign in**.
3.  Click the **Lost your mobile device?** link below the **Sign in** button. A new field for the recovery code will appear.
4.  Enter your recovery code in the new field and click **Sign in**.

Using the account's recovery code will temporarily deactivate two-factor authentication. This gives you a chance to configure a new two-factor authentication device, enable [SAML SSO authentication](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
 for teams, or decide not to reactivate 2FA for the account.

**Questions about enabling MFA or accessing an account with a recovery code? Visit the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 for recommendations and next steps to contact support.**

Delete your account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#delete-your-account "Direct link to Delete your account")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can delete an account by logging into your account, scrolling to the bottom of the [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
, and clicking the **Delete account** button:

**Account deletion is a permanent action that cannot be undone.** We will not be able to recover your account, and the same account ID (username) cannot be re-used to create another account.

After clicking the button to delete an account, there will be a modal that requires additional confirmation that you do intend to fully delete the account. When an account is deleted, the following are effective immediately:

*   All styles, projects, and data associated with the account are no longer available.
*   The account cannot be logged into
*   Access tokens tied to that account will be immediately terminated, and map implementations using them will no longer be able to effectively access Mapbox services.

Mapbox will not automatically prorate for any unused services or subscriptions, [contact our team for help](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#if-i-cannot-resolve-my-issue-what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request)
.

Privacy[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#privacy "Direct link to Privacy")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### GDPR compliance[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#gdpr-compliance "Direct link to GDPR compliance")

Mapbox Services are fully compliant with GDPR. For more information, read our [DPA](https://www.mapbox.com/dpa/)
 and [subprocessors](https://www.mapbox.com/dpa/subprocessors/)
 pages.

Mapbox is also Privacy Shield certified and is listed at [privacyshield.org](https://www.privacyshield.gov/participant?id=a2zt0000000CbWQAA0&status=Active)
.

Troubleshooting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#troubleshooting "Direct link to Troubleshooting")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Are you having trouble updating account settings, enabling SAML SSO, or logging into your account? Start here for some common troubleshooting tips for regaining account access or updating account settings. If you still cannot access your account after reviewing this documentation, include the details listed below in a [support request](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
. Our support team is standing by to help.

**I'm not receiving the email to verify my account's email address. What can I do?**

First, check that the email address registered to your account is a valid email address that has been spelled correctly, and that both the email address and email domain are configured to receive vendor email.

1.  **Invalid email addresses.** Emails cannot be delivered if the address contains a spelling error, the mailbox is full, the inbox does not exist, or one of many other reasons why an email address is unable to receive messages. If the address is invalid, follow the prompts on the [email confirmation page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/check-email/)
     to update spelling errors or change the account's registered email. This begins the verification process again with the new address.
    
2.  **Valid email addresses.** If you confirm that the email address is spelled properly and that the emails are not in your Spam folder or Promotions tab, _allow for up to 60 minutes of latency throughout your email system_. It's possible that your IT team maintains a filter above your inbox that requires additional processing and is delaying the email's arrival. Consider opening an internal ticket letting your IT team know about the problem, recommending they run a global search for emails from `no-reply@mapbox.com` or with the subject "verify your email address".
    

If the email address has not received an email after 60 minutes and is configured for receiving vendor email, [contact our team](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 so we troubleshoot with you based on the specific error codes being surfaced by your email provider.

**I'm not receiving the password reset email. What can I try next?**

First, check your spam folder to make sure our email hasn't been routed there. Not seeing it there, either? Sometimes password reset emails are undeliverable - maybe emails from Mapbox to your email address have bounced in the past or there have been other problems preventing the email from being delivered (especially for inactive email addresses).

If you are not receiving the password reset email, let us know [by submitting the support form](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

**I am locked out of a Mapbox account and need to regain access. What steps can I try?**

If you need to gain access to a Mapbox account, read the [account lockout troubleshooting guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/account-lockout/)
 which provides recommendations for common scenarios. Still have questions? Submit a [support request including these details](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#if-i-cannot-resolve-my-issue-what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request)
 to get in touch with our team.

**I do not have my two-factor (2FA) authentication recovery code. What are my options?**

If you do not have a 2FA recovery code for an account with 2FA enabled, the account must have a payment method on file for the Mapbox team to verify ownership of the account and remove two-factor authentication. [Contact our support team](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to get started.

_If an account does not have a credit card on file, we **will not** be able to verify account ownership._ In this case, the only next step will be to create a new Mapbox account and [add access tokens](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#creating-and-managing-access-tokens)
 from your new account into your map implementations.

**I'm having trouble setting up and/or configuring SAML Single Sign-on (SSO). Can you help?**

If you're having issues setting up SSO after following [the steps](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
 in our documentation, [submit a support request](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to get in touch with our team. As we troubleshoot with you, the more information about error codes and screenshots from your identity provider that you can include with your support request, the better!

It's possible that your question will be specific enough to your identity provider (IdP) that our team is unable to replicate your issue. In those cases, we may recommend that you contact your IdP's support resources directly with your configuration question.

### If I cannot resolve my issue, what information should I include in my support request?[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/#if-i-cannot-resolve-my-issue-what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request "Direct link to If I cannot resolve my issue, what information should I include in my support request?")

We're happy to help with your questions - [contact our support team with this form](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
. To help our team provide the most prompt resolution, include as much of the following information with your request:

**For account access issues:**

*   Account ID (username)
*   Account's registered email address
*   Access token (if applicable)

**For failed login issues [(with supported browsers)](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/mapbox-browser-support/#mapboxcom)
:**

*   Web browser(s) and versions
*   Any enabled web browser extensions you're using
*   Any enabled experimental flags (chrome://flags/) if using Google Chrome
*   Computer operating system and versions
*   Whether you have tested this issue while in incognito mode (if applicable)
*   Whether you have tested this issue with no browser extensions (if applicable)

If you do not know your browser information, you can go to the browser settings or the About page to find these details.

With the information you provide, we'll try to diagnose the issue and provide tips that help you produce the desired behavior. Thanks in advance for providing as much information as you can!

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# Statistics and usage | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Your account's **Statistics page** displays usage information for all _billable_ products found on the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/)
.

To access the Statistics page, log into your account and navigate to the [Statistics page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/statistics/)
 of your Developer Console.

Not included in this guide

The purpose of the Statistics page is to provide insight into an account's usage data for the billable products listed on the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/)
. Information about [API rate limits](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/#rate-limits)
, API performance, and HTTP status codes of requests to the Mapbox APIs are not displayed on the Statistics page. Visit the [API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/)
 for additional information about each service.

Filter usage data[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#filter-usage-data "Direct link to Filter usage data")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are two ways to filter the Mapbox usage data on your Statistics page:

*   **By date**: Choose a standard period (such as week or month) or enter a custom date range. All dates are in UTC (Greenwich Mean Time). Usage data will be available on the Statistics page within 24 hours.
*   **By access token**: See usage for all access tokens or one specific, active [access token](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/access-token/)
    . Legacy SDKs for Mobile statistics are not available when filtering by access token. To see Legacy SDKs for Mobile statistics, filter by **All tokens and connected apps**.

Browse by product[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#browse-by-product "Direct link to Browse by product")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your account usage is organized by product on your Statistics page. For each product, you'll see the number of metered units for the token and time period selected.You will only see statistics for the products you’ve used within the specified time period and with the specified token.

Additional metrics[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#additional-metrics "Direct link to Additional metrics")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For many products, you can load additional metrics about the _Countries_, _Browsers_, _Domains_, and _Tileset IDs_ associated with your requests. These additional metrics are presented as percentages of the total number of metered units for the specified time period and token.

Additional metrics are not available for products that are metered by user count (such as Maps SDK for Mobile). They are also not available for time periods exceeding 31 days.

Troubleshooting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#troubleshooting "Direct link to Troubleshooting")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Update frequency[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#update-frequency "Direct link to Update frequency")

If you began using a service for the first time or updated your implementation recently, allow up to 24 hours for the usage to be reflected on your Statistics page. You can check the status for all Mapbox services, including the Statistic's page, at [https://status.mapbox.com/](https://status.mapbox.com/)
.

### Relating service usage to service costs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#relating-service-usage-to-service-costs "Direct link to Relating service usage to service costs")

When reviewing your Statistics page, you can reference the [Pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/)
 and the [Pricing by product](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/)
 documentation to understand each service's metric of billing, monthly free tier, pricing, and progressive discounts. For a general overview of how Pay-as-you-go billing works, we recommend the guide for [What to Expect](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#what-to-expect)
 and the [Billing emails](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#billing-emails)
 documentation.

### _Not set_ and _Other_ values[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#not-set-and-other-values "Direct link to not-set-and-other-values")

When you see _Not set_ as an additional metric value, it means that for some requests we are not able to find a value for that metric. With the additional metric _Domains_, for example, _Not set_ means the request did not include a referrer from which we could parse a domain, which may because your requests are not coming from web browsers, or because you are using a legacy version of GL JS. Upgrading to [the latest version of GL JS](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/releases)
 should reduce the number of _Not set_ domains and is a rule for [implementing URL restrictions](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#url-restrictions)
.

When you see _Other_ as an additional metric value, it means our systems are not able to convert the value to a legible display format. For example, for the additional metric _Browsers_, _Other_ means we are not able to simplify the request's user agent to a display format.

### Statistics for deleted access tokens[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#statistics-for-deleted-access-tokens "Direct link to Statistics for deleted access tokens")

When an access token is [deleted](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#rotating-access-tokens)
, caching throughout our [global infrastructure](https://www.mapbox.com/platform/)
 may result in successful requests to that deleted token. Caching settings vary - not all Mapbox services cache requests for the same length of time.

It is not possible to isolate statistics for a token after it has been deleted. Deleted tokens do not show up in the drop-down filter list, rather, usage data tied to a token before and after its deletion will be aggregated in the **All tokens and connected apps** view.

### Statistics for temporary access tokens[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#statistics-for-temporary-access-tokens "Direct link to Statistics for temporary access tokens")

Temporary tokens (`tk`) are not displayed in the drop-down filter list. Instead, usage data tied to [temporary tokens](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#create-a-temporary-token)
 will be aggregated in the **All tokens and connected apps** view. To visualize statistics generated by `tk` tokens in a time period, append `?&token=tk` to the end of the statistic page's URL.

### Statistics for legacy services by token[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#statistics-for-legacy-services-by-token "Direct link to Statistics for legacy services by token")

It's possible that your Mapbox implementation is using a legacy version of a Mapbox service that does not pass access tokens to our servers with it's requests. If this is the case, usage data cannot be isolated by token. Usage in this category will be aggregated and displayed when the **All tokens and connected apps** view is selected.

Similarly, Legacy SDKs for Mobile statistics are not available when filtering by access token. To see Legacy SDKs for Mobile statistics, filter by **All tokens and connected apps**. To isolate MAUs by token from a specific time moving forward, upgrade your applications to the latest versions of the Maps SDKs, and review statistics after the update is deployed.

### Inconsistencies between usage on your Statistics page and charges on your monthly invoice[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#inconsistencies-between-usage-on-your-statistics-page-and-charges-on-your-monthly-invoice "Direct link to Inconsistencies between usage on your Statistics page and charges on your monthly invoice")

If you've set the custom date on your Statistics page to match your billing cycle and notice a discrepancy in the values of services you were billed for, it's most likely an issue with timezones. Requests attributed to your billing period that were not invoiced during that month will roll over to the next invoice.

**Have questions about the Statistics page? [Let our Support team know](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
\- we're happy to help!**

Feature requests[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#feature-requests "Direct link to Feature requests")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### CSV Export[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#csv-export "Direct link to CSV Export")

The Statistics page does not support an export or download feature. Contact your Technical Account Manager or Account Manager to submit a feature request, or [contact support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to share your use case.

### Programmatic access[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#programmatic-access "Direct link to Programmatic access")

Mapbox usage data is not available through an API service. Contact your Technical Account Manager or Account Manager to submit a feature request, or [contact support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to share your use case.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# Statistics and usage | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Your account's **Statistics page** displays usage information for all _billable_ products found on the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/)
.

To access the Statistics page, log into your account and navigate to the [Statistics page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/statistics/)
 of your Developer Console.

Not included in this guide

The purpose of the Statistics page is to provide insight into an account's usage data for the billable products listed on the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/)
. Information about [API rate limits](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/#rate-limits)
, API performance, and HTTP status codes of requests to the Mapbox APIs are not displayed on the Statistics page. Visit the [API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/)
 for additional information about each service.

Filter usage data[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#filter-usage-data "Filter usage data への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are two ways to filter the Mapbox usage data on your Statistics page:

*   **By date**: Choose a standard period (such as week or month) or enter a custom date range. All dates are in UTC (Greenwich Mean Time). Usage data will be available on the Statistics page within 24 hours.
*   **By access token**: See usage for all access tokens or one specific, active [access token](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/access-token/)
    . Legacy SDKs for Mobile statistics are not available when filtering by access token. To see Legacy SDKs for Mobile statistics, filter by **All tokens and connected apps**.

Browse by product[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#browse-by-product "Browse by product への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your account usage is organized by product on your Statistics page. For each product, you'll see the number of metered units for the token and time period selected.You will only see statistics for the products you’ve used within the specified time period and with the specified token.

Additional metrics[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#additional-metrics "Additional metrics への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For many products, you can load additional metrics about the _Countries_, _Browsers_, _Domains_, and _Tileset IDs_ associated with your requests. These additional metrics are presented as percentages of the total number of metered units for the specified time period and token.

Additional metrics are not available for products that are metered by user count (such as Maps SDK for Mobile). They are also not available for time periods exceeding 31 days.

Troubleshooting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#troubleshooting "Troubleshooting への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Update frequency[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#update-frequency "Update frequency への直接リンク")

If you began using a service for the first time or updated your implementation recently, allow up to 24 hours for the usage to be reflected on your Statistics page. You can check the status for all Mapbox services, including the Statistic's page, at [https://status.mapbox.com/](https://status.mapbox.com/)
.

### Relating service usage to service costs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#relating-service-usage-to-service-costs "Relating service usage to service costs への直接リンク")

When reviewing your Statistics page, you can reference the [Pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/)
 and the [Pricing by product](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/pricing/)
 documentation to understand each service's metric of billing, monthly free tier, pricing, and progressive discounts. For a general overview of how Pay-as-you-go billing works, we recommend the guide for [What to Expect](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#what-to-expect)
 and the [Billing emails](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#billing-emails)
 documentation.

### _Not set_ and _Other_ values[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#not-set-and-other-values "not-set-and-other-values への直接リンク")

When you see _Not set_ as an additional metric value, it means that for some requests we are not able to find a value for that metric. With the additional metric _Domains_, for example, _Not set_ means the request did not include a referrer from which we could parse a domain, which may because your requests are not coming from web browsers, or because you are using a legacy version of GL JS. Upgrading to [the latest version of GL JS](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/releases)
 should reduce the number of _Not set_ domains and is a rule for [implementing URL restrictions](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#url-restrictions)
.

When you see _Other_ as an additional metric value, it means our systems are not able to convert the value to a legible display format. For example, for the additional metric _Browsers_, _Other_ means we are not able to simplify the request's user agent to a display format.

### Statistics for deleted access tokens[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#statistics-for-deleted-access-tokens "Statistics for deleted access tokens への直接リンク")

When an access token is [deleted](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#rotating-access-tokens)
, caching throughout our [global infrastructure](https://www.mapbox.com/platform/)
 may result in successful requests to that deleted token. Caching settings vary - not all Mapbox services cache requests for the same length of time.

It is not possible to isolate statistics for a token after it has been deleted. Deleted tokens do not show up in the drop-down filter list, rather, usage data tied to a token before and after its deletion will be aggregated in the **All tokens and connected apps** view.

### Statistics for temporary access tokens[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#statistics-for-temporary-access-tokens "Statistics for temporary access tokens への直接リンク")

Temporary tokens (`tk`) are not displayed in the drop-down filter list. Instead, usage data tied to [temporary tokens](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/accounts/#create-a-temporary-token)
 will be aggregated in the **All tokens and connected apps** view. To visualize statistics generated by `tk` tokens in a time period, append `?&token=tk` to the end of the statistic page's URL.

### Statistics for legacy services by token[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#statistics-for-legacy-services-by-token "Statistics for legacy services by token への直接リンク")

It's possible that your Mapbox implementation is using a legacy version of a Mapbox service that does not pass access tokens to our servers with it's requests. If this is the case, usage data cannot be isolated by token. Usage in this category will be aggregated and displayed when the **All tokens and connected apps** view is selected.

Similarly, Legacy SDKs for Mobile statistics are not available when filtering by access token. To see Legacy SDKs for Mobile statistics, filter by **All tokens and connected apps**. To isolate MAUs by token from a specific time moving forward, upgrade your applications to the latest versions of the Maps SDKs, and review statistics after the update is deployed.

### Inconsistencies between usage on your Statistics page and charges on your monthly invoice[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#inconsistencies-between-usage-on-your-statistics-page-and-charges-on-your-monthly-invoice "Inconsistencies between usage on your Statistics page and charges on your monthly invoice への直接リンク")

If you've set the custom date on your Statistics page to match your billing cycle and notice a discrepancy in the values of services you were billed for, it's most likely an issue with timezones. Requests attributed to your billing period that were not invoiced during that month will roll over to the next invoice.

**Have questions about the Statistics page? [Let our Support team know](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
\- we're happy to help!**

Feature requests[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#feature-requests "Feature requests への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### CSV Export[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#csv-export "CSV Export への直接リンク")

The Statistics page does not support an export or download feature. Contact your Technical Account Manager or Account Manager to submit a feature request, or [contact support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to share your use case.

### Programmatic access[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#programmatic-access "Programmatic access への直接リンク")

Mapbox usage data is not available through an API service. Contact your Technical Account Manager or Account Manager to submit a feature request, or [contact support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to share your use case.

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Accounts and pricing
====================

*   check
    
    Create and manage access tokens
    
*   check
    
    View usage statistics
    
*   check
    
    Manage invoices and billing methods
    
*   check
    
    Update account profile and security settings
    

Learn how to navigate your Mapbox accounts page, understand how usage is measured, and how billing works.

Account page[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/#account-page "Direct link to Account page")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your [Account page](https://console.mapbox.com/)
 contains all the information and preferences related to your Mapbox account. It's also where you:

*   Create and manage your **access tokens**, which enable you to use Mapbox tools, including our mobile SDKs and APIs. Read more in [Token management](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/)
    .
*   View **statistics** and gain insights into how your applications are being used by tracking the number of mobile users, API requests, and map loads associated with the applications using your access tokens. Read more in [Statistics and usage](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/)
    .
*   View, print, and add additional information (for example, your address) to your Pay-as-you-go **invoices**. Read more in [Invoices and billing](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/)
    .
*   Manage general account **settings** including profile information (like your organization name, email address, and avatar), your password, two-factor authentication, payment information for your account, and more. Read more in [Settings and account management](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/settings/)
    .

Pricing by product[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/#pricing-by-product "Direct link to Pricing by product")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Read more about how your unique workflows and applications translate to usage and invoice line items in the [Pricing by product](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/)
 section.

Related resources[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/#related-resources "Direct link to Related resources")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Learn terms related to accounts and billing in the [**Glossary**](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/)
    . The Glossary includes terms like map load, monthly active users, and more.
*   Get answers about access tokens, learn how to interpret your usage, and more in our [**Troubleshooting guides**](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/#access-tokens)
    .
*   Read reference documentation for the Tokens API in our [**API documentation**](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#tokens)
    , including information about [rate limits](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/#rate-limits)
     and explanations about [error codes by service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#tokens-api-errors)
    .
*   Review the Mapbox [Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos/)
    , [Privacy policy](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/privacy)
    , and [Service Level Agreement (SLA)](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/sla)
     for your account.
