# Invoices and billing | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Mapbox provides Pay-as-you-go customers an invoice for all costs accrued each month. An account's [invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
 contains monthly invoices and billing history.

To access the account's, log into the account and navigate to the [Invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
 of the Developer Console.

What to expect[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#what-to-expect "What to expect への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mapbox accounts are free to create, and all customers with Pay-as-you-go billing receive a monthly [free tier of services](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/)
 that resets on the first day of the account's monthly billing period. We will send an [email notification](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#exceeded-free-tier)
 to the account's email address the first time a free tier is exceeded during each billing period.

Every month, a new invoice is generated on the first day of a new billing period. This upcoming invoice is generated even if an account has not yet incurred overages for that billing period. Usage data is reflected on the upcoming invoice within 24 hours, and charges are always in USD.

Invoices will be finalized on the last day of the account's billing period. When your invoice is finalized, your payment method on file is automatically charged. If the charge fails, the account will be at risk of deactivation and service interruptions. By using Mapbox services, you agree to pay all fees associated with the services.

### Billing period[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#billing-period "Billing period への直接リンク")

The [invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
 provides insight into an account's monthly billing period. Billing periods start on the first day of the month and end on the last day of the month (for example: May 1 - May 31, September 1 - September 30).

#### Shortened first month[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#shortened-first-month "Shortened first month への直接リンク")

The account's billing period will be shortened during the first month of account creation or account reactivation. For example, if the account is created or reactivated on September 15, the first billing period will be September 15 - September 30. Every following billing period will be from the first day of the month to the last day of the month (November 1 - November 30, December 1 - December 31, etc).

### Free tiers[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#free-tiers "Free tiers への直接リンク")

Most Mapbox services include a free tier of services available to all accounts during each billing period. The number of requests, elements, users, sessions, or other units included in the free tier varies by Mapbox product. You can review the free tier for each product on the [Pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing)
.

Invoices[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#invoices "Invoices への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Understanding invoices[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#understanding-invoices "Understanding invoices への直接リンク")

Each invoice listed on an account's [invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
 includes the following:

*   **Invoice Number**: a unique identifier, distinct for each Mapbox invoice.
*   **Status**: the status of the invoice. [View all possible invoice statuses](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#invoice-statuses)
    .
*   **Invoice Date**: the date on which the invoice will be finalized. This is the date on which the amount due will be charged to the account's credit card or [prepaid credit](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#prepaid-credit)
    .
*   **Amount Due**: the total dollar value in USD due for all services, after factoring in bulk discounts (if applicable).
*   **Pay button**: a link to a secure, self-serve portal for paying a `Past Due` invoice, hosted by our payment provider.

### Invoice statuses[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#invoice-statuses "Invoice statuses への直接リンク")

Each Mapbox invoice displayed on the [invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
 includes an invoice status, indicating:

| Status | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Paid` | The invoice has been successfully paid. |
| `Upcoming` | This is the account's current invoice. The current invoice is still actively being updated with usage from the current billing period, and has not yet been finalized. |
| `Past Due` | This invoice has not yet been paid, and is past its due date. |
| `Open` | The invoice is awaiting payment according to the account's payment terms. Only available for [Monthly Invoicing](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#monthly-invoicing)<br> customers. |
| `Uncollectible` | This invoice will likely not be paid. Invoices can only have this status if the Mapbox Support team has taken action on the invoice. |
| `Void` | This invoice contains a mistake and does not require payment. Invoices can only have this status if the Mapbox Support team has taken action on the invoice. |

#### Account deactivation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#account-deactivation "Account deactivation への直接リンク")

A `Past Due` invoice will automatically be retried multiple times over a short grace period. During this grace period, we will send multiple email notifications detailing any failed charges and reminding the account holder about the upcoming deactivation. If the invoice is still `Past Due` after the final charge attempt, the account will be deactivated. When an account is deactivated, all access tokens and Mapbox services are immediately suspended until the account is reactivated.

Current Month Usage

If your account is deactivated in the middle of a billing cycle, an invoice will be created containing the usage accumulated so far from that cycle. This invoice will be added alongside your other previous invoices and must be paid for your account to be reactivated.

#### Reactivating an account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#reactivating-an-account "Reactivating an account への直接リンク")

An account that has been deactivated will be locked until a valid payment method is entered into the persistent pop-up on [Developer Console](https://console.mapbox.com/)
 and all `Past Due` invoices on the have been paid.

To pay `Past Due` invoices, navigate to your [invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
 and click the **Pay** button. You cannot reactivate your account until you have paid your `Past Due` invoices.

### Structure of invoices[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#structure-of-invoices "Structure of invoices への直接リンク")

Each Mapbox invoice includes three kinds of information:

*   **Account information**: including the email address, VAT Tax ID, and Purchase Order Number associated with an account and any [additional information](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#adding-custom-information-to-an-invoice)
     that has been added to the invoices.
*   **Invoice information**: including the status, date issued, payment due date, billing period, and a unique invoice number.
*   **Usage information**: including line item for each service for which the account's usage exceeded what is included in the free tier, and any applicable volume-based discounts.

#### Details of charges[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#details-of-charges "Details of charges への直接リンク")

If an account has a recurring subscription or service overage charges, there will be line items listing those services. See an example line items below:

Optimization API:

product

20,000

quantity

requests

unit

$9.50 chevron-down

cost and details

The product identifies the product the account is using. The quantity for each line item on the invoice will reflect amount of the usage accrued throughout the billing period. The unit describes the billing units associated with the quantity used. The cost will show the total amount in USD for that product, and you can click chevron-down to see details about how that total was calculated with any applicable discount tiers.

#### Line items[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#line-items "Line items への直接リンク")

To learn more about how applications built with various Mapbox tools translate to particular line items, see the [pricing by products guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/pricing/)
.

If a discount has been applied to an account, it will be displayed as a `discount` line item on the invoices during each billing period in which the discount is active. Information about the discount's expiration date can be viewed in the [billing settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing)
.

#### Preview line items[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#preview-line-items "Preview line items への直接リンク")

One type of line item that may be included on an account's invoices are "preview line items." Preview line items are free and provide insight into the costs for beta products which have not launched in general availability yet. Once the beta product has launched in general availability, the costs will no longer be free. When viewing an invoice, it's possible to toggle preview line items `on` to visualize future costs, or toggle them `off` to focus on active billable products. You will only see preview line items if the account is using beta products.

If a Mapbox service updates their preview pricing, only invoices with `Upcoming` status _on or after the date of the update_ will reflect the updated preview pricing. Changes to preview pricing are not added on any of an account's `Paid` or `Past Due` invoices.

#### Downloading or printing invoices[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#downloading-or-printing-invoices "Downloading or printing invoices への直接リンク")

To download or print the invoice:

1.  Log into the account and navigate to the [invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
    .
2.  Open the invoice you want to download/print.
3.  Click the **Print** button and use the print options to save the file as a PDF.

#### Adding custom information to an invoice[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#adding-custom-information-to-an-invoice "Adding custom information to an invoice への直接リンク")

To add a Purchase Order Number (PO Number), VAT Tax ID, or other optional information to all future invoices, follow these steps:

1.  Log into the account and navigate to the [settings page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing)
    .
2.  Enter the PO Number, VAT Tax ID, and/or a billing email address in the appropriate text fields.
3.  Enter any additional optional information in the `Memo` section
4.  Click **Save changes**.

### Sales tax and VAT information[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#sales-tax-and-vat-information "Sales tax and VAT information への直接リンク")

Services provided by Mapbox are subject to being taxed depending on the location of your company address and products consumed. For instance, customers based in the United States may be charged US Sales Tax. Those in the European Union, UK, Mexico, South Africa, Switzerland, and some other countries may be charged VAT instead.

Mapbox will not apply taxes to services provided for customers who provide valid tax exemption documentation.

#### U.S. sales tax[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#us-sales-tax "U.S. sales tax への直接リンク")

U.S. customers may be subject to state and local sales taxes. Sales tax charges will be included as a line item on your invoice.

States where products and services of Mapbox, Inc. are subject to sales tax

Arizona (AZ),Colorado (CO),Connecticut (CT),District of Columbia (DC),Florida (FL),Georgia (GA),Illinois (IL),Indiana (IN),Iowa (IA),Kansas (KS),Kentucky (KY),Louisiana (LA),Maine (ME),Maryland (MD),Massachusetts (MA),Michigan (MI),Minnesota (MN),Nebraska (NE),Nevada (NV),New Jersey (NJ),New Mexico (NM),New York (NY),North Carolina (NC),North Dakota (ND),Ohio (OH),Pennsylvania (PA),South Carolina (SC),Tennessee (TN),Texas (TX),Utah (UT),Virginia (VA),Vermont (VT),Washington (WA),Wisconsin (WI) State and local tax laws are constantly changing. As tax laws continue to evolve, these jurisdictions are subject to change.

#### Value-added tax (VAT)[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#value-added-tax-vat "Value-added tax (VAT) への直接リンク")

For international customers, Mapbox will charge value-added tax (VAT) on products and services where applicable.

#### Add or update tax information[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#add-or-update-tax-information "Add or update tax information への直接リンク")

To add or update your VAT ID, login to your account and go to your [billing settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing)
 and use the `VAT Tax ID` field. The number entered here will be added to your monthly invoices.

#### Request an exemption[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#request-an-exemption "Request an exemption への直接リンク")

If you are a tax-exempt customer, contact the Mapbox Accounts Receivable team ([accountsreceivable@mapbox.com](mailto:accountsreceivable@mapbox.com)
) to provide your sales tax exemption certificate.

#### Request a sales tax refund[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#request-a-sales-tax-refund "Request a sales tax refund への直接リンク")

Sales tax is generally applied to all invoices with taxable products where valid exemption documentation hasn’t been provided to Mapbox. If sales tax has been applied to your invoice, but you can provide valid exemption documentation covering the purchase period, you can get a refund on the sales tax portion of your payment if you contact [accountsreceivable@mapbox.com](mailto:accountsreceivable@mapbox.com)
 within 30 days of the invoice date. After 30 days from the invoice date, you will need to contact your local state tax authority to process a refund.

Payments[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#payments "Payments への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Payment setting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#payment-setting "Payment setting への直接リンク")

#### Automatic payments[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#automatic-payments "Automatic payments への直接リンク")

The default payment setting for all customers is automatic payments. With automatic payments, the account's credit card on file (or [prepaid credit](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#prepaid-credit)
) will be automatically charged when the **Upcoming** invoice is finalized. Mapbox accepts the following credit, debit, prepaid, and gift cards: Visa, MasterCard, American Express, JCB, Discover, and Diner's club.

If you do not have access to a credit or debit card, consider using a prepaid card available from several vendors, including:

*   [American Express](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/prepaid/view-all-cards/)
    
*   [Visa](https://usa.visa.com/pay-with-visa/cards/card-finder/prepaid-finder-page.html)
    
*   [MasterCard](http://www.mastercard.us/prepaid-card.html)
    

If your organization is unable to make payments with a credit card, [contact our Sales team](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/)
 to learn more about the requirements for the [Monthly Invoicing](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#monthly-invoicing)
 beta program.

#### Monthly Invoicing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#monthly-invoicing "Monthly Invoicing への直接リンク")

Public beta

Monthly Invoicing is in public beta. There are minimum monthly spending requirements to qualify for the beta. To learn more about the spending requirements and enroll in the beta, [contact our Sales team](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/)
.

With Monthly Invoicing, the account's invoice is not automatically charged to a credit card on file. Instead, an invoice is emailed to the account's billing email address. The invoice then must be paid promptly.

When an account is enrolled in Monthly Invoicing, the option to add a billing email address will automatically appear in the [account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing/)
 page.

Monthly Invoicing is not available for all customers. To qualify for Monthly Invoicing, you must be working with a Mapbox Account Manager and your account must be in good standing. [Reach out](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/sales/)
 to our Sales team to inquire about Monthly Invoicing options for your organization.

### Add or update payment method[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#add-or-update-payment-method "Add or update payment method への直接リンク")

You can add or update a credit card on an account by visiting the [billing settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing)
 and clicking the **Add payment method** button or the **Edit** button if you are updating the credit card.

### Prepaid credit[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#prepaid-credit "Prepaid credit への直接リンク")

You can add prepaid credit to be used for future Mapbox services. Prepaid credit can be added any time and cannot be refunded in the case it's no longer needed. Account credit will be applied to future invoices on a recurring basis until the credit is $0.00. Mapbox won't charge the credit card on file until the account's prepaid balance is $0.00.

To add prepaid credit to the account, follow these instructions:

1.  Navigate to the [billing settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing)
    .
2.  Make sure you have a valid credit card on file.
3.  Click the **Add credit** button.
4.  Enter the amount of credit you want to buy.
5.  The credit card will be charged immediately, and the account's email address will receive a receipt for the payment.
6.  After buying credit, a record will be available in the billing settings and on the `Upcoming` invoice.

Prepaid credit cannot be applied to a Past Due invoice

If an account has experienced a failed payment, **prepaid credit _cannot_ be used to pay a `Past Due` invoice**. The account balance will only be applied to invoices with `Upcoming` status or an invoice date in the future. To pay a `Past Due` invoice directly, [see the troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#paying-invoices-with-past-due-status)
.

### Discounts[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#discounts "Discounts への直接リンク")

Coupon codes can be added to the account's [billing settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/billing)
. Only one coupon can be active at a time.

#### Non-profit and educational discounts[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#non-profit-and-educational-discounts "Non-profit and educational discounts への直接リンク")

The Mapbox [Community team](https://www.mapbox.com/community/)
 provides tailored support for non-profits, educational institutions, and positive-impact organizations. If you need a discount for your positive-impact project [contact the Community team](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000301392)
.

### Refunds[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#refunds "Refunds への直接リンク")

If Mapbox has issued a refund, the funds will be returned to the account's original payment method 5-10 business days after the refund has been processed. Individual Mapbox invoices will not be reflected to show refunds - if you require additional receipts for the refund, [reach out to our support team](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request)
.

Billing emails[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#billing-emails "Billing emails への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We will send the following billing emails to the profile email address registered under the account's [main settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
, even if the account is enrolled in the [Monthly Invoicing beta](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#monthly-invoicing)
.

### Exceeded free tier[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#exceeded-free-tier "Exceeded free tier への直接リンク")

Email subject: `[Mapbox Billing] Usage notification`

Mapbox sends the exceeded free tier email when an account's usage exceeds one or more of a service's free tier during a billing period, we will send an email with the subject. This notification is a courtesy notice sent to all accounts the first time a free tier is exceeded during a billing period, even if there is a valid payment method or adequate prepaid credit on file.

### Successful payment[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#successful-payment "Successful payment への直接リンク")

Email subject: `[Mapbox Billing] Successful payment receipt`

Mapbox will send a payment receipt for all successful payments. The email will include a link to download a PDF payment receipt.

### Failed payment[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#failed-payment "Failed payment への直接リンク")

Email subject: `[Mapbox Billing] payment failure`

Mapbox will send a series of failed payment emails every time a payment fails. The emails include instructions for updating your registered payment method or paying an unpaid invoice with a secure, one-time payment page. See the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#troubleshooting)
 for more detailed recommendations.

### Account deactivation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#account-deactivation-1 "Account deactivation への直�接リンク")

Email subject: `[Mapbox Billing] Account deactivation notice`

If an account as been deactivated for failed payments, Mapbox will send a deactivation notice. This email includes a link to add or update the account's payment method, which will reactivate the account and restore Mapbox services.

### Updated payment information[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#updated-payment-information "Updated payment information への直接リンク")

Email subject: `[Mapbox Billing] Your payment info has been updated`

Mapbox sends a notification when the account's [payment method](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#add-or-update-payment-method)
 is updated.

### Coupon expiring[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#coupon-expiring "Coupon expiring への直接リンク")

Email subject: `[Mapbox Billing] Your coupon expires soon`

If the account has a discount applied, Mapbox will send an email reminder when the coupon is within 10 days of expiring.

### Low credit[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#low-credit "Low credit への直接リンク")

Email subject: `[Mapbox Billing] Low credit notice`

Mapbox will send a low credit notice when the prepaid credit balance is low. The low credit notice includes the account ID, the account balance (amount of credit remaining), and a link to the account's billing settings where you can add more credit.

Feature requests[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#feature-requests "Feature requests への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Below are common feature requests. If any of these features are important to you, [let us know](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

### Secondary billing email addresses[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#secondary-billing-email-addresses "Secondary billing email addresses への直接リンク")

Secondary billing email addresses can be used to share invoices directly with your Finance departments or with clients. Secondary billing emails are only available to customers on [Monthly Invoicing](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#monthly-invoicing)
. For all other customers, Mapbox recommends using a group or alias email address for the account or setting up a forwarding rule in your email provider that automatically forwards billing emails to relevant stakeholders.

You can also consider using a group or alias email address like `mapbox-team@company.com` for your account and granting multiple people access to that address so additional stakeholders have visibility into account notifications like billing emails.

### Shipping address[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#shipping-address "Shipping address への直接リンク")

For the vast majority of customers, your billing address is the same as the location where you are predominately using our products (access to our services). In this case, you do _not_ need to have a shipping address on your profile.

If you are a customer where Mapbox’s products (access to our services) are delivered to a location other than your billing address, then a shipping address will need to be included on your profile. The shipping address will be used for the calculation of sales tax, GST, or VAT. If you need to update your shipping address, reach out to [tax@mapbox.com](mailto:tax@mapbox.com)
.

### Spending alerts[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#spending-alerts "Spending alerts への直接リンク")

Mapbox does not yet offer configurable usage notifications via email or SMS notifications. For example, there is no option to send an email when the account reaches 50% of any product's free tier during the billing period.

### Spending cap[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#spending-cap "Spending cap への直接リンク")

Mapbox accounts do not support a spending cap customizable usage limit after which services cut off during a billing period.

For now, you can create predictable monthly spending by adding a set amount of prepaid credit and monitoring the statistics and invoices closely. Once an invoice reaches an amount you'd like to pay for that billing period, you can [rotate the account's access tokens](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#rotating-access-tokens)
 (deactivating older tokens).

When access tokens are rotated, production maps will become deactivated and the requests from the deleted tokens will fail. When the new billing period begins, rotate the new tokens into Mapbox implementations to resume service usage.

Troubleshooting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#troubleshooting "Troubleshooting への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Credit card decline[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#credit-card-decline "Credit card decline への直接リンク")

To protect your privacy, banks don't give detailed information to Mapbox when credit card payments fail. If the card information seems correct, contact your bank to ask for more information or to allow payments to Mapbox.com. If payments are still being declined after contacting your bank, try adding a different credit card for the payment.

[Contact our support team](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request)
 to let us know when you're working with your bank so we can help prevent account deactivation to keep your account's Mapbox services live.

### Insufficient prepaid credit[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#insufficient-prepaid-credit "Insufficient prepaid credit への直接リンク")

If an account has [prepaid credit](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#prepaid-credit)
 on file but the credit balance is not enough to cover the full amount due for an invoice, Mapbox will charge the remaining amount to your credit card on file. In these cases, if there is no valid credit card on file as a secondary payment method or the credit card charge fails, the invoice will remain `Past Due` and the account will be at risk of [deactivation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#account-deactivation)
.

To prevent deactivation, add or update the credit card on the account. Prepaid credit [_cannot be used_](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#prepaid-credit)
 to pay for an invoice that has already finalized.

### Paying invoices with `Past Due` status[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#paying-invoices-with-past-due-status "paying-invoices-with-past-due-status への直接リンク")

To pay invoices with `Past Due` statuses:

1.  Log into the account.
2.  Navigate to the account's [invoices page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/invoices/)
    .
3.  Click the **Pay** button next to any invoice with a `Past Due` status.

Note

*   Paying via the **Pay** button _will not_ save or update the credit card on the account.- [Prepaid credit](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#prepaid-credit)
     _cannot_ be used to pay `Past Due` invoices.- Mapbox reserves the right to retry charges for `Past Due` invoices based on the [Mapbox Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos/)
    .

### Currency[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#currency "Currency への直接リンク")

Mapbox invoices are issued in USD. An invoice with `Paid` status will display the total paid in USD, even if your payment processed in another currency. If you are comparing a financial statement from a credit card or bank to a Mapbox invoice and the amounts are not aligned, apply the exchange rate on the date of the charge for your currency to USD.

How to contact Mapbox support with issues[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#how-to-contact-mapbox-support-with-issues "How to contact Mapbox support with issues への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you cannot resolve an issue with your account, you can reach out to Mapbox support and we'll be happy to help with your questions.

### What information should I include in my support request?[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request "What information should I include in my support request? への直接リンク")

We can provide the most prompt resolution when you include the following information in [your support submission](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
:

*   Invoice Number
*   Invoice Date
*   Account ID or registered email address
*   Explanation of the issue you are experiencing
*   What you've already tried to remedy the situation

この page は役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

---

# Settings and account access | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

The **Settings page** is where you can [delete an account](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#delete-your-account)
 or manage the account's profile information, billing and authentication methods, and connected apps. To access the Settings page, log into the account and navigate to the [settings section](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
 of your account. Once on the page, scroll to the bottom and you'll see the blue delete account button.

Note that for accounts using [SAML Single-sign on](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
 authentication, only users with the `Root` role assigned in their identity provider can access and update settings.

Profile[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#profile "Profile への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All users who login to an account with the account's password assume `Root` user privileges by default, and can access and update the account's settings. Or, if the account has enabled [SAML Single sign-on authentication](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
, there are two user roles that are available: `Root` and `Admin`, which are assigned to users in an identity provider (like Okta). For accounts with SSO enabled, only users with the `Root` role will be able to access and update settings as described below. [Learn more about user roles in the SAML SSO documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
.

### Verify an account's email address[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#verify-an-accounts-email-address "Verify an account's email address への直接リンク")

To be sure that you can receive important notifications about your account, all accounts must verify the registered email address by clicking a link that we send immediately upon account creation. The verification process must be completed to gain full access to the Mapbox applications. Verification links are valid for 24 hours and can be re-requested when logged into your account. If an account's email address is updated, you will be prompted to verify the new address.

Having trouble verifying your account's email address? Visit the [troubleshooting](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 section for recommendations.

### Change an account's email address[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-email-address "Change an account's email address への直接リンク")

Each Mapbox account supports one registered email address. To update an account's email address, follow these steps:

1.  Log in and navigate to your [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
     page, and click the `Profile` tab.
2.  Enter the new email address and click **Save changes**.

An account's registered email address is the primary email address to which we send important account and [billing notifications](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#billing-emails)
. If you are [inheriting an account](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/#inheriting-an-account-from-a-developer-designer-or-consultant)
 from a team member, consultant, or client, update the primary email address to be one that multiple members of your organization have access to, like `developers@mapbox.com`. Similarly, if you are transferring an account to a team, consultant, or client, changing the registered email address on the account from your own to one they have access to make sure important notifications are not missed.

### Change an account's username (not possible)[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-username-not-possible "Change an account's username (not possible) への直接リンク")

It is not possible to change an account's ID (username). Doing so would break links to maps tied to that account and could cause other unanticipated problems with implementations that depend on that account ID.

Instead, you can [change the account holder's name](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#change-an-account-holders-name)
 or create a new account, [transfer your map styles](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/transfer-styles-between-accounts/)
 and update your web and mobile applications to reference the new account's tokens and styles. For more information about setting up an account for collaboration or preparing for an ownership transition, see [our guide for collaboration](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/#best-practices-for-setting-up-an-account)
.

Once this transition is complete, you can [delete](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#delete-your-account)
 the original account without breaking any links to your maps.

### Change an account holder's name[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#change-an-account-holders-name "Change an account holder's name への直接リンク")

To change the account holder's name, follow these steps:

1.  Log in and navigate to your [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
     page, and click the `Profile` tab.
2.  Enter the updated name in the "Name" field and click **Save changes**.

If your account's type is "Business", you can also update your organization's name on the same `Profile` tab of the [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
 page.

To update the details about your organization that are included on your invoice, see the documentation for [adding custom information to an invoice](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/#adding-custom-information-to-an-invoice)
.

**Questions about updating the profile information for an account? Visit the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 for recommendations and next steps to contact support.**

Password[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#password "Password への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Change your password[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#change-your-password "Change your password への直接リンク")

1.  Log in and navigate to your [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/change-password/)
    .
2.  Enter your current password and new password in the appropriate fields.
3.  If you have two-factor authentication enabled, enter your two-factor authentication code.
4.  Click **Save new password**.

### Reset your password[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#reset-your-password "Reset your password への直接リンク")

If you're having trouble logging in to an account, try [resetting the account's password](https://account.mapbox.com/reset-password)
 by providing the email address that's associated with the account. We'll send the registered email on file for that account an email with further instructions to reset the password.

The link that we send in the password reset email is only valid for 24 hours. If you receive an `Invalid token` error message or have waited more than 24 hours, you'll need to request another password reset email.

**Questions about changing or resetting an account's password? Not receiving the reset password email? Visit the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 for recommendations and next steps to contact support.**

Security[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#security "Security への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mapbox is critical infrastructure for our customers. We go to significant lengths to protect the security of your account, your data, and your users. Visit our [Security page](https://www.mapbox.com/platform/security)
 for more information about how we [process payments](https://www.mapbox.com/platform/security/#payment-processing)
, [store data](https://www.mapbox.com/platform/security/#data-security)
, and [conduct regular audits](https://www.mapbox.com/platform/security/#regular-audits)
.

An account's [**Security Settings**](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security)
 page is where you can enable and manage SAML Single sign-on and/or two-factor authentication for an account.

### Single Sign-on authentication (SSO)[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso "Single Sign-on authentication (SSO) への直接リンク")

Manage your organization's access to Mapbox accounts while adding another level of security with SAML Single sign-on (SSO). SSO enables members of your organization to authenticate into a Mapbox account through any trusted, third-party identity provider that supports the [SAML2.0 protocol](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SAML_2.0)
.

#### SAML SSO capabilities[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#saml-sso-capabilities "SAML SSO capabilities への直接リンク")

**Supports:**

*   SAML2.0 protocol
*   Identity provider (IdP) initiated login
*   Shared accounts that multiple users can access
*   [User roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     of `Root` and `Admin`

**Does not support:**

*   JIT (Just in Time) provisioning
*   SCIM provisioning / de-provisioning
*   Domain control or domain lockout
*   Service provider (SP) initiated login
*   Identity provider (IdP) initiated single logout
*   OAuth, OpenID Connect, Kerberos, other protocols
*   Multiple identity providers for a single account
*   SAML authentication for [Atlas on-premises](https://www.mapbox.com/atlas)
    
*   Individual user accounts, a nested sub-account hierarchy, or separate, connected accounts

#### Setting up SAML SSO for your Mapbox account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#setting-up-saml-sso-for-your-mapbox-account "Setting up SAML SSO for your Mapbox account への直接リンク")

SAML Single sign-on for Mapbox can be configured with any identity provider that supports the SAML 2.0 protocol. Integrate the Mapbox SAML applications offered by [Okta](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#okta)
, [Azure AD](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#azure-active-directory)
, and [OneLogin](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#onelogin)
 for streamlined setup and IdP specific documentation, or [learn more about the general steps for setting up SSO](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#identity-providers-that-support-saml20)
 with any identity provider below.

##### Okta[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#okta "Okta への直接リンク")

*   Okta Integration Network (OIN): [Mapbox Okta verified SAML app](https://www.okta.com/integrations/mapbox/)
    
*   Tutorial: [How to configure SAML 2.0 with Mapbox](https://saml-doc.okta.com/SAML_Docs/How-to-Configure-SAML-2.0-for-Mapbox.html)
    

##### Azure Active Directory[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#azure-active-directory "Azure Active Directory への直接リンク")

*   Tutorial: [Azure Active Directory SSO integration with Mapbox](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/saas-apps/mapbox-tutorial)
    

##### OneLogin[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#onelogin "OneLogin への直接リンク")

*   OneLogin App Store: A Mapbox SAML app is available. Login to your [OneLogin portal](https://app.onelogin.com/login)
    , navigate to the Administration panel, click "Browse app catalog", and search for "Mapbox".

How to set up SAML 2.0 SSO Authentication for your Mapbox account

Learn how to enable SAML Single Sign-on (SSO) to connect Mapbox accounts to Okta and other enterprise-grade identity solutions.

#### Identity providers that support SAML2.0[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#identity-providers-that-support-saml20 "Identity providers that support SAML2.0 への直接リンク")

You can create a custom SAML app for Mapbox if there is not a Mapbox SAML application available in your IdP. The setup workflow and terminology used by each identity provider can be unique, but there are general themes:

1.  Login to the Mapbox account you want to set up with SSO authentication
2.  Navigate to the account's [SSO setup page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/sso)
    
3.  In your identity provider (IdP), create a new SAML application
4.  Copy and paste the required details in the **Configure your identity provider** section of the Mapbox [SSO setup page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/sso)
     into your IdP’s configuration workflow
5.  Create a custom attribute within your app that will pass the required [user roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     from your IdP to Mapbox in the SAML assertion.
6.  Copy and paste the required details from your SAML app within your IdP into the **Setup SAML single sign-on for Mapbox** section of the [SSO setup page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/sso)
    
7.  Click **Enable single sign-on** to save the integration
8.  [Validate the integration](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#validating-the-saml-sso-integration)
     is working as expected by assigning yourself one of the required [user roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     for the Mapbox SAML app. If required, assign yourself the application within your IdP.
9.  Assign users to the Mapbox application in your IdP, and assign a required Mapbox [user roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     to each user (or user groups)
10.  Follow the recommendations for [enforcing SAML authentication](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#enforcing-saml-sso-authentication)
    ,then flip the **Activate Mapbox SSO** toggle from `Off` to `On` to immediately terminate all sessions.

#### Configuring your identity provider[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#configuring-your-identity-provider "Configuring your identity provider への直接リンク")

Log into your identity provider with the required administrative privileges, then create a custom SAML2.0 application for Mapbox. See the following external links for IdP specific instructions:

*   [AWS SSO](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/singlesignon/latest/userguide/samlapps.html)
    
*   [GSuite](https://support.google.com/a/answer/6087519?hl=en)
    
*   [Auth0](https://auth0.com/docs/authenticate/single-sign-on/outbound-single-sign-on/configure-auth0-saml-identity-provider)
    

In this new application, enter the following values from the [Mapbox SSO setup page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/sso)
:

*   `Single sign-on URL`, also could be referred to as the `SSO URL`, `Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) URL`, `Application ACS URL`, `Reply URL`, `Callback URL`, or `Post-back URL` in your IdP.
*   `Audience Restriction`, also could be referred to as `Audience URI`, `SP Entity ID`, `Identifier`, or `Application SAML Audience` in your IdP.

**Configuration notes**

*   The application username must be in **email format**
*   The **SHA256** encryption algorithm is required
*   One of the supported [user roles](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider)
     must be present in the SAML assertion for a successful SAML login.

#### Configuring user roles in your identity provider[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#configuring-user-roles-in-your-identity-provider "Configuring user roles in your identity provider への直接リンク")

Through SAML SSO you can assign users roles that provide certain permissions in the Mapbox Account and Studio apps that are also enforced by all Mapbox APIs. **User roles are assigned in the identity provider and transferred to Mapbox in the SAML assertion.** The available user roles are:

| Role | Permissions | Typical users |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Root` | Users with the `Root` role can access invoices, read and write to account settings, and read and write to all resources and APIs. | IT Admins, Product Owners, CTOs |
| `Admin` | Users with the `Admin` role can read and write to all resources and APIs. They **cannot** access invoices, nor can they read or write to account settings. | Developers, Designers, Project contributors |

Many identity providers use **custom attributes** and **attribute statements** for roles. The attribute key name and role values are case-insensitive. Typically, roles can be assigned to individuals or groups. Consult the documentation for your specific IdP, such as the [Okta documentation for Mapbox SAML apps](https://saml-doc.okta.com/SAML_Docs/How-to-Configure-SAML-2.0-for-Mapbox.html)
 for details.

#### Enabling SAML SSO for the Mapbox account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#enabling-saml-sso-for-the-mapbox-account "Enabling SAML SSO for the Mapbox account への直接リンク")

To complete the initial connection between your identity provider and mapbox.com, enter the required information in the [Mapbox account's SSO setup page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/sso)
:

*   `Identity Provider sign-on URL`, also could be referred to as `SSO URL`, `SAML endpoint`, or `SSO sign-in URL` in your IdP
*   `Issuer ID`, also could be referred to as `Entity ID`, `Issuer`, or `Issuer URL` in your IdP
*   `X.509 Certificate`, pasted as text into the field.

Be sure to include `— BEGIN CERTIFICATE —` and `— END CERTIFICATE —` when pasting your X.509 certificate into the Mapbox form. You may need to open the X.509 certificate in a text editor to accurately copy the full body.

Click **Enable single sign-on** to submit the form. You will be prompted to re-authenticate.

#### Validating the SAML SSO integration[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#validating-the-saml-sso-integration "Validating the SAML SSO integration への直接リンク")

Once you have saved the integration, return to your identity provider to assign the application to yourself and/or another member of the organization for testing. When assigned, try clicking the tile, "chiclet", or link for your Mapbox app to login from your identity provider's portal. If you receive an error when attempting to login with SAML, you can adjust the settings by clicking the `edit single sign on`.

#### Enforcing SAML SSO authentication[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#enforcing-saml-sso-authentication "Enforcing SAML SSO authentication への直接リンク")

Enabling SSO for an account does not invalidate password authentication. The account's password is still a valid authentication method to make sure your organization's transition period to SAML authentication is seamless, and that direct password access to the account is available in the case of your identity provider has a service outage. Note that any users logging in with the account's password (and optional 2FA) will assume the `Root` user role.

To encourage your users transition from password based authentication to SAML login through your IdP, we recommend the following once the integration has been tested successfully:

*   Assign yourself the `Root` user role in your IdP so you will have access to settings
*   Assign most other users the `Admin` user role so they will not have access to settings
*   [Change the email](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#change-an-accounts-email-address)
     for the account to an email address that the `Root` user(s) can access
*   Save the new password in a safe location with least-privilege access (identity provider, shared password manager, IT vault)
*   If 2FA is enabled for the account, either turn it off or save the recovery codes with the new password
*   Announce to your organization that SAML SSO will be the primary mechanism for Mapbox access as of a future date. Encourage all users to test that they can access Mapbox with SAML before that date.
*   On the date of enforcement, toggle the `Activate single sign on` button on the account's [Security Settings page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security/)
     to **immediately terminate all the account's active sessions**.
*   [Change the password](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#change-your-password)
     for the account, making the previous password invalid. As with activating Mapbox SSO, when the account's password is changed, all active sessions will be terminated. We recommend taking these two steps at the same time to prevent confusion for your users.

Once SSO is activated and the password has been changed, these updates should effectively push all application users to authentication with Single sign-on, as they'll no longer have the password.

**Questions about setting up SAML SSO? Visit the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 for recommendations and next steps to contact support.**

#### Deleting SAML SSO integration[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#deleting-saml-sso-integration "Deleting SAML SSO integration への直接リンク")

You have the option to delete your SAML SSO integration. This is something you would need to do only in a rare situation -- generally, only if your organization has setup Single sign-on with a test account. Some identity providers can only be associated with a single Mapbox account. If you or your IT team has associated an IdP with a test account, we recommend deleting that integration before beginning setup for the organization's main account.

### Two-factor authentication[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#two-factor-authentication "Two-factor authentication への直接リンク")

**Two-factor authentication (2FA)**, also known as multi-factor authentication (MFA) or two-step authentication, provides an optional, but recommended, layer of security for Mapbox accounts. Once enabled, all users will be prompted to enter the account's password as well as a security code generated on registered mobile devices whenever they log in to the account.

#### Enable two-factor authentication[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#enable-two-factor-authentication "Enable two-factor authentication への直接リンク")

When logged in to an account, you can enable two-factor authentication from the [Security page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security)
. The Security page will include a barcode which you'll be prompted to scan with your mobile device, or a code that can by typed into password management services like 1Password.

Scan the generated barcode using an authenticator app on your mobile device. We recommend using Google Authenticator - it's free and available for [iOS](https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/google-authenticator/id388497605?mt=8)
 and [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.google.android.apps.authenticator2)
.

Your mobile device will display a 6-digit code. Type this code into the field below the barcode to complete the process.

#### Save your recovery code in a safe place so you can access the account if you lose your device[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#save-your-recovery-code-in-a-safe-place-so-you-can-access-the-account-if-you-lose-your-device "Save your recovery code in a safe place so you can access the account if you lose your device への直接リンク")

Note that enabling 2FA for an account raises the likelihood that you or other users of the account may have trouble logging into the account in the future. Mapbox verifies ownership of an account by asking users to confirm the credit card number on file for that account. When 2FA is enabled on an account without a credit card, there is no way that the Support team can help users regain access to that account. Exercise care by storing 2FA recovery codes in a safe place and distributing them to new account owners when transferring ownership of an account. Consider enabling [SAML SSO](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
 for team accounts or accounts with multiple users, since many SSO providers require 2FA for logging into the IdP.

#### Use a recovery code to access an account without the registered two-factor device[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#use-a-recovery-code-to-access-an-account-without-the-registered-two-factor-device "Use a recovery code to access an account without the registered two-factor device への直接リンク")

After you've set up two-factor authentication on an account, you will be redirected to a page with a **recovery code**. A recovery code is a single-use code that lets you sign in without your two-factor device.

**Write down this code and keep it in a safe place.** Treat your recovery code like a password to your account. If you lose your mobile device, you will need this code to log in to your account.

If you have already set up two-factor authentication on your account and do not have a recovery code, but can access the account with your 2FA device, go to your [Security page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/security)
 to generate and retrieve a new recovery code for the account. Immediately store this code is a safe place, like a 1Password vault for individuals or teams.

To use the recovery code to login to an account, you'll need the account's account ID (username) or email, and the account's password. Follow the steps below to use your recovery code:

1.  Navigate to the [Sign in page](https://account.mapbox.com/auth/signin/)
    .
2.  Enter the account ID (username) and password, then click **Sign in**.
3.  Click the **Lost your mobile device?** link below the **Sign in** button. A new field for the recovery code will appear.
4.  Enter your recovery code in the new field and click **Sign in**.

Using the account's recovery code will temporarily deactivate two-factor authentication. This gives you a chance to configure a new two-factor authentication device, enable [SAML SSO authentication](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
 for teams, or decide not to reactivate 2FA for the account.

**Questions about enabling MFA or accessing an account with a recovery code? Visit the [troubleshooting section](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#troubleshooting)
 for recommendations and next steps to contact support.**

Delete your account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#delete-your-account "Delete your account への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can delete an account by logging into your account, scrolling to the bottom of the [Account settings](https://console.mapbox.com/account/settings/)
, and clicking the **Delete account** button:

**Account deletion is a permanent action that cannot be undone.** We will not be able to recover your account, and the same account ID (username) cannot be re-used to create another account.

After clicking the button to delete an account, there will be a modal that requires additional confirmation that you do intend to fully delete the account. When an account is deleted, the following are effective immediately:

*   All styles, projects, and data associated with the account are no longer available.
*   The account cannot be logged into
*   Access tokens tied to that account will be immediately terminated, and map implementations using them will no longer be able to effectively access Mapbox services.

Mapbox will not automatically prorate for any unused services or subscriptions, [contact our team for help](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#if-i-cannot-resolve-my-issue-what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request)
.

Privacy[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#privacy "Privacy への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### GDPR compliance[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#gdpr-compliance "GDPR compliance への直接リンク")

Mapbox Services are fully compliant with GDPR. For more information, read our [DPA](https://www.mapbox.com/dpa/)
 and [subprocessors](https://www.mapbox.com/dpa/subprocessors/)
 pages.

Mapbox is also Privacy Shield certified and is listed at [privacyshield.org](https://www.privacyshield.gov/participant?id=a2zt0000000CbWQAA0&status=Active)
.

Troubleshooting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#troubleshooting "Troubleshooting への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Are you having trouble updating account settings, enabling SAML SSO, or logging into your account? Start here for some common troubleshooting tips for regaining account access or updating account settings. If you still cannot access your account after reviewing this documentation, include the details listed below in a [support request](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
. Our support team is standing by to help.

**I'm not receiving the email to verify my account's email address. What can I do?**

First, check that the email address registered to your account is a valid email address that has been spelled correctly, and that both the email address and email domain are configured to receive vendor email.

1.  **Invalid email addresses.** Emails cannot be delivered if the address contains a spelling error, the mailbox is full, the inbox does not exist, or one of many other reasons why an email address is unable to receive messages. If the address is invalid, follow the prompts on the [email confirmation page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/check-email/)
     to update spelling errors or change the account's registered email. This begins the verification process again with the new address.
    
2.  **Valid email addresses.** If you confirm that the email address is spelled properly and that the emails are not in your Spam folder or Promotions tab, _allow for up to 60 minutes of latency throughout your email system_. It's possible that your IT team maintains a filter above your inbox that requires additional processing and is delaying the email's arrival. Consider opening an internal ticket letting your IT team know about the problem, recommending they run a global search for emails from `no-reply@mapbox.com` or with the subject "verify your email address".
    

If the email address has not received an email after 60 minutes and is configured for receiving vendor email, [contact our team](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 so we troubleshoot with you based on the specific error codes being surfaced by your email provider.

**I'm not receiving the password reset email. What can I try next?**

First, check your spam folder to make sure our email hasn't been routed there. Not seeing it there, either? Sometimes password reset emails are undeliverable - maybe emails from Mapbox to your email address have bounced in the past or there have been other problems preventing the email from being delivered (especially for inactive email addresses).

If you are not receiving the password reset email, let us know [by submitting the support form](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

**I am locked out of a Mapbox account and need to regain access. What steps can I try?**

If you need to gain access to a Mapbox account, read the [account lockout troubleshooting guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/account-lockout/)
 which provides recommendations for common scenarios. Still have questions? Submit a [support request including these details](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#if-i-cannot-resolve-my-issue-what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request)
 to get in touch with our team.

**I do not have my two-factor (2FA) authentication recovery code. What are my options?**

If you do not have a 2FA recovery code for an account with 2FA enabled, the account must have a payment method on file for the Mapbox team to verify ownership of the account and remove two-factor authentication. [Contact our support team](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to get started.

_If an account does not have a credit card on file, we **will not** be able to verify account ownership._ In this case, the only next step will be to create a new Mapbox account and [add access tokens](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#creating-and-managing-access-tokens)
 from your new account into your map implementations.

**I'm having trouble setting up and/or configuring SAML Single Sign-on (SSO). Can you help?**

If you're having issues setting up SSO after following [the steps](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#single-sign-on-authentication-sso)
 in our documentation, [submit a support request](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
 to get in touch with our team. As we troubleshoot with you, the more information about error codes and screenshots from your identity provider that you can include with your support request, the better!

It's possible that your question will be specific enough to your identity provider (IdP) that our team is unable to replicate your issue. In those cases, we may recommend that you contact your IdP's support resources directly with your configuration question.

### If I cannot resolve my issue, what information should I include in my support request?[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/#if-i-cannot-resolve-my-issue-what-information-should-i-include-in-my-support-request "If I cannot resolve my issue, what information should I include in my support request? への直接リンク")

We're happy to help with your questions - [contact our support team with this form](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
. To help our team provide the most prompt resolution, include as much of the following information with your request:

**For account access issues:**

*   Account ID (username)
*   Account's registered email address
*   Access token (if applicable)

**For failed login issues [(with supported browsers)](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/mapbox-browser-support/#mapboxcom)
:**

*   Web browser(s) and versions
*   Any enabled web browser extensions you're using
*   Any enabled experimental flags (chrome://flags/) if using Google Chrome
*   Computer operating system and versions
*   Whether you have tested this issue while in incognito mode (if applicable)
*   Whether you have tested this issue with no browser extensions (if applicable)

If you do not know your browser information, you can go to the browser settings or the About page to find these details.

With the information you provide, we'll try to diagnose the issue and provide tips that help you produce the desired behavior. Thanks in advance for providing as much information as you can!

# ガイド | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

アカウントと価格設定
==========

*   check
    
    アクセストークンの作成と管理
    
*   check
    
    利用統計の表示
    
*   check
    
    請求書と支払い方法の管理
    
*   check
    
    アカウントプロファイルとセキュリティ設定の更新
    

Mapboxアカウントページをナビゲートし、使用量の計測方法や請求処理について理解しましょう。

アカウントページ[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/#account-page "アカウントページ への直接リンク")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

あなたの[アカウントページ](https://console.mapbox.com/)
には、Mapboxアカウントに関連するすべての情報や設定が含まれています。ここでは以下のようなことができます：

*   Mapboxのツール、モバイルSDKやAPIなどを使用するための**アクセストークン**を作成し管理する。詳細は[トークン管理](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/)
    を参照してください。
*   **統計データ**を表示し、アクセストークンを使用したアプリケーションのモバイルユーザー数、APIリクエスト数、地図ロード数などを追跡してどのように使用されているかを把握する。詳細は[統計と使用状況](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/)
    を参照してください。
*   従量課金の**請求書**を表示、印刷し、追加情報（例：住所）を追加する。詳細は[請求書と課金](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/invoices/)
    を参照してください。
*   プロフィール情報（組織名、メールアドレス、アバターなど）、パスワード、二要素認証、アカウント支払い情報など、一般的なアカウント**設定**を管理する。詳細は[設定とアカウント管理](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/settings/)
    を参照してください。

製品別価格設定[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/#pricing-by-product "製品別価格設定 への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

独自のワークフローやアプリケーションが、使用量や請求書の項目にどのように変換されるかについて詳しく知るには、[製品別価格設定](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/pricing/)
セクションを参照してください。

関連リソース[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/#related-resources "関連リソース への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [**用語集**](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/)
    でアカウントと課金に関連する用語を学びましょう。用語集には、地図のロード、月間アクティブユーザーなどの用語が含まれています。
*   アクセストークンに関する質問に答え、使用状況の解釈方法などについて学ぶためには、[**トラブルシューティングガイド**](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/#access-tokens)
    を参照してください。
*   Tokens APIのリファレンスドキュメントを[**APIドキュメント**](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/accounts/#tokens)
    で確認し、[レート制限](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/#rate-limits)
    や[サービスごとのエラーコード](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/accounts/#tokens-api-errors)
    に関する説明などを得ることができます。
*   アカウントのMapbox [利用規約](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos/)
    、[プライバシーポリシー](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/privacy)
    、[サービスレベル契約（SLA）](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/sla)
    を確認してください。
