# Token management | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Your **Tokens page** is where you can manage access tokens for your Mapbox account. To access the Tokens page, log into your account and navigate to the [Access Tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
 of your Developer Console.

Default public access token[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#default-public-access-token "Direct link to Default public access token")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your account will always contain a default public access token, referred to as your **default public token**. When you're logged into your account, this token will automatically be embedded in example code on Mapbox.com, like this:

Sample [Mapbox Geocoding API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/search/#geocoding)
 request:

    https://api.mapbox.com/geocoding/v5/mapbox.places/Los%20Angeles.json?access_token=YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN

Your default public access token contains all public scopes (read more about [public scopes](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#public-scopes)
 below).

If you **refresh** and delete this token, another one will be automatically generated. You cannot add secret scopes or URL restrictions to your default public access token

Creating and managing access tokens[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#creating-and-managing-access-tokens "Direct link to Creating and managing access tokens")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are two options for creating and managing Mapbox access tokens: use the [Tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
 to manage tokens using the account UI or use the [Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#tokens)
 to create, list, update, and delete tokens programmatically. You can create as many access tokens as you want. We recommend creating distinct tokens for specific environments, projects, and/or [client implementations](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/#setting-up-an-account-for-a-client)
 to track usage on the account's Statistics page.

### Mapbox Account Dashboard[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#mapbox-account-dashboard "Direct link to Mapbox Account Dashboard")

Access tokens can be created, deleted, and updated on your [Access Tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
. Deleting a token is an action that cannot be reversed - token deletion is permanent. Deleted tokens or the timestamp corresponding to their deletion are not available on the Access Tokens page.

To learn more about token basics, like creating a token, see our [Getting Started with Access Tokens guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/getting-started/access-tokens/)
.

### Mapbox Tokens API[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#mapbox-tokens-api "Direct link to Mapbox Tokens API")

With the [Mapbox Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#tokens)
 you can create, list, update, and delete your access tokens.

The Tokens API enables you to create public `pk` and secret `sk` tokens as well as temporary `tk` tokens, which can support an [expiration parameter](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#create-a-temporary-token)
 up to 1 hour in the future. Note that `tk` tokens will not be displayed on your [Access Tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
 in your account, and billable statistics generated by `tk` tokens will only be aggregated in the `All tokens` view on your [Statistics page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/statistics/)
.

Get started with the [Tokens API tutorial](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/tutorials/get-started-tokens-api/)
 or read more on our [API documentation page](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#tokens)
.

Rotating access tokens[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#rotating-access-tokens "Direct link to Rotating access tokens")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Any access token you include on a public webpage or in source code will be visible to anyone who makes an effort to look for it. Access tokens can be deleted and replaced in your code at any time if you suspect misuse. To rotate access tokens, [create a new token](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#creating-and-managing-access-tokens)
, replace it in your project's code, redeploy the application, and delete the older token. Invalidation for requests that have not been cached locally, in the browser, or in Mapbox infrastructure will happen immediately. The length of cached requests varies by service.

We recommend using distinct tokens for specific projects and [separate client implementations](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/#setting-up-an-account-for-a-client)
 to limit the number of projects you might need to update if a token has been compromised. [URL restrictions](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#url-restrictions)
 can add an additional layer of security to your tokens, but have certain limitations to be aware of while implementing.

Scopes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#scopes "Direct link to Scopes")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each access token you create will have a set of permissions that allow you or your applications to make certain types of requests to Mapbox APIs — these are called **scopes**. Access tokens can have either public or secret scopes. Our [API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/)
 lists the scopes required for each Mapbox API.

When choosing scopes, consider what you plan to do with the token. **To protect your account and your data, do not grant more scopes than necessary to each token**. For example, if you are creating a token to upload data to Mapbox with the [Mapbox Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#uploads)
, you will want to make sure you select the `uploads:write` and `uploads:read` scopes when creating a new `sk` token. To display a map in a web or mobile application, you should create a _separate_ access token that does not include the private uploads-related scopes, but does include the public `styles:read` and `fonts:read` scopes.

Read more about what each scope is for below and visit the [How to Use Mapbox Securely guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/how-to-use-mapbox-securely/#access-tokens)
 for recommendations.

### Public scopes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#public-scopes "Direct link to Public scopes")

| Scope | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `styles:tiles` | Read style as PNG tiles and static images. This is the scope that is necessary for retrieving a static map from a style with the [Static Images API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#static-images)<br> or for retrieving raster tiles from a style with the [Static Tiles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#static-tiles)<br>. |
| `styles:read` | Read styles. This is the scope that is necessary to initialize a Mapbox map style in Mapbox GL JS and our mobile Maps SDKs. |
| `fonts:read` | Read fonts. This is the scope that is necessary to generate fonts that render on your map styles and to retrieve font glyphs and ranges using the [Mapbox Fonts API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#fonts)<br>. |
| `datasets:read` | Read datasets. This scope is necessary for retrieving information about datasets using the [Datasets API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#datasets)<br>. |

### Secret scopes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#secret-scopes "Direct link to Secret scopes")

There are a few important things to note about secret scopes before creating a secret `sk` token:

1.  Secret scopes cannot be added to a public (`pk`) token. You must create a new secret (`sk`) token with secret scopes.
2.  If you choose to add any secret scopes to a new token, you will have only one chance to view the token. Follow the instructions to copy the token and save it in a secure location, like a password manager or secret key manager.
3.  **Do not expose access tokens with secret scopes.** Secret token API requests should never be exposed to the client. If someone else gets access to tokens with secret scopes they may be able to make changes to your account. Make all requests requiring a token with secret scopes on a server.

| Scope | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `scopes:list` | List all available scopes. This scope is necessary to list all potential scopes that an account has access to using the [Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#list-scopes)<br>. |
| `map:read` | Read tilesets, tilestats, and legacy projects. |
| `map:write` | Create and update tilesets and tilestats. |
| `user:read` | Read account profile information. Use this scope to list details about your account. |
| `user:write` | Write user profile information. Use this scope to update your account information. |
| `uploads:read` | Read data uploads. This scope is necessary for tracking your upload statuses using the [Mapbox Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#uploads)<br>. |
| `uploads:list` | List tileset uploads. This scope is necessary for retrieving multiple upload statuses using the [Mapbox Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#retrieve-upload-status)<br>. |
| `uploads:write` | Create tileset uploads. This scope is necessary for creating an upload using the [Mapbox Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#uploads)<br>. |
| `styles:write` | Create and update styles. This scope is necessary for creating a style in your account using the [Mapbox Styles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#create-a-style)<br>. |
| `styles:list` | List styles. This scope is necessary for listing styles for a specific account using the [Mapbox Styles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#list-styles)<br>. This endpoint returns style metadata instead of returning full styles. _You do not need a token with this scope to request Mapbox styles in your application. You should use separate, public tokens in your application._ |
| `styles:protect` | Protect write access to styles and their assets. This scope is necessary for protecting a style using the [Mapbox Styles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/styles/#protect-a-style)<br>. |
| `tokens:read` | Read tokens. This scope is necessary to list all the tokens that belong to an account using the [Mapbox Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#list-tokens)<br>. |
| `tokens:write` | Create, update, and delete tokens. This scope is necessary to use the [Mapbox Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#create-a-token)<br>. Every requested scope must be present in the access token used to allow the request. It is not possible to create a token with access to more scopes than the token that created it. |
| `datasets:list` | List datasets. This scope is necessary to list all the datasets that belong to a particular account using the [Datasets API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#list-datasets)<br>. |
| `datasets:write` | Create and update datasets using the [Datasets API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#datasets)<br>. |
| `tilesets:list` | List tilesets, tileset sources, and information about publishing jobs. This scope is necessary to use [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#list-tileset-sources)<br> and the [Tilesets CLI](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/tutorials/get-started-mts-and-tilesets-cli/)<br>. |
| `tilesets:read` | Read tilesets, tileset sources, tileset information about a publishing job, and view the global queue using [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/)<br>. This scope is necessary to use [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/)<br> and the [Tilesets CLI](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/tutorials/get-started-mts-and-tilesets-cli/)<br>. |
| `tilesets:write` | Create, update, publish, and delete tilesets using [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#create-a-tileset)<br>. Create, update, replace, and delete tileset sources using [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#create-a-tileset-source)<br>. Validate, update, and read tileset recipes using [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#validate-a-recipe)<br>. Update a tileset's information using [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#update-tileset-information)<br>. This scope is necessary to use [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/)<br> and the [Tilesets CLI](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/tutorials/get-started-mts-and-tilesets-cli/)<br>. |
| `downloads:read` | Download the Mapbox Maps and Navigation SDKs. This scope is necessary for downloading the [Maps SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/guides/install/)<br>, the [Maps SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/install/)<br>, the [Navigation SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/guides/install/)<br>, and the [Navigation SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/install/)<br>. _You do not need a token with this scope to use these SDKs. You should use separate, public tokens in your application._ |
| `atlas:read` | Read Mapbox Atlas data and software with the Atlas installer. This scope is necessary to set up a working instance of [Mapbox Atlas](https://www.mapbox.com/atlas/)<br>. This scope is only available to accounts that have access to Atlas. |

Questions about scopes? [Contact support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

URL restrictions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#url-restrictions "Direct link to URL restrictions")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can make your access tokens more secure by adding URL restrictions from the account dashboard [tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
 or with the [Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#tokens)
. When you add URL restrictions to a token, that token will only work for requests to billable Mapbox services that originate from the URLs you specify. Requests from unauthorized URLs will return status code `403: Forbidden`.

Tokens without restrictions will work for requests originating from any URL.

403 errors from Terminal or Postman

If you use an access token with URL restrictions to make a Mapbox API request in Terminal, Postman, or a similar tool, you will receive a `403` error since the request originated from an unauthorized URL. To run API requests in Terminal or Postman, use a token that does not have any URL restrictions.

### Requirements and limitations[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#requirements-and-limitations "Direct link to Requirements and limitations")

This feature is compatible with many Mapbox tools with some limitations.

**Supports:**

*   API requests that include a [referer header](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Referer)
    
*   Versions of Mapbox GL JS greater than or equal to [`0.53.1`](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#0531)
    
*   Up to 100 distinct URL restrictions per token
*   Billable services listed on the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing)
    

**Does not support:**

*   Default access tokens
*   Wildcard characters
*   IP addresses
*   Requests from websites with a `noreferrer` or `same-origin` [Referrer policy](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Referrer-Policy)
    
*   Requests from mobile applications built with the Mapbox Maps or Navigation SDKs
*   Requests from versions of Mapbox GL JS before [`0.53.1`](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#0531)
    
*   Requests from websites or browsers with privacy blocking extensions (including but not limited to [Brave Shields](https://support.brave.com/hc/en-us/articles/360022973471-What-is-Shields)
     and [Ghostery](https://www.ghostery.com/)
    )

Note that requests from `localhost` with a restricted token will be blocked unless `localhost` is specifically added to a token's allowed list. To develop locally, we recommend creating a separate token with more permissive URL restrictions.

URL restriction limitations

URL restrictions are a best-effort mitigation technique to prevent your token from being used in unauthorized applications. If you believe your token is being used without consent, [contact Mapbox](https://www.mapbox.com/support)
.

### Add URL restrictions to access tokens[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#add-url-restrictions-to-access-tokens "Direct link to Add URL restrictions to access tokens")

An **allowed URL** is an absolute or partial web address to which a token grants access. If no allowed URLs are provided, the token will work for requests originating from any URL. Depending on your use case, you might need to add `api.mapbox.com` to your access token's allowed list.

There are several URL components that can be combined to make an allowed URL entry.

**Example URL structure:**

`https://a-specific-subdomain.example.com:8000/all/paths?search=foo`

*   Protocol is https
*   Subdomain is a-specific-subdomain
*   Domain is example.com
*   Port is 8000
*   Path is /all/paths
*   Search with query string parameters is ?search=foo

**Valid URL formats**:

*   Domain only: `mapbox.com`
*   Domain with port: `mapbox.com:2019`
*   Protocol and domain: `http://mapbox.com`
*   Subdomain: `docs.mapbox.com`
*   Domain with path: `mapbox.com/help/getting-started/access-tokens`
*   Domain with query parameter: `mapbox.com/?page=1`

**Matching rules:**

*   Only a domain is required for the URL restriction
*   Protocol, subdomain, port, path, and search with query parameters are optional.
*   Paths are case sensitive.
*   If a port is not provided, ports 80 and 443 are allowed by default.

### Example scenarios[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#example-scenarios "Direct link to Example scenarios")

Subdomains of any allowed URLs are also allowed:

*   `http://example.com` also authorizes `http://www.example.com`
*   `http://example.com` also authorizes `http://www.production.example.com`
*   `http://example.com` also authorizes `http://www.subdomain2.production.example.com`

Subpaths of an allowed path are also allowed. Paths are case-sensitive:

*   `http://example.com` also authorizes `http://example.com/anything/else`
*   `http://example.com/path` also authorizes `http://example.com/path/more`
*   `http://example.com/path` does not authorize `http://example.com/another/path`
*   `http://example.com/path` does not authorize `http://example.com/Path`

If a protocol is specified it must be matched:

*   `http://example.com` does not authorize `https://example.com`

If no protocol is specified, then any protocol is acceptable:

*   example.com also authorizes `http://example.com` and `https://example.com`

### Troubleshooting URL restrictions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#troubleshooting-url-restrictions "Direct link to Troubleshooting URL restrictions")

**Why don't all versions of Mapbox GL JS support URL restrictions?**

Requests from versions of Mapbox GL JS less than [`0.53.1`](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#0531)
 can be denied because those requests may not include [referer headers](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Referer)
. If the account's Statistics page shows ["not set"](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/statistics/#not-set-and-other-values)
, upgrade GL JS versions before adding URL restrictions to an access token.

**What steps can I take if my URL restrictions aren't working, and I don't know why?**

There are a few troubleshooting steps you can take to try and resolve common issues:

1.  **Mitigate caching**. Try your request in an incognito browser or on another machine, as either your device and/or the Mapbox infrastructure may have cached the response to requests made before URL restrictions were added to the token. Note that not all Mapbox services cache requests for the same length of time.
2.  **Confirm requests include a referer header that matches your allowed URLs**. Open your browser's developer tools and `inspect` the requests in the network tab. Look to make sure the referer header of the live requests matches the URL that you have added to your tokens allowed list. If the request does not have a referer header (it's blank), requests from that URL with a restricted token will return `403: forbidden`. Review the Mapbox GL JS [Referrer policy recommendations](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/#referrer-policies)
    .
3.  **Check the website's Content Security Policy (CSP)**. Review the website's [Content Security Policy (CSP)](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CSP)
     and the Mapbox GL JS [CSP recommendations](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/#csp-directives)
    . A strict CSP could be stripping the referer headers from requests, which would result in a response of `403:forbidden`.
4.  **Verify allowed URLs don't contain unsupported characters**. Check the strings you have added to the token's allowed list for wildcard characters and/or IP addresses, which are not supported.
5.  **Add api.mapbox.com as an allowedURL**. Depending on the use case, you might need to add `api.mapbox.com` to your access token's allowed list. For example, if you are generating a URL with the Mapbox Studio [Share Button](https://docs.mapbox.com/studio-manual/guides/publish-your-style/#share-button)
     or using an iFrame that points to `api.mapbox.com`, with a restricted token, you should include `api.mapbox.com` on the token's allowed list.

**If I cannot resolve my issue, what information should I include in my support request?**

We're happy to [help with your questions](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
. We can provide the most prompt resolution when you include the following information with your request:

*   Account ID or registered email address
*   Token name or ID
*   Browser(s) and versions
*   Full failing API request (if possible)
*   Screenshot of a failing request, including the referer header of the request (with developer tools / inspect)
*   Description of expected behavior, actual behavior, and things you have tried to mediate the situation

We'll try to diagnose the issue and provide tips that help you produce the desired behavior.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# FAQs | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/faq/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

# Token management | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Your **Tokens page** is where you can manage access tokens for your Mapbox account. To access the Tokens page, log into your account and navigate to the [Access Tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
 of your Developer Console.

Default public access token[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#default-public-access-token "Default public access token への直接リンク")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your account will always contain a default public access token, referred to as your **default public token**. When you're logged into your account, this token will automatically be embedded in example code on Mapbox.com, like this:

Sample [Mapbox Geocoding API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/search/#geocoding)
 request:

    https://api.mapbox.com/geocoding/v5/mapbox.places/Los%20Angeles.json?access_token=YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN

Your default public access token contains all public scopes (read more about [public scopes](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#public-scopes)
 below).

If you **refresh** and delete this token, another one will be automatically generated. You cannot add secret scopes or URL restrictions to your default public access token

Creating and managing access tokens[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#creating-and-managing-access-tokens "Creating and managing access tokens への直接リンク")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are two options for creating and managing Mapbox access tokens: use the [Tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
 to manage tokens using the account UI or use the [Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/accounts/#tokens)
 to create, list, update, and delete tokens programmatically. You can create as many access tokens as you want. We recommend creating distinct tokens for specific environments, projects, and/or [client implementations](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/#setting-up-an-account-for-a-client)
 to track usage on the account's Statistics page.

### Mapbox Account Dashboard[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#mapbox-account-dashboard "Mapbox Account Dashboard への直接リンク")

Access tokens can be created, deleted, and updated on your [Access Tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
. Deleting a token is an action that cannot be reversed - token deletion is permanent. Deleted tokens or the timestamp corresponding to their deletion are not available on the Access Tokens page.

To learn more about token basics, like creating a token, see our [Getting Started with Access Tokens guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/getting-started/access-tokens/)
.

### Mapbox Tokens API[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#mapbox-tokens-api "Mapbox Tokens API への直接リンク")

With the [Mapbox Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/accounts/#tokens)
 you can create, list, update, and delete your access tokens.

The Tokens API enables you to create public `pk` and secret `sk` tokens as well as temporary `tk` tokens, which can support an [expiration parameter](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/accounts/#create-a-temporary-token)
 up to 1 hour in the future. Note that `tk` tokens will not be displayed on your [Access Tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
 in your account, and billable statistics generated by `tk` tokens will only be aggregated in the `All tokens` view on your [Statistics page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/statistics/)
.

Get started with the [Tokens API tutorial](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/tutorials/get-started-tokens-api/)
 or read more on our [API documentation page](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/accounts/#tokens)
.

Rotating access tokens[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#rotating-access-tokens "Rotating access tokens への直接リンク")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Any access token you include on a public webpage or in source code will be visible to anyone who makes an effort to look for it. Access tokens can be deleted and replaced in your code at any time if you suspect misuse. To rotate access tokens, [create a new token](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#creating-and-managing-access-tokens)
, replace it in your project's code, redeploy the application, and delete the older token. Invalidation for requests that have not been cached locally, in the browser, or in Mapbox infrastructure will happen immediately. The length of cached requests varies by service.

We recommend using distinct tokens for specific projects and [separate client implementations](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/collaboration-best-practices/#setting-up-an-account-for-a-client)
 to limit the number of projects you might need to update if a token has been compromised. [URL restrictions](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#url-restrictions)
 can add an additional layer of security to your tokens, but have certain limitations to be aware of while implementing.

Scopes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#scopes "Scopes への直接リンク")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each access token you create will have a set of permissions that allow you or your applications to make certain types of requests to Mapbox APIs — these are called **scopes**. Access tokens can have either public or secret scopes. Our [API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/)
 lists the scopes required for each Mapbox API.

When choosing scopes, consider what you plan to do with the token. **To protect your account and your data, do not grant more scopes than necessary to each token**. For example, if you are creating a token to upload data to Mapbox with the [Mapbox Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#uploads)
, you will want to make sure you select the `uploads:write` and `uploads:read` scopes when creating a new `sk` token. To display a map in a web or mobile application, you should create a _separate_ access token that does not include the private uploads-related scopes, but does include the public `styles:read` and `fonts:read` scopes.

Read more about what each scope is for below and visit the [How to Use Mapbox Securely guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/how-to-use-mapbox-securely/#access-tokens)
 for recommendations.

### Public scopes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#public-scopes "Public scopes への直接リンク")

| Scope | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `styles:tiles` | Read style as PNG tiles and static images. This is the scope that is necessary for retrieving a static map from a style with the [Static Images API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#static-images)<br> or for retrieving raster tiles from a style with the [Static Tiles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#static-tiles)<br>. |
| `styles:read` | Read styles. This is the scope that is necessary to initialize a Mapbox map style in Mapbox GL JS and our mobile Maps SDKs. |
| `fonts:read` | Read fonts. This is the scope that is necessary to generate fonts that render on your map styles and to retrieve font glyphs and ranges using the [Mapbox Fonts API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#fonts)<br>. |
| `datasets:read` | Read datasets. This scope is necessary for retrieving information about datasets using the [Datasets API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#datasets)<br>. |

### Secret scopes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#secret-scopes "Secret scopes への直接リンク")

There are a few important things to note about secret scopes before creating a secret `sk` token:

1.  Secret scopes cannot be added to a public (`pk`) token. You must create a new secret (`sk`) token with secret scopes.
2.  If you choose to add any secret scopes to a new token, you will have only one chance to view the token. Follow the instructions to copy the token and save it in a secure location, like a password manager or secret key manager.
3.  **Do not expose access tokens with secret scopes.** Secret token API requests should never be exposed to the client. If someone else gets access to tokens with secret scopes they may be able to make changes to your account. Make all requests requiring a token with secret scopes on a server.

| Scope | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `scopes:list` | List all available scopes. This scope is necessary to list all potential scopes that an account has access to using the [Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/accounts/#list-scopes)<br>. |
| `map:read` | Read tilesets, tilestats, and legacy projects. |
| `map:write` | Create and update tilesets and tilestats. |
| `user:read` | Read account profile information. Use this scope to list details about your account. |
| `user:write` | Write user profile information. Use this scope to update your account information. |
| `uploads:read` | Read data uploads. This scope is necessary for tracking your upload statuses using the [Mapbox Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#uploads)<br>. |
| `uploads:list` | List tileset uploads. This scope is necessary for retrieving multiple upload statuses using the [Mapbox Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#retrieve-upload-status)<br>. |
| `uploads:write` | Create tileset uploads. This scope is necessary for creating an upload using the [Mapbox Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#uploads)<br>. |
| `styles:write` | Create and update styles. This scope is necessary for creating a style in your account using the [Mapbox Styles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#create-a-style)<br>. |
| `styles:list` | List styles. This scope is necessary for listing styles for a specific account using the [Mapbox Styles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#list-styles)<br>. This endpoint returns style metadata instead of returning full styles. _You do not need a token with this scope to request Mapbox styles in your application. You should use separate, public tokens in your application._ |
| `styles:protect` | Protect write access to styles and their assets. This scope is necessary for protecting a style using the [Mapbox Styles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/styles/#protect-a-style)<br>. |
| `tokens:read` | Read tokens. This scope is necessary to list all the tokens that belong to an account using the [Mapbox Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/accounts/#list-tokens)<br>. |
| `tokens:write` | Create, update, and delete tokens. This scope is necessary to use the [Mapbox Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/accounts/#create-a-token)<br>. Every requested scope must be present in the access token used to allow the request. It is not possible to create a token with access to more scopes than the token that created it. |
| `datasets:list` | List datasets. This scope is necessary to list all the datasets that belong to a particular account using the [Datasets API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#list-datasets)<br>. |
| `datasets:write` | Create and update datasets using the [Datasets API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#datasets)<br>. |
| `tilesets:list` | List tilesets, tileset sources, and information about publishing jobs. This scope is necessary to use [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#list-tileset-sources)<br> and the [Tilesets CLI](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/tutorials/get-started-mts-and-tilesets-cli/)<br>. |
| `tilesets:read` | Read tilesets, tileset sources, tileset information about a publishing job, and view the global queue using [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/)<br>. This scope is necessary to use [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/)<br> and the [Tilesets CLI](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/tutorials/get-started-mts-and-tilesets-cli/)<br>. |
| `tilesets:write` | Create, update, publish, and delete tilesets using [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#create-a-tileset)<br>. Create, update, replace, and delete tileset sources using [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#create-a-tileset-source)<br>. Validate, update, and read tileset recipes using [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#validate-a-recipe)<br>. Update a tileset's information using [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#update-tileset-information)<br>. This scope is necessary to use [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/)<br> and the [Tilesets CLI](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/tutorials/get-started-mts-and-tilesets-cli/)<br>. |
| `downloads:read` | Download the Mapbox Maps and Navigation SDKs. This scope is necessary for downloading the [Maps SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/)<br>, the [Maps SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/maps/guides/install/)<br>, the [Navigation SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/guides/install/)<br>, and the [Navigation SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/navigation/guides/install/)<br>. _You do not need a token with this scope to use these SDKs. You should use separate, public tokens in your application._ |
| `atlas:read` | Read Mapbox Atlas data and software with the Atlas installer. This scope is necessary to set up a working instance of [Mapbox Atlas](https://www.mapbox.com/atlas/)<br>. This scope is only available to accounts that have access to Atlas. |

Questions about scopes? [Contact support](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
.

URL restrictions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#url-restrictions "URL restrictions への直接リンク")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can make your access tokens more secure by adding URL restrictions from the account dashboard [tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
 or with the [Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/accounts/#tokens)
. When you add URL restrictions to a token, that token will only work for requests to billable Mapbox services that originate from the URLs you specify. Requests from unauthorized URLs will return status code `403: Forbidden`.

Tokens without restrictions will work for requests originating from any URL.

403 errors from Terminal or Postman

If you use an access token with URL restrictions to make a Mapbox API request in Terminal, Postman, or a similar tool, you will receive a `403` error since the request originated from an unauthorized URL. To run API requests in Terminal or Postman, use a token that does not have any URL restrictions.

### Requirements and limitations[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#requirements-and-limitations "Requirements and limitations への直接リンク")

This feature is compatible with many Mapbox tools with some limitations.

**Supports:**

*   API requests that include a [referer header](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Referer)
    
*   Versions of Mapbox GL JS greater than or equal to [`0.53.1`](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#0531)
    
*   Up to 100 distinct URL restrictions per token
*   Billable services listed on the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing)
    

**Does not support:**

*   Default access tokens
*   Wildcard characters
*   IP addresses
*   Requests from websites with a `noreferrer` or `same-origin` [Referrer policy](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Referrer-Policy)
    
*   Requests from mobile applications built with the Mapbox Maps or Navigation SDKs
*   Requests from versions of Mapbox GL JS before [`0.53.1`](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#0531)
    
*   Requests from websites or browsers with privacy blocking extensions (including but not limited to [Brave Shields](https://support.brave.com/hc/en-us/articles/360022973471-What-is-Shields)
     and [Ghostery](https://www.ghostery.com/)
    )

Note that requests from `localhost` with a restricted token will be blocked unless `localhost` is specifically added to a token's allowed list. To develop locally, we recommend creating a separate token with more permissive URL restrictions.

URL restriction limitations

URL restrictions are a best-effort mitigation technique to prevent your token from being used in unauthorized applications. If you believe your token is being used without consent, [contact Mapbox](https://www.mapbox.com/support)
.

### Add URL restrictions to access tokens[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#add-url-restrictions-to-access-tokens "Add URL restrictions to access tokens への直接リンク")

An **allowed URL** is an absolute or partial web address to which a token grants access. If no allowed URLs are provided, the token will work for requests originating from any URL. Depending on your use case, you might need to add `api.mapbox.com` to your access token's allowed list.

There are several URL components that can be combined to make an allowed URL entry.

**Example URL structure:**

`https://a-specific-subdomain.example.com:8000/all/paths?search=foo`

*   Protocol is https
*   Subdomain is a-specific-subdomain
*   Domain is example.com
*   Port is 8000
*   Path is /all/paths
*   Search with query string parameters is ?search=foo

**Valid URL formats**:

*   Domain only: `mapbox.com`
*   Domain with port: `mapbox.com:2019`
*   Protocol and domain: `http://mapbox.com`
*   Subdomain: `docs.mapbox.com`
*   Domain with path: `mapbox.com/help/getting-started/access-tokens`
*   Domain with query parameter: `mapbox.com/?page=1`

**Matching rules:**

*   Only a domain is required for the URL restriction
*   Protocol, subdomain, port, path, and search with query parameters are optional.
*   Paths are case sensitive.
*   If a port is not provided, ports 80 and 443 are allowed by default.

### Example scenarios[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#example-scenarios "Example scenarios への直接リンク")

Subdomains of any allowed URLs are also allowed:

*   `http://example.com` also authorizes `http://www.example.com`
*   `http://example.com` also authorizes `http://www.production.example.com`
*   `http://example.com` also authorizes `http://www.subdomain2.production.example.com`

Subpaths of an allowed path are also allowed. Paths are case-sensitive:

*   `http://example.com` also authorizes `http://example.com/anything/else`
*   `http://example.com/path` also authorizes `http://example.com/path/more`
*   `http://example.com/path` does not authorize `http://example.com/another/path`
*   `http://example.com/path` does not authorize `http://example.com/Path`

If a protocol is specified it must be matched:

*   `http://example.com` does not authorize `https://example.com`

If no protocol is specified, then any protocol is acceptable:

*   example.com also authorizes `http://example.com` and `https://example.com`

### Troubleshooting URL restrictions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#troubleshooting-url-restrictions "Troubleshooting URL restrictions への直接リンク")

**Why don't all versions of Mapbox GL JS support URL restrictions?**

Requests from versions of Mapbox GL JS less than [`0.53.1`](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#0531)
 can be denied because those requests may not include [referer headers](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Referer)
. If the account's Statistics page shows ["not set"](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/#not-set-and-other-values)
, upgrade GL JS versions before adding URL restrictions to an access token.

**What steps can I take if my URL restrictions aren't working, and I don't know why?**

There are a few troubleshooting steps you can take to try and resolve common issues:

1.  **Mitigate caching**. Try your request in an incognito browser or on another machine, as either your device and/or the Mapbox infrastructure may have cached the response to requests made before URL restrictions were added to the token. Note that not all Mapbox services cache requests for the same length of time.
2.  **Confirm requests include a referer header that matches your allowed URLs**. Open your browser's developer tools and `inspect` the requests in the network tab. Look to make sure the referer header of the live requests matches the URL that you have added to your tokens allowed list. If the request does not have a referer header (it's blank), requests from that URL with a restricted token will return `403: forbidden`. Review the Mapbox GL JS [Referrer policy recommendations](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/api/#referrer-policies)
    .
3.  **Check the website's Content Security Policy (CSP)**. Review the website's [Content Security Policy (CSP)](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CSP)
     and the Mapbox GL JS [CSP recommendations](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/api/#csp-directives)
    . A strict CSP could be stripping the referer headers from requests, which would result in a response of `403:forbidden`.
4.  **Verify allowed URLs don't contain unsupported characters**. Check the strings you have added to the token's allowed list for wildcard characters and/or IP addresses, which are not supported.
5.  **Add api.mapbox.com as an allowedURL**. Depending on the use case, you might need to add `api.mapbox.com` to your access token's allowed list. For example, if you are generating a URL with the Mapbox Studio [Share Button](https://docs.mapbox.com/studio-manual/ja/guides/publish-your-style/#share-button)
     or using an iFrame that points to `api.mapbox.com`, with a restricted token, you should include `api.mapbox.com` on the token's allowed list.

**If I cannot resolve my issue, what information should I include in my support request?**

We're happy to [help with your questions](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=360000279191)
. We can provide the most prompt resolution when you include the following information with your request:

*   Account ID or registered email address
*   Token name or ID
*   Browser(s) and versions
*   Full failing API request (if possible)
*   Screenshot of a failing request, including the referer header of the request (with developer tools / inspect)
*   Description of expected behavior, actual behavior, and things you have tried to mediate the situation

We'll try to diagnose the issue and provide tips that help you produce the desired behavior.

# Pricing by products | Accounts and pricing | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This guide will walk you through which line items match up to some typical applications of:

*   [Web maps using older library versions](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#web-maps-using-older-library-versions)
    
*   [Mobile applications using older SDK versions](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#mobile-applications-using-older-sdk-versions)
    
*   [Web services APIs](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#web-services-apis)
    
*   [Hybrid frameworks](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#hybrid-frameworks)
    
*   [Third-party tools](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#third-party-tools)
    

Not included in this guide

This page does not include the number of units included in the free tier, the exact cost per billable unit, or volume-based discounts. For this information, see the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/)
.

Web maps using older library versions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#web-maps-using-older-library-versions "Direct link to Web maps using older library versions")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Pricing for web map usage is based on API requests (i.e., **tile requests**), sessions (i.e., **map loads**) or monthly users (i.e., [**seats**](https://docs.mapbox.com/gl-js-seats/)
). The pricing method that applies to your application depends on which product you are using, and in some cases, which _version_ of that product. These are the libraries, tools, and versions that are included in this section:

*   [Mapbox GL JS < v1.0.0](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#mapbox-gl-js--v100)
    
*   [Leaflet](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#leaflet)
    

For pricing information relating to Mapbox GL JS v1.0.0 and higher, see the [current product pricing page](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/pricing/)
.

### Mapbox GL JS < v1.0.0[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#mapbox-gl-js--v100 "Direct link to Mapbox GL JS < v1.0.0")

Overview

*   Billed by **tile requests**
*   Upgrade to new pricing **by updating your Mapbox GL JS version**
*   See rates and discounts per Vector Tiles API request or Raster Tiles API request in the pricing page's **[Maps](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#maps)
    ** section

Web maps using any version of Mapbox GL JS before v1.0.0 are billed by **tile requests**. There are two types of tile requests made from Mapbox GL JS:

*   **Vector Tiles API requests** for any tiles from vector tilesets including Mapbox-maintained tilesets like [Mapbox Streets](https://docs.mapbox.com/vector-tiles/reference/mapbox-streets-v8/)
     or any custom vector tileset uploaded to your Mapbox account.
*   **Raster Tiles API requests** for any tiles from raster tilesets including Mapbox-maintained tilesets like [Mapbox Satellite](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/mapbox-satellite/)
     or any custom raster tileset uploaded to your Mapbox account.

There are a few key differences between this tile-based pricing and the map load pricing used by higher versions of Mapbox GL JS, including:

*   **Map interaction**: The more the user interacts with the map, the more tiles are requested. Interact with the map in the demo below to see the relationship between user interaction and the number of tiles requested. (In map load based pricing, it counts as one map load regardless of how much the user interacts with the map.)
*   **Composite sources vs. non-composite sources**: You can combine any Mapbox style with a Mapbox-maintained tileset, like [Terrain v2](https://docs.mapbox.com/vector-tiles/reference/mapbox-terrain-v2/)
     or [Traffic v1](https://docs.mapbox.com/vector-tiles/reference/mapbox-traffic-v1/)
    , or tilesets that you upload to Mapbox Studio. Sources added on the client _will not be composited_ so every tile from each vector source will be requested separately, counting toward total tile requests. You can composite vector tile sources in Mapbox Studio to reduce the number of tiles that are requested.
*   **Vector tiles vs. raster tiles**: As described above, vector tiles and raster tiles are counted and billed separately.

circlecirclecircle

arrow-leftarrow-rightrotate

heartmapboxmenu

### Leaflet[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#leaflet "Direct link to Leaflet")

Overview

*   Billed by **tile requests**
*   See rates and discounts per Static Tiles API request or Raster Tiles API request in the pricing page's **[Maps](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#maps)
    ** section

If you are using Leaflet with Mapbox-hosted map tiles, usage is determined by **tile requests**. Leaflet applications display raster tiles, but there are two different kinds of tile requests you may be making: Static Tiles API requests or Raster Tiles API requests.

#### Raster tiles from Mapbox GL styles[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#raster-tiles-from-mapbox-gl-styles "Direct link to Raster tiles from Mapbox GL styles")

If you are using a Mapbox GL map style (using a [style URL](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/style-url/)
) via [`L.tileLayer`](https://leafletjs.com/reference.html#tilelayer)
, you are requesting map tiles from the [**Static Tiles API**](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#static-tiles)
, which takes GL-based styles and renders raster tiles.

The example below uses a GL-based style, `mapbox://styles/mapbox/streets-v12`, with Leaflet. Each tile has a red border to help you see how many are required to display the map you see on the screen.

    var map = L.map('map')        .setView([38.8929, -77.0252], 14)        .addLayer(          L.tileLayer(            'https://api.mapbox.com/styles/v1/mapbox/streets-v12/tiles/256/{z}/{x}/{y}?access_token=YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN',            {              maxZoom: 19,              attribution:                '&copy; <a href="http://www.openstreetmap.org/copyright">OpenStreetMap</a> © <a href="https://www.mapbox.com/about/maps/">Mapbox</a> <strong><a href="https://labs.mapbox.com/contribute/" target="_blank">Improve this map</a></strong>'            }          )        );

circlecirclecircle

arrow-leftarrow-rightrotate

heartmapboxmenu

#### Raster tiles from raster tilesets[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#raster-tiles-from-raster-tilesets "Direct link to Raster tiles from raster tilesets")

If you are setting the map style via a raster tileset's [tileset ID](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/tileset-id/)
, you are requesting map tiles from the [**Raster Tiles API**](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#raster-tiles)
. Raster tiles can be satellite imagery or another raster data source.

The example below uses the raster tileset, `mapbox.satellite`, with Leaflet. Each tile has a red border to help you see how many are required to display the map you see on the screen.

    var map = L.map('map').setView([51.505, -0.09], 13);L.tileLayer('https://api.mapbox.com/styles/v1/mapbox/satellite-streets-v12/tiles/256/{z}/{x}/{y}?access_token=YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN',    {      maxZoom: 19,      attribution:        '&copy; <a href="http://www.openstreetmap.org/copyright">OpenStreetMap</a> © <a href="https://www.mapbox.com/about/maps/">Mapbox</a> <strong><a href="https://labs.mapbox.com/contribute/" target="_blank">Improve this map</a></strong>'    }  ).addTo(map);

circlecirclecircle

arrow-leftarrow-rightrotate

heartmapboxmenu

Mobile applications using older SDK versions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#mobile-applications-using-older-sdk-versions "Direct link to Mobile applications using older SDK versions")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Pricing for mobile applications is either based on **monthly active users (MAUs)** or **tile requests**. The pricing method that applies to your application depends on which product you are using, and in some cases, the version of that product.

**[MAUs](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/monthly-active-users/)
** are users that accessed Mapbox services within your applications during a given month. The month-long period aligns with your [billing period](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/invoices/#billing-period)
, which is likely _not_ aligned with calendar months.

This guide does not include the number of units included in the free tier, the exact cost per billable unit, or volume-based discounts for each service. For this information, [see the pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#session-user-pricing)
.

These are the SDKs and versions that are included in this section:

*   [Maps SDK for iOS and Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#maps-sdk-for-ios-and-android)
    
*   [Navigation SDK for iOS and Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#navigation-sdk-for-ios-and-android)
    
*   [Maps SDK for Unity](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#maps-sdk-for-unity)
    

### Maps SDK for iOS and Android[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#maps-sdk-for-ios-and-android "Direct link to Maps SDK for iOS and Android")

Applications built with the Mapbox Maps SDK are billed based on [**monthly active users**](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/monthly-active-users/)
 (MAUs). The MAU line item shown in your invoice will vary based on the version of the Maps SDK you are using. This guide discusses pricing for older versions of the Maps SDK (less than v5.0.0 for iOS or less than v8.0.0 for Android), which are billed by \_Legacy SDKs for mobile MAUs\*.

For pricing information about more recent versions of the Maps SDK, which are billed by _Maps SDKs for mobile MAUs_, see the product documentation for the [Maps SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/guides/pricing/)
 (v5.0.0 and higher) and the [Maps SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/pricing/)
 (v8.0.0 and higher).

Overview

*   Billed by **Legacy SDKs for mobile MAUs**
*   Upgrade to new pricing **by updating your Maps SDK version**
*   See rates and discounts per Maps MAUs in the pricing page's **[Session & user pricing](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#mapsmaus)
    ** section

The free tier and cost per _Legacy SDKs for mobile MAU_ is equal to the free tier and cost for _Maps SDK for mobile MAUs_. To see the number of MAUs included in the free tier and the cost per MAU beyond the free tier, see the [Maps SDK for mobile section of our pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#mapsmaus)
.

### Navigation SDK for iOS and Android[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#navigation-sdk-for-ios-and-android "Direct link to Navigation SDK for iOS and Android")

Applications built with the Mapbox Navigation SDK are billed based on [**monthly active users**](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/monthly-active-users/)
 (MAUs). The MAU line items shown in your invoices will vary based on the version of the Navigation SDK you are using. This guide discusses pricing for older versions of the Navigation SDK, which are billed either by _Maps SDKs for mobile MAUs_ and _Directions API requests_, or _Legacy SDKs for mobile MAUs_, _Directions API requests_, and _Vector Tiles API requests_.

For pricing information about more recent versions of the Navigation SDK, which are billed by _Navigation SDK MAUs_, see the product documentation for the [Navigation SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/guides/pricing/)
 (v1.0.0 and higher) and the [Navigation SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/guides/pricing/)
 (v1.0.0).

#### Maps SDKs for mobile MAUs + Directions API requests[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#maps-sdks-for-mobile-maus--directions-api-requests "Direct link to Maps SDKs for mobile MAUs + Directions API requests")

Overview

*   Billed by **Maps SDKs for mobile MAUs + Directions API requests**
*   Upgrade to new pricing **by updating your Navigation SDK version**

This line item applies to the following versions of the Navigation SDK:

*   **Android: 0.41.0 to 0.42.x**
*   **iOS: 0.36.0 to 0.40.x**

Applications built with these versions are billed based on a combination of [**monthly active users**](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/monthly-active-users/)
 (specifically "Maps SDKs for mobile" MAUs) and **Directions API requests**. Maps SDKs for mobile MAUs include Vector Tiles API and Raster Tiles API requests. Directions API requests will appear as a separate line item in your monthly invoice.

To see the number of MAUs and requests included in the free tier and the cost per MAU or request beyond the free tier, see:

*   [Maps SDKs for mobile section of our pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#mapsmaus)
    
*   [Directions API section of our pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#directions)
    

#### Legacy SDKs for mobile MAUs + Directions API requests + Vector Tiles API requests[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#legacy-sdks-for-mobile-maus--directions-api-requests--vector-tiles-api-requests "Direct link to Legacy SDKs for mobile MAUs + Directions API requests + Vector Tiles API requests")

Overview

*   Billed by **Legacy SDK MAUs + Directions API requests + Vector Tiles API requests**
*   Upgrade to new pricing **by updating your Navigation SDK version**

This line item applies to the following versions of the Navigation SDK:

*   **Android: less than 0.41.0**
*   **iOS: less than 0.36.0**

Applications built with these versions are billed based on a combination of [**monthly active users**](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/monthly-active-users/)
 (specifically "Legacy SDKs for mobile" MAUs), **Directions API requests**, and **Vector Tile API requests**. Legacy versions of the Navigation SDKs billed by Legacy SDKs for mobile MAUs do not include Vector Tiles API, Raster Tiles API requests, and Directions API requests - those will appear as separate line items in your monthly invoice.

Cost per Legacy SDKs for mobile MAU

The free tier and cost per _Legacy SDKs for mobile MAU_ is equal to the free tier and cost for **Maps SDK for mobile MAUs** as listed on our [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#session-user-pricing)
.

To see the number of MAUs and requests included in the free tier and the cost per MAU or request beyond the free tier, see:

*   [Maps SDKs for mobile section of our pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#mapsmaus)
     (equivalent to Legacy SDK for mobile rates and tiers)
*   [Directions API section of our pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#directions)
    
*   [Vector Tiles API section of our pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#vectortile)
    

### Maps SDK for Unity[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#maps-sdk-for-unity "Direct link to Maps SDK for Unity")

Overview

*   Billed by **Maps SDK for Unity MAUs or Legacy SDKs for mobile MAUs**
*   Upgrade to new pricing **by updating your Maps SDK for Unity version**
*   See rates and discounts per Maps SDK for Unity MAUs in the pricing page's **[Maps SDK for Unity](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#unitymaus)
    ** section

Applications built with the [Mapbox Maps SDK for Unity](https://docs.mapbox.com/unity/maps/guides/)
 are billed based on [**monthly active users**](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/monthly-active-users/)
. For more details, see the [Maps SDK for Unity pricing documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/unity/maps/guides/pricing/)
.

Web services APIs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#web-services-apis "Direct link to Web services APIs")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Maps-related APIs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#maps-related-apis "Direct link to Maps-related APIs")

Your use of Maps-related APIs is measured in **tile requests**, **processing and hosting events**, or **API requests**.

#### Tile requests[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#tile-requests "Direct link to Tile requests")

Use of the Vector Tiles API and the Raster Tiles API is measured in **tile requests**. For more information, see the relevant API documentation:

*   [Vector Tiles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/vector-tiles/#vector-tiles-api-pricing)
    
*   [Raster Tiles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/raster-tiles/#raster-tiles-api-pricing)
    

The Static Tiles API may be billed by tile requests if it is used with _[Leaflet](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#leaflet)
, a [third-party tool such as ArcGIS or Carto](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#third-party-tools)
, or a [hybrid framework](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#hybrid-frameworks)
_. Otherwise, use of the Static Tiles API is billed by [API requests](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#api-requests)
 rather than tile requests.

#### Processing and hosting events[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#processing-and-hosting-events "Direct link to Processing and hosting events")

Use of Mapbox Tiling Service (MTS) and the Uploads API is measured in **processing and hosting events**. For more information, see the relevant documentation:

*   [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/guides/pricing/)
    
*   [Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/uploads/#pricing)
    

#### API requests[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#api-requests "Direct link to API requests")

Use of the Static Images API, the Static Tiles API, and the Tilequery API is measured in **API requests**. For more information, see the relevant API documentation:

*   [Static Images API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/static-images/#static-images-api-pricing)
    
*   [Static Tiles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/static-tiles/#static-tiles-api-pricing)
    
*   [Tilequery API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/tilequery/#tilequery-api-pricing)
    

#### Mapbox Studio[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#mapbox-studio "Direct link to Mapbox Studio")

##### Prints[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#prints "Direct link to Prints")

For pricing information about prints created using the Mapbox Studio print panel, see the [Studio Manual](https://docs.mapbox.com/studio-manual/reference/styles/#print-panel)
.

##### Tilesets uploaded in Studio[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#tilesets-uploaded-in-studio "Direct link to Tilesets uploaded in Studio")

While Mapbox Studio is powered by the Uploads API to create tilesets, it is subject to usage restrictions and alternative pricing. See the [Studio Manual](https://docs.mapbox.com/studio-manual/reference/tilesets/#pricing)
 for pricing details.

### Search-related APIs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#search-related-apis "Direct link to Search-related APIs")

#### Geocoding API[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#geocoding-api "Direct link to Geocoding API")

For pricing information for the Geocoding API, which is measured in **API requests**, see the [Geocoding API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/search/geocoding/#geocoding-api-pricing)
.

### Navigation-related APIs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#navigation-related-apis "Direct link to Navigation-related APIs")

For pricing information for the Navigation-related APIs measured in **API requests**, see the relevant API documentation:

*   [Directions API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/directions/#directions-api-pricing)
    
*   [Isochrone API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/isochrone/#isochrone-api-pricing)
    
*   [Map Matching API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/map-matching/#map-matching-api-pricing)
    
*   [Optimization API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/optimization/#optimization-api-pricing)
    

For pricing information for the Matrix API, which is measured in [**elements**](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/matrix-api-elements/)
, see the [Matrix API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/matrix/#matrix-api-pricing)
.

Hybrid frameworks[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#hybrid-frameworks "Direct link to Hybrid frameworks")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Overview

*   Billed by **Legacy SDK MAUs or tile requests**
*   See rates and discounts per Vector Tiles API request, Raster Tiles API request, or Static Tiles API request in the pricing page's **[Maps](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#maps)
    ** section

Third-party frameworks built from the native Mapbox Mobile SDKs are typically billed based on **monthly active users**. Apps built with other third-party frameworks are billed based on **tile requests**. Below are some examples of popular SDKs and frameworks and whether Mapbox tracks usage through mobile users or tile requests.

| **SDK or framework** | **Legacy SDK MAUs** | **Tile requests** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Community-managed Maps SDK for React Native](https://github.com/nitaliano/react-native-mapbox-gl) | check |     |
| Cordova + native SDK | check |     |
| Xamarin + native SDK | check |     |
| Phonegap + Mapbox GL JS |     | check |
| Cordova + Mapbox GL JS |     | check |
| Mapbox GL JS-based hybrid SDKs |     | check |

Cost per Legacy SDKs for mobile MAU

For all products listed above, the free tier and cost per _Legacy SDKs for mobile MAU_ is equal to the free tier and cost for **Maps SDK for mobile MAUs** as listed on our [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#session-user-pricing)
.

Third-party tools[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/pricing/#third-party-tools "Direct link to Third-party tools")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Overview

*   Billed by **tile requests or Map Loads for Web**
*   See rates and discounts per Vector Tiles API request, Static Tiles API request, or Raster Tiles API request in the pricing page's **[Maps](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#maps)
    ** section

Web maps that rely on Mapbox-hosted tiles, but are not served via Mapbox GL JS, are billed by **tile requests**. This includes vector tiles from vector tilesets, GL-based styles rendered as raster tiles, and raster tiles from raster tilesets. The table below includes some popular third-party integrations. _Not all possible third-party tools are included in this list._

| Products | What will appear on your invoice |
| --- | --- |
| FramerX | [Vector Tiles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/vector-tiles/#vector-tiles-api-pricing) |
| PowerBI | [Map Loads for Web](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/map-loads/) |
| Carto with a [style URL](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/style-url/) | Static Tiles API |
| ArcGIS with a [style URL](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/style-url/) | Static Tiles API |
| QGIS with a [style URL](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/style-url/) | Static Tiles API |
