# tileset | Help | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/tileset/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

A **tileset** is a collection of raster or vector data broken up into a uniform grid of square tiles at 22 preset [zoom levels](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/zoom-level/)
. In Mapbox libraries and SDKs, tilesets are necessary to make maps visible on mobile devices or in the browser. They are cacheable and load quickly, and Mapbox relies heavily on tilesets to keep our maps performant.

Edit tilesets with Mapbox Tiling Service (MTS)

Once a tileset has been created, the geometry and data properties cannot be edited in Mapbox Studio. Only style properties can be applied to change its appearance. Both the Mapbox Tiling Service (MTS) and the Uploads API allow you to update an existing tileset. To learn more, read the MTS documentation on [Updating a tileset](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/overview/#update-a-tileset-with-mts)
 or the Uploads API documentation on [Creating an upload](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/uploads/#create-an-upload)
 using the tileset ID of an existing tileset to replace its contents.

Tilesets can be **raster** or **vector**. The differences are outlined below.

Raster tilesets[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/tileset/#raster-tilesets "Raster tilesets への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Uploading raster images ([TIFF](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/tiff/)
 or GeoTIFF files) to your Mapbox account results in raster tilesets. **Raster tilesets** are what Mapbox tools use to load a grid of images to display on a map.

You can use raster tilesets in [Mapbox Studio](https://docs.mapbox.com/studio-manual/ja/)
, [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/)
, and the Mapbox Maps SDKs for [Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/maps/)
 and [iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/)
. Web mapping libraries like [Leaflet](https://leafletjs.com/)
 also use raster tiles to display maps on a webpage.

Vector tilesets[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/tileset/#vector-tilesets "Vector tilesets への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Uploading vector data ([GeoJSON](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/geojson/)
, [Shapefile](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/shapefile/)
, [KML](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/kml/)
, or [GPX](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/gpx/)
 files) to your Mapbox account results in vector tilesets. **Vector tilesets** are the vector data equivalent of raster tilesets. Instead of storing raster data in the form of pixels, vector tilesets store vector data in the form of points, lines, and polygons as [vector tiles](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/vector-tiles/)
.

You can use vector tiles as [sources](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/source/)
 for map [styles](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/style/)
. You can also manipulate or query vector tiles directly to create interactive experiences with [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/ja/)
 and the Mapbox Maps SDKs for [Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/maps/)
 and [iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/)
.

* * *

**Related resources:**

*   Mapbox Studio manual: [Tilesets](https://docs.mapbox.com/studio-manual/ja/reference/tilesets/)
    
*   Getting started: [Our map data](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/dive-deeper/mapbox-data/)
    
*   [Mapbox Tiling Service API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/)
    
*   [Uploads API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/uploads/)
    
*   Troubleshooting guide: [Manage your tileset processing and tileset hosting costs](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/manage-tileset-costs/)
    
*   Troubleshooting guide: [Adjust the zoom extent of your tileset](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/adjust-tileset-zoom-extent/)

# Glyphs | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/style-spec/reference/glyphs/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

This property is created by Mapbox Studio automatically, and it is unlikely you will need to change it. You can use custom fonts without changing this property by adding them in [Mapbox Studio](https://console.mapbox.com/studio/)
 or by uploading them with the [Fonts API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/fonts/#add-a-font)
.

A style's `glyphs` property provides a URL template for loading signed-distance-field glyph sets in PBF format. Your map client (Mapbox GL JS, for example) will generate and send glyph requests to the specified font server using the URL template you provide.

    "glyphs": "mapbox://fonts/mapbox/{fontstack}/{range}.pbf"

This URL template should include two tokens:

*   `{fontstack}` When requesting glyphs, this token is replaced with a comma separated list of fonts from a font stack specified in the [`text-font`](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/style-spec/reference/layers/#layout-symbol-text-font)
     property of a symbol layer.
*   `{range}` When requesting glyphs, this token is replaced with a range of 256 Unicode code points. For example, to load glyphs for the [Unicode Basic Latin and Basic Latin-1 Supplement blocks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unicode_block)
    , the range would be `0-255`. The actual ranges that are loaded are determined at runtime based on what text needs to be displayed.

# Unity SDK | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

Maps SDK for Unity
==================

現在のバージョン：`v2.1.1`[変更履歴を表示](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-unity-sdk/blob/develop/documentation/docs/05-changelog.md)

*   check
    
    Vector maps with 3D buildings
    
*   check
    
    Cloudless satellite base map
    
*   check
    
    Global digital elevation model
    
*   check
    
    Custom data
    

[インストール](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/unity/maps/guides/install/)
[githubGithubで貢献](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-unity-sdk)

The Mapbox Maps SDK for Unity is a collection of tools for building Unity applications from real map data. It enables Unity developers to interact with Mapbox web services APIs and create game objects via a C#-based API and graphical user interface.

Version 2 is no longer in active development

Version 2 of the Maps SDK for Unity is no longer in active development. Version 3 is in development and will be released soon.

This documentation has been reduced to include only an installation guide for modern versions of unity.

Visit the [#unity channel](https://discord.com/channels/1004826913229000704/1092426736215535676)
 in the Mapbox Developer Discord to connect with the user community and get updates on version 3.

Get Started[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/unity/maps/guides/#get-started "Get Started への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/unity/maps/guides/install/)
 for instructions on how to install the Maps SDK for Unity and load a map in your scene.

# Glyphs | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/glyphs/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

This property is created by Mapbox Studio automatically, and it is unlikely you will need to change it. You can use custom fonts without changing this property by adding them in [Mapbox Studio](https://console.mapbox.com/studio/)
 or by uploading them with the [Fonts API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/fonts/#add-a-font)
.

A style's `glyphs` property provides a URL template for loading signed-distance-field glyph sets in PBF format. Your map client (Mapbox GL JS, for example) will generate and send glyph requests to the specified font server using the URL template you provide.

    "glyphs": "mapbox://fonts/mapbox/{fontstack}/{range}.pbf"

This URL template should include two tokens:

*   `{fontstack}` When requesting glyphs, this token is replaced with a comma separated list of fonts from a font stack specified in the [`text-font`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/layers/#layout-symbol-text-font)
     property of a symbol layer.
*   `{range}` When requesting glyphs, this token is replaced with a range of 256 Unicode code points. For example, to load glyphs for the [Unicode Basic Latin and Basic Latin-1 Supplement blocks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unicode_block)
    , the range would be `0-255`. The actual ranges that are loaded are determined at runtime based on what text needs to be displayed.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# Address Autofill | Search SDK | Android Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/address-autofill/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

The Address Autofill feature in the Search SDK is designed to streamline the process of entering a valid home or delivery address. This feature is particularly useful during registration or checkout processes, where a smooth user experience can help prevent churn. After a user types only a few characters, the Address Autofill feature will offer suggestions based on the user's input, which significantly enhances the user experience.

User experience[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/address-autofill/#user-experience "Direct link to User experience")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  When it’s time to fill in the address in your app, the user is presented with a form. The top field in the form is focused for input.
2.  When the user starts typing, a list of valid, matching addresses is displayed, with the addresses closest to the user presented first.
3.  The user taps one of the suggested addresses and the form fields are filled.
4.  Your app shows the map with a pin placed for the selected address, which enables the user to visually confirm that they selected the correct address.
5.  Users also have the option of adding a comment for a housing unit, specific delivery instructions, or confirm the address they otherwise would need to type in manually.

  

Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Open [link to the video](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/assets/medias/search-autofill-demo-257a6197a64ee4750643d7a1f14cb429.mp4)
.

We have a [ready-made sample app](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-search-android/blob/main/MapboxSearch/sample/src/main/java/com/mapbox/search/sample/)
 that you can try in Android Studio with Address Autofill and other scenarios. We encourage you to install and try it for yourself.

[EXAMPLE\
\
Full example of Address Autofill\
\
Learn how to integrate Search SDK Address Autofill with UI for suggestions, filled-in form components, and a visual map confirmation (includes Maps SDK) in a sample app.](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/examples/autofill-ui/)

Integration[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/address-autofill/#integration "Direct link to Integration")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Address Autofill provides coroutine-based API. In the following integration steps, we assume that you are familiar with the Kotlin coroutines and your Android project has `Kotlin-ktx` dependencies added. See the [Kotlin coroutines guide](https://developer.android.com/topic/libraries/architecture/coroutines)
 for more information.

[GUIDE\
\
Installation guide\
\
Before using any of Mapbox's Search products, you need to do some common installation steps. Follow this guide to complete installation, then continue with the steps below.](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/install/)

1.  Get instance of `AddressAutofill`:

    val addressAutofill = AddressAutofill.create(mapboxAccessToken)

2.  Add a request for input from the user. There's only one function called `suggestions()` which has overloadings for different use cases. As was mentioned before, the Address Autofill provides coroutine-based API, and `suggestions()` is a suspend function which [should be called only from a coroutine or another suspend function](https://developer.android.com/topic/libraries/architecture/coroutines)
    . In this short example we will assume that we call it from Activity's `lifecycleScope`:

    val query = receivedInputlifecycleScope.launchWhenCreated {    val response = addressAutofill.suggestions(query, AddressAutofillOptions())    response.onValue { suggestions ->        showSuggestions(suggestions)    }.onError { error ->        // process error    }}

3.  Process results to fill in the UI fields. When a user has selected a suggestion, call `select()` function. This function returns `AddressAutofillResult` object that provides detailed address components.

    val suggestion = pickSuggestion(response.suggestions)val selectionResponse = addressAutofill.select(suggestion)selectionResponse.onValue { result ->    val resultAddress = result.address    placeField.setText(resultAddress.place)    countryField.setText(resultAddress.country)}.onError { e ->    // process error}

[RELATED\
\
API Reference\
\
For more information, see the full API reference for the Android Search SDK.](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/api-reference/)

Address Autofill Pricing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/search/guides/address-autofill/#address-autofill-pricing "Direct link to Address Autofill Pricing")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There is no separate charge if you choose to use Mapbox Search SDK for Android to access our APIs, you only pay by the Search API usage.

Usage of the Address Autofill is measured in monthly API sessions. Details about the number of API requests included in the free tier and the cost per request beyond what is included in the free tier are available on the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#autofill-session)
.

* * *

Want to use Autofill on your website?

For using Address Autofill on the web see our [Mapbox Search JS SDK](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-search-js/guides/autofill/)
.

* * *

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# Schema | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

Schema[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#schema "Direct link to Schema")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A schema describes how a style can be configured. If a schema is present in a style, developers can only change the style through specifying configuration options, and cannot access individual layers. The `schema` key is a JSON object with its keys representing the option name and the value [option](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#option)
 objects.

### \*[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#schema-* "Direct link to *")

Optional [option](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#option)
 .

Specification of a configuration option.

As an example, here’s part of the schema of the Mapbox Standard style:

    {    "schema": {        "showPointOfInterestLabels": {            "default": true,            "type": "boolean"        },        "lightPreset": {            "default": "noon",            "values": [ "dawn", "noon", "dusk", "night" ]        }    }}

Option[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#option "Direct link to Option")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An option describes how a particular setting can be configured. The only _required_ key is the `default` value, and all other properties are optional. If they are present, the provided configuration value will be checked against the specification, and might be changed to meet the requirements.

### default[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#option-default "Direct link to default")

Required .

Default configuration value for this option.

### array[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#option-array "Direct link to array")

Optional [boolean](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#boolean)
 .

If true, this option is returned as an array

### maxValue[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#option-maxValue "Direct link to maxValue")

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 .

If this option is a number, this specifies the maximum allowed value. Values higher than this will be clamped to the maximum value.

### metadata[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#option-metadata "Direct link to metadata")

Optional .

Arbitrary properties useful to track with the layer, but do not influence rendering. Properties should be prefixed to avoid collisions, like 'mapbox:'.

### minValue[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#option-minValue "Direct link to minValue")

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 .

If this option is a number, this specifies the minimum allowed value. Values lower than this will be clamped to the minimum value.

### stepValue[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#option-stepValue "Direct link to stepValue")

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 .

If this option is a number, this specifies the increment between allowed values. Values will be rounded towards the nearest allowed value.

### type[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#option-type "Direct link to type")

Optional [enum](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#enum)
 . One of `"string"`, `"number"`, `"boolean"`, `"color"`.

The type this value is coerced to after evaluating the expression. If unspecified, the result is returned as is and is not validated.

`"string"`:

The result will be coerced to a string.

`"number"`:

The result will be coerced to a number.

`"boolean"`:

The result will be coerced to a boolean.

`"color"`:

The result will be coerced to a color.

### values[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/schema/#option-values "Direct link to values")

Optional [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
 of .

If this option is specified, the result must be one of the given values. Otherwise, the default value is used instead.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# Imports | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

Overview[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Imports can be used to add the contents of other styles to the current style. Instead of copying the individual layers, only the other style URL is referenced, so that the imported style is always the most recent version. Imported styles are rendered together with the layers in the current style, but unlike them, they are not available through runtime APIs. Instead, you can specify [configuration values](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#config)
 for the imported style:

    {    "imports": [        {            "id": "basemap",            "url": "mapbox://styles/mapbox/standard",            "config": {                "lightPreset": "dusk",                "showPointOfInterestLabels": false            }        }    ]}

A style can import multiple other styles, which in turn can import other styles (up to 5 levels deep).

Import[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#import "Direct link to Import")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An import description describes the style to be imported, and optionally provides configuration values to be applied to the imported style.

### id[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#import-id "Direct link to id")

Required [string](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#string)
 .

Unique import name.

### url[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#import-url "Direct link to url")

Required [string](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#string)
 .

The URL of the style.

### color-theme[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#import-color-theme "Direct link to color-theme")

Optional [colorTheme](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#colorTheme)
 .

If specified, it overrides the color-theme of the imported style.

### config[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#import-config "Direct link to config")

Optional [config](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#config)
 .

Configuration values for the imported style's options.

### data[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#import-data "Direct link to data")

Optional [style](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/root/)
 .

The inlined style that must correspond to the contents of the specified URL.

Config[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#config "Direct link to Config")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When [importing a style](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#import)
, you can set values for the available configuration [options](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/config#option)
. If present, the value specified for the import will be used, and otherwise the option’s default value will be used.

### \*[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/#config-* "Direct link to *")

Optional .

Value of the imported style's configuration option.

If the imported style does not have a configuration option with the specified name, the value will be ignored. The value provided in the `config` object will be validated against the [option](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/config#option)
 specified in the schema.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# Slots | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/slots/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

Overview[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/slots/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Slots allow style authors to create well-defined insertion points in a style's layer stack that users can put their own layers into. This enables multiple styles to use the same named slots so that custom layers appear at the correct position. Users don’t have find the most appropriate basemap layer name, and insert their own layer in relation to it. Instead, they can rely on an explicitly named slot.

Slots are defined by style authors as a layer of type `slot`. A slot layer only acts as a placeholder and does not render anything by itself. It has the [generic layer properties](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/layers#layer-properties)
 like `id`, `type`, but does not have any other styling properties like `layout`, `paint`, or `source`.

Note that the slot names depend on the basemap style you’re using. For information on what slots are available in a given style, consult the documentation of that style.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# Appearances | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/appearances/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

The [`appearances`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/layers/#layer-properties-appearances)
 [layer](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/layers/)
 property defines sets of [`appearance`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#appearance)
 objects used to quickly change the visual styling of a layer based on a condition. Each appearance object can specify a set of paint and layout property values, allowing for dynamic styling of layers based on application state or user interactions.

    {  "id": "some-layer",  "type": "symbol",  ...  "appearances": [    {      "name": "selected",      "condition": ["feature-state", "select"],      "properties": {        "icon-image": ["image", "poi", {"params": {"fill": "red"}}],        "icon-size": 1.3      }    },    {      "name": "highlighted",      "condition": ["feature-state", "highlighted"],      "properties": {        "icon-rotate": 90      }    }  ]}

Paint and Layout properties compatible with appearances[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/appearances/#paint-and-layout-properties-compatible-with-appearances "Direct link to Paint and Layout properties compatible with appearances")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### icon-image[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/appearances/#icon-image "Direct link to icon-image")

Optional [resolvedImage](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#resolvedImage)
 . paletteWorks with appearances.

Name of image in sprite to use for drawing an image background.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 0.10.0 | \>= 2.0.1 | \>= 2.0.0 |
| data-driven styling | \>= 0.35.0 | \>= 5.1.0 | \>= 3.6.0 |

### icon-offset[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/appearances/#icon-offset "Direct link to icon-offset")

Optional [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
 of [numbers](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number) . Defaults to `[0,0]`. _Requires_ icon-image. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. paletteWorks with appearances.

Offset distance of icon from its anchor. Positive values indicate right and down, while negative values indicate left and up. Each component is multiplied by the value of `icon-size` to obtain the final offset in pixels. When combined with `icon-rotate` the offset will be as if the rotated direction was up.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 0.10.0 | \>= 2.0.1 | \>= 2.0.0 |
| data-driven styling | \>= 0.29.0 | \>= 5.0.0 | \>= 3.5.0 |

### icon-rotate[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/appearances/#icon-rotate "Direct link to icon-rotate")

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 . Units in degrees. Defaults to `0`. _Requires_ icon-image. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. paletteWorks with appearances.

Rotates the icon clockwise.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 0.10.0 | \>= 2.0.1 | \>= 2.0.0 |
| data-driven styling | \>= 0.21.0 | \>= 5.0.0 | \>= 3.5.0 |

### icon-size[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/appearances/#icon-size "Direct link to icon-size")

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 greater than or equal to `0`. Units in factor of the original icon size. Defaults to `1`. _Requires_ icon-image. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. paletteWorks with appearances.

Scales the original size of the icon by the provided factor. The new pixel size of the image will be the original pixel size multiplied by `icon-size`. 1 is the original size; 3 triples the size of the image.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 0.10.0 | \>= 2.0.1 | \>= 2.0.0 |
| data-driven styling | \>= 0.35.0 | \>= 5.1.0 | \>= 3.6.0 |

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# Fog | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/fog/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

A style's `fog` property is a global styling effect that can be used to create an atmospheric gradient, stars, and haze. These properties can increase realism or match a designer's aesthetic. Fog provides a visual cue to improves depth perception and can boost performance by reducing the number of tiles loaded in the distance.

Fog is supported only in `globe` and `mercator` [projections](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/projection/)
.

    "fog": {    "range": [0.8, 8],    "color": "#dc9f9f",    "horizon-blend": 0.5,    "high-color": "#245bde",    "space-color": "#000000",    "star-intensity": 0.15}

color[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/fog/#color "Direct link to color")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Optional [color](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#color)
 . Defaults to `"#ffffff"`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

The color of the atmosphere region immediately below the horizon and within the `range` and above the horizon and within `horizon-blend`. Using opacity is recommended only for smoothly transitioning fog on/off as anything less than 100% opacity results in more tiles loaded and drawn.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 2.3.0 | \>= 10.6.0 | \>= 10.6.0 |

high-color[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/fog/#high-color "Direct link to high-color")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Optional [color](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#color)
 . Defaults to `"#245cdf"`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

The color of the atmosphere region above the horizon, `high-color` extends further above the horizon than the `color` property and its spread can be controlled with `horizon-blend`. The opacity can be set to `0` to remove the high atmosphere color contribution.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 2.9.0 | \>= 10.6.0 | \>= 10.6.0 |

horizon-blend[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/fog/#horizon-blend "Direct link to horizon-blend")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `["interpolate",["linear"],["zoom"],4,0.2,7,0.1]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Horizon blend applies a smooth fade from the color of the atmosphere to the color of space. A value of zero leaves a sharp transition from atmosphere to space. Increasing the value blends the color of atmosphere into increasingly high angles of the sky.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 2.3.0 | \>= 10.6.0 | \>= 10.6.0 |

range[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/fog/#range "Direct link to range")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Optional [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
 of [numbers](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number) between `-20` and `20` inclusive. Defaults to `[0.5,10]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

The start and end distance range in which fog fades from fully transparent to fully opaque. The distance to the point at the center of the map is defined as zero, so that negative range values are closer to the camera, and positive values are farther away.

    [  0.5,  10]

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 2.3.0 | \>= 10.6.0 | \>= 10.6.0 |

space-color[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/fog/#space-color "Direct link to space-color")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Optional [color](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#color)
 . Defaults to `["interpolate",["linear"],["zoom"],4,"#010b19",7,"#367ab9"]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

The color of the region above the horizon and after the end of the `horizon-blend` contribution. The opacity can be set to `0` to have a transparent background.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 2.9.0 | \>= 10.6.0 | \>= 10.6.0 |

star-intensity[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/fog/#star-intensity "Direct link to star-intensity")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `["interpolate",["linear"],["zoom"],5,0.35,6,0]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

A value controlling the star intensity where `0` will show no stars and `1` will show stars at their maximum intensity.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 2.9.0 | \>= 10.6.0 | \>= 10.6.0 |

vertical-range[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/fog/#vertical-range "Direct link to vertical-range")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Optional [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
 of [numbers](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number) greater than or equal to `0`. Defaults to `[0,0]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

An array of two number values, specifying the vertical range, measured in meters, over which the fog should gradually fade out. When both parameters are set to zero, the fog will be rendered without any vertical constraints.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# Other | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

Some style properties are no longer the preferred method of accomplishing a particular styling goal. While they are still supported, they will eventually be removed from the Mapbox Style Specification and it is not recommended to use them in new styles. The following is provided as reference for users who need to continue maintaining legacy styles while transitioning to preferred style properties.

Function[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#function "Direct link to Function")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Warning

As of [v0.41.0](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0410-october-11-2017)
, [property expressions](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/)
 is the preferred method for styling features based on zoom level or the feature's properties. Zoom and property functions are still supported, but will be phased out in a future release.

The value for any layout or paint property may be specified as a _function_. Functions allow you to make the appearance of a map feature change with the current zoom level and/or the feature's properties.

### stops[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#function-stops "Direct link to stops")

Required (except for identity functions) [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
.

A set of one input value and one output value is a "stop." Stop output values must be literal values (in other words not functions or expressions), and appropriate for the property. For example, stop output values for a `fill-color` function property must be [colors](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#color)
.

### property[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#function-property "Direct link to property")

Optional [string](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#string)
.

If specified, the function will take the specified feature property as an input. See Zoom Functions and Property Functions below for more information.

### base[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#function-base "Direct link to base")

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
. Default is 1.

The exponential base of the interpolation curve. It controls the rate at which the function output increases. Higher values make the output increase more towards the high end of the range. With values close to 1 the output increases linearly.

### type[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#function-type "Direct link to type")

Optional [string](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#string)
. One of `"identity"`, `"exponential"`, `"interval"`, or `"categorical"`.

`"identity"`

A function that returns its input as the output.

`"exponential"`

A function that generates an output by interpolating between stops just less than and just greater than the function input. The domain (input value) must be numeric, and the style property must support interpolation. Style properties that support interpolation are marked with

smooth-ramp

, the "exponential" symbol, and exponential is the default function type for these properties.

`"interval"`

A function that returns the output value of the stop just less than the function input. The domain (input value) must be numeric. Any style property may use interval functions. For properties marked with

step-ramp

, the "interval" symbol, this is the default function type.

`"categorical"`

A function that returns the output value of the stop equal to the function input.

### default[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#function-default "Direct link to default")

A value to serve as a fallback function result when a value isn't otherwise available. It is used in the following circumstances:

*   In categorical functions, when the feature value does not match any of the stop domain values.
*   In property and zoom-and-property functions, when a feature does not contain a value for the specified property.
*   In identity functions, when the feature value is not valid for the style property (for example, if the function is being used for a circle-color property but the feature property value is not a string or not a valid color).
*   In interval or exponential property and zoom-and-property functions, when the feature value is not numeric.

If no default is provided, the style property's default is used in these circumstances.

### colorSpace[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#function-colorSpace "Direct link to colorSpace")

Optional [string](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#string)
. One of `"rgb"`, `"lab"`, `"hcl"`.

The color space in which colors interpolated. Interpolating colors in perceptual color spaces like LAB and HCL tend to produce color ramps that look more consistent and produce colors that can be differentiated more easily than those interpolated in RGB space.

`"rgb"`

Use the RGB color space to interpolate color values

`"lab"`

Use the LAB color space to interpolate color values.

`"hcl"`

Use the HCL color space to interpolate color values, interpolating the Hue, Chroma, and Luminance channels individually.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 0.10.0 | \>= 2.0.1 | \>= 2.0.0 |
| `property` | \>= 0.18.0 | \>= 5.0.0 | \>= 3.5.0 |
| `code` | \>= 0.18.0 | \>= 5.0.0 | \>= 3.5.0 |
| `exponential` type | \>= 0.18.0 | \>= 5.0.0 | \>= 3.5.0 |
| `interval` type | \>= 0.18.0 | \>= 5.0.0 | \>= 3.5.0 |
| `categorical` type | \>= 0.18.0 | \>= 5.0.0 | \>= 3.5.0 |
| `identity` type | \>= 0.26.0 | \>= 5.0.0 | \>= 3.5.0 |
| `default` | \>= 0.33.0 | \>= 5.0.0 | \>= 3.5.0 |
| `colorSpace` | \>= 0.26.0 | Not yet supported | Not yet supported |

**Zoom functions** allow the appearance of a map feature to change with map’s zoom level. Zoom functions can be used to create the illusion of depth and control data density. Each stop is an array with two elements: the first is a zoom level and the second is a function output value.

    {    "circle-radius": {        "stops": [            // zoom is 5 -> circle radius will be 1px            [5, 1],            // zoom is 10 -> circle radius will be 2px            [10, 2]        ]    }}

The rendered values of [color](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#color)
, [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
, and [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
 properties are interpolated between stops. [Boolean](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#boolean)
 and [string](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#string)
 property values cannot be interpolated, so their rendered values only change at the specified stops.

There is an important difference between the way that zoom functions render for _layout_ and _paint_ properties. Paint properties are continuously re-evaluated whenever the zoom level changes, even fractionally. The rendered value of a paint property will change, for example, as the map moves between zoom levels `4.1` and `4.6`. Layout properties, however, are evaluated only once for each integer zoom level. To continue the prior example: the rendering of a layout property will _not_ change between zoom levels `4.1` and `4.6`, no matter what stops are specified; but at zoom level `5`, the function will be re-evaluated according to the function, and the property's rendered value will change. (You can include fractional zoom levels in a layout property zoom function, and it will affect the generated values; but, still, the rendering will only change at integer zoom levels.)

**Property functions** allow the appearance of a map feature to change with its properties. Property functions can be used to visually differentiate types of features within the same layer or create data visualizations. Each stop is an array with two elements, the first is a property input value and the second is a function output value. Note that support for property functions is not available across all properties and platforms.

    {    "circle-color": {        "property": "temperature",        "stops": [            // "temperature" is 0   -> circle color will be blue            [0, 'blue'],            // "temperature" is 100 -> circle color will be red            [100, 'red']        ]    }}

**Zoom-and-property functions** allow the appearance of a map feature to change with both its properties _and_ zoom. Each stop is an array with two elements, the first is an object with a property input value and a zoom, and the second is a function output value. Note that support for property functions is not yet complete.

    {    "circle-radius": {        "property": "rating",        "stops": [            // zoom is 0 and "rating" is 0 -> circle radius will be 0px            [{zoom: 0, value: 0}, 0],            // zoom is 0 and "rating" is 5 -> circle radius will be 5px            [{zoom: 0, value: 5}, 5],            // zoom is 20 and "rating" is 0 -> circle radius will be 0px            [{zoom: 20, value: 0}, 0],            // zoom is 20 and "rating" is 5 -> circle radius will be 20px            [{zoom: 20, value: 5}, 20]        ]    }}

Other filters[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#other-filters "Direct link to Other filters")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Warning

In previous versions of the style specification, [filters](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/layers/#layer-properties-filter)
 were defined using the deprecated syntax documented below. Though filters defined with this syntax will continue to work, we recommend using the more flexible [expression](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/)
 syntax instead. Expression syntax and the deprecated syntax below cannot be mixed in a single filter definition.

### Existential filters[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#existential-filters "Direct link to Existential filters")

`["has", key]` feature\[key\] exists

`["!has", key]` feature\[key\] does not exist

### Comparison filters[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#comparison-filters "Direct link to Comparison filters")

`["==", key, value]` equality: feature\[key\] = value

`["!=", key, value]` inequality: feature\[key\] ≠ value

`[">", key, value]` greater than: feature\[key\] > value

`[">=", key, value]` greater than or equal: feature\[key\] ≥ value

`["<", key, value]` less than: feature\[key\] < value

`["<=", key, value]` less than or equal: feature\[key\] ≤ value

### Set membership filters[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#set-membership-filters "Direct link to Set membership filters")

`["in", key, v0, ..., vn]` set inclusion: feature\[key\] ∈ {v0, ..., vn}

`["!in", key, v0, ..., vn]` set exclusion: feature\[key\] ∉ { v0, ..., vn}

### Combining filters[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/other/#combining-filters "Direct link to Combining filters")

`["all", f0, ..., fn]` logical `AND`: f0 ∧ ... ∧ fn

`["any", f0, ..., fn]` logical `OR`: f0 ∨ ... ∨ fn

`["none", f0, ..., fn]` logical `NOR`: ¬f0 ∧ ... ∧ ¬fn

A key must be a string that identifies a feature property, or one of the following special keys:

*   `"$type"`: the feature type. This key may be used with the `"=="`,`"!="`, `"in"`, and `"!in"` operators. Possible values are `"Point"`, `"LineString"`, and `"Polygon"`.
*   `"$id"`: the feature identifier. This key may be used with the `"=="`,`"!="`, `"has"`, `"!has"`, `"in"`, and `"!in"` operators.

A value (and v0, ..., vn for set operators) must be a [string](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#string)
, [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
, or [boolean](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#boolean)
 to compare the property value against.

Set membership filters are a compact and efficient way to test whether a field matches any of multiple values.

The comparison and set membership filters implement strictly-typed comparisons; for example, all of the following evaluate to false: `0 &lt; "1"`, `2 == "2"`, `"true" in [true, false]`.

The `"all"`, `"any"`, and `"none"` filter operators are used to create compound filters. The values f0, ..., fn must be filter expressions themselves.

    ["==", "$type", "LineString"]

This filter requires that the `class` property of each feature is equal to either "street\_major", "street\_minor", or "street\_limited".

    ["in", "class", "street_major", "street_minor", "street_limited"]</code>

The combining filter "all" takes the three other filters that follow it and requires all of them to be true for a feature to be included: a feature must have a `class` equal to "street\_limited", its `admin_level` must be greater than or equal to 3, and its type cannot be Polygon. You could change the combining filter to "any" to allow features matching any of those criteria to be included - features that are Polygons, but have a different `class` value, and so on.

    [    "all",    ["==", "class", "street_limited"],    [">=", "admin_level", 3],    ["!in", "$type", "Polygon"]]

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 0.10.0 | \>= 2.0.1 | \>= 2.0.0 |
| `has` / `!has` | \>= 0.19.0 | \>= 4.1.0 | \>= 3.3.0 |

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# Lights | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

There are two lighting models available:

*   [**3D lighting**](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#3d-lighting)
     is a new lighting mode introduced in Maps SDK v11 for iOS and Android, and Web SDK v3. It is used for illuminating the entire scene, including extruded features, 3D models and all other layers. You should choose this mode if your map uses 3D models, or fill extrusions that should cast shadows, or if you want the global lights to affect the illumination of the entire scene.
*   [**Flat lighting**](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#flat-lighting)
     is the traditional lighting mode which is used to illuminate fill extrusions and the hillshade layer. You should choose this mode if your map doesn’t contain models or fill extrusions, and you don’t need complex lighting options.

3D lighting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#3d-lighting "Direct link to 3D lighting")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This mode enables dynamic lighting, enhanced sense of depth and symbolic realism on the whole map. It enables 3D extrusions and models to cast shadows. The root-level `lights` array takes a list of light source objects.

### Light source[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#light-source "Direct link to Light source")

Each light has its own properties that can be specified under `properties`. These, together with the layer properties determine the final color of the layer. The layers are lit uniformly with the ambient light (left image). Directional light shades the parts that face towards it more brightly (middle image). Directional light also casts shadows causing the occluded areas to receive less light and hence appear darker. The combined lighting terms give the full lighting solution (right image).

[`ambient`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#ambient)
 and [`directional`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#directional)
 lights are combined to give the final lighting solution.

##### id[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#id "Direct link to id")

Required [string](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#string)
 .

Unique light name.

##### properties[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#properties "Direct link to properties")

Optional properties .

Properties of the light.

##### type[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#type "Direct link to type")

Optional [enum](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#enum)
 . One of `"ambient"`, `"directional"`, `"flat"`.

Type of the light to be added

`"ambient"`:

An indirect light affecting all objects in the map adding a constant amount of light on them. It has no explicit direction and cannot cast shadows.

`"directional"`:

A light that has a direction and is located at infinite distance, so its rays are parallel. It simulates the sun light and can cast shadows.

`"flat"`:

A global directional light source which is only applied on 3D and hillshade layers. Using this type disables other light sources.

#### `*-emissive-strength` paint property[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#-emissive-strength-paint-property "Direct link to -emissive-strength-paint-property")

The `*-emissive-strength` property of some of the layers provides an additional control mechanism that affects the lighting calculations for those layers. A value of `0` indicates that the layer does not have any emissive component and its final color is affected by the layer color and the 3D lighting settings described earlier. A value of `1` indicates that the layer’s final color is determined solely by the layer color. Note that shadows also affect the final color of the layer if [`shadow-intensity`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#properties_light-directional-shadow-intensity)
 is non-zero.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

#### `measure-light` expression[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#measure-light-expression "Direct link to measure-light-expression")

Using the [`measure-light`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#measure-light)
 expression, a layer can change its color based on the light configuration.

### Light source types[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#light-source-types "Direct link to Light source types")

#### Directional[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#directional "Direct link to Directional")

A light that has a direction and is located at infinite distance, so its rays are parallel. It simulates the sun light and can cast shadows.

##### cast-shadows[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#properties_light-directional-cast-shadows "Direct link to cast-shadows")

Optional [boolean](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#boolean)
 . Defaults to `false`.

Enable/Disable shadow casting for this light

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

##### color[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#properties_light-directional-color "Direct link to color")

Optional [color](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#color)
 . Defaults to `"#ffffff"`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Color of the directional light.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

##### direction[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#properties_light-directional-direction "Direct link to direction")

Optional [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
 of [numbers](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number) \[ between `0` and `360` inclusive, between `0` and `90` inclusive \]. Defaults to `[210,30]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Direction of the light source specified as \[a azimuthal angle, p polar angle\] where a indicates the azimuthal angle of the light relative to north (in degrees and proceeding clockwise), and p indicates polar angle of the light (from 0°, directly above, to 180°, directly below).

    [  90,  40]

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

##### intensity[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#properties_light-directional-intensity "Direct link to intensity")

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `0.5`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

A multiplier for the color of the directional light.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

##### shadow-intensity[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#properties_light-directional-shadow-intensity "Direct link to shadow-intensity")

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `1`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Determines the shadow strength, affecting the shadow receiver surfaces final color. Values near 0.0 reduce the shadow contribution to the final color. Values near to 1.0 make occluded surfaces receive almost no directional light. Designed to be used mostly for transitioning between values 0 and 1.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

##### shadow-quality[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#properties_light-directional-shadow-quality "Direct link to shadow-quality")

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `1`.

Determines the quality of the shadows on the map. A value of 1 ensures the highest quality and is the default value.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | Not yet supported | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

##### Ambient[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#ambient "Direct link to Ambient")

An indirect light affecting all objects in the map adding a constant amount of light on them. It has no explicit direction and cannot cast shadows.

##### color[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#properties_light-ambient-color "Direct link to color")

Optional [color](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#color)
 . Defaults to `"#ffffff"`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Color of the ambient light.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

##### intensity[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#properties_light-ambient-intensity "Direct link to intensity")

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `0.5`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

A multiplier for the color of the ambient light.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 | \>= 11.0.0 |

##### Flat[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#flat "Direct link to Flat")

A global directional light source which is only applied on 3D and hillshade layers. Using this type disables other light sources. See the following [flat lighting](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#flat-lighting)
 for available configuration options. Specifying a light with type `flat` is equivalent to specifying it as a root-level `light` property.

Flat lighting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#flat-lighting "Direct link to Flat lighting")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A style’s `light` property provides a global light source for that style. Since this property is only used to light extruded or terrain features, you will only see visible changes to your map style if you are using extrusions or terrain.

Deprecated

If your style has a [3D `lights` property](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#3d-lighting)
, the root-level `light` property will be ignored.

Deprecated

Prefer using [`flat` light type](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#flat)
 instead in the `lights` API if all your clients support it. It takes the same properties as specified here.

    "light": {  "anchor": "viewport",  "color": "white",  "intensity": 0.4}

### anchor[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#anchor "Direct link to anchor")

Optional [enum](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#enum)
 . One of `"map"`, `"viewport"`. Defaults to `"viewport"`.

Whether extruded geometries are lit relative to the map or viewport.

`"map"`:

The position of the light source is aligned to the rotation of the map.

`"viewport"`:

The position of the light source is aligned to the rotation of the viewport.

    "map"

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 0.27.0 | \>= 5.1.0 | \>= 3.6.0 |

### color[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#color "Direct link to color")

Optional [color](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#color)
 . Defaults to `"#ffffff"`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Color tint for lighting extruded geometries.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 0.27.0 | \>= 5.1.0 | \>= 3.6.0 |

### intensity[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#intensity "Direct link to intensity")

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `0.5`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Intensity of lighting (on a scale from 0 to 1). Higher numbers will present as more extreme contrast.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 0.27.0 | \>= 5.1.0 | \>= 3.6.0 |

### position[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/light/#position "Direct link to position")

Optional [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
 of [numbers](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number) . Defaults to `[1.15,210,30]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Position of the light source relative to lit (extruded) geometries, in \[r radial coordinate, a azimuthal angle, p polar angle\] where r indicates the distance from the center of the base of an object to its light, a indicates the position of the light relative to 0° (0° when `light.anchor` is set to `viewport` corresponds to the top of the viewport, or 0° when `light.anchor` is set to `map` corresponds to due north, and degrees proceed clockwise), and p indicates the height of the light (from 0°, directly above, to 180°, directly below).

    [  1.5,  90,  80]

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 0.27.0 | \>= 5.1.0 | \>= 3.6.0 |

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# Featuresets | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

A style's `featuresets` property defines groups of style layers that are available for _interaction_ when the style is imported from another style. Our mapping libraries and SDKs allow you add interactions such as _click_, and _hover_ to featuresets, and quickly access the underlying feature data for use in your application or to set feature states for dynamic styling.

When using an [imported style](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/imports/)
, its layers are not accessible at runtime. Featuresets exist to allow style designers to expose a subset of layers for interaction. You can think of featuresets as a way of defining an API for the imported style.

In the example below the style defines featureset `poi` that combines `poi-label` and `transition-label` layers.

    {    "featuresets": {        "poi": {            "selectors": [                {                    "layer": "poi-label",                    "properties": { // Defines which properties are available to user of POI featuerset                        "name": ["get", "name"],                        "group": "poi"                    }                },                {                    "layer": "transit-label",                    "properties": {                        "name": ["get", "stop_name"],                        "group": "transit"                    },                    "featureNamespace": "poi-B"                }            ]        }    },    "layers": [        {"id": "poi-label", ...}        {"id": "transit-label", ...}    ]}

Featuresets also define the feature properties to expose during map interactions. Each selector includes a [properties](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#Selector-properties)
 object to identify the available properties. The properties can be accessed in the interaction handler (see the _Add and interaction to a featureset_ code snippet below).

A common pattern when adding featuresets to a style is to define useful feature states into the layers exposed via featuresets. For example, the style designer could define a `highlight` feature state which can be used to change a feature's color when it is interacted with. The following snippet shows how to define a `highlight` feature state in the `poi-label` layer.

    {    "id": "poi-label",    "paint": {        // Highlight the label when feature state is set.        // Note: this is just an example, and may not work as expected in practice.        // The actual implementation may require additional logic to handle different states.        "text-color": ["case", ["boolean", ["feature-state", "highlight"], false], "#f00", "#000"]    }}

Our mapping libraries and SDKs include similar APIs for interacting with featuresets. The following code snippets show how to add a click interaction to notional `poi` featureset defined above on various platforms.

Mapbox GL JS

Android SDK

iOS SDK

Add an interaction to featureset

Mapbox GL JS

Android SDK

iOS SDK

    // Add a click interaction to `poi` featureset defined in the style imported with `my-poi-fragment` id.map.addInteraction('poi-click-interaction', {    type: 'click',    target: {featuresetId: 'poi', importId: 'my-poi-fragment'},    handler: (e) => {        // The properties defined in the selectors `properties` objects.        console.log("Poi clicked", e.feature.properties.name, e.feature.properties.group)        map.setFeatureState(e.feature, {highlight: true});    }});

Add an interaction to featureset

Mapbox GL JS

Android SDK

iOS SDK

    // Add a click interaction to `poi` featureset defined in the style imported with `my-poi-fragment` id.mapboxMap.addInteraction(    ClickInteraction.featureset(id = "poi", importId = "my-poi-fragment") { feature, context ->    val name = feature.properties.getString("name")    val group = feature.properties.getString("group")    // Log the clicked POI information    println("Poi clicked: $name, $group")    // Highlight the clicked feature    mapboxMap.setFeatureState(        featuresetFeature = feature,        state = mapOf("highlight" to true)    )    true // Stops propagation    })

Add an interaction to featureset

Mapbox GL JS

Android SDK

iOS SDK

    // Add a click interaction to `poi` featureset defined in the style imported with `my-poi-fragment` id.mapView.mapboxMap.addInteraction(TapInteraction(.featureset("poi", importId: "my-poi-fragment")) { [weak mapView] feature, context in    if let name = feature.properties["name"] as? String,        let group = feature.properties["group"] as? String {        print("Poi clicked: \(name), \(group)")    }    // Highlight the clicked feature    mapView?.mapboxMap.setFeatureState(feature, state: ["highlight": true])    return true // Stops propagation})

Note

The [Mapbox Standard](https://docs.mapbox.com/map-styles/standard/)
 and Mapbox Standard Satellite styles define featuresets that can be used to interact with the layers in the style. For example, the Mapbox Standard style defines a `poi` featureset that can be used to interact with the POI layers in the style.

Featureset[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#featureset "Direct link to Featureset")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A featureset defines a named group of layers that are available for interaction when the style is imported from another style.

### metadata[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#featureset-metadata "Direct link to metadata")

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional .

Arbitrary properties useful to track with the stylesheet, but do not influence rendering. Properties should be prefixed to avoid collisions, like 'mapbox:'.

### selectors[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#featureset-selectors "Direct link to selectors")

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
 of [selector](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#selector) .

A collection of categorized selectors.

Selector[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#selector "Direct link to Selector")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A selector describes a layer from the current style and the properties from its features that will be available for interactions.

### layer[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#Selector-layer "Direct link to layer")

EXPERIMENTAL

Required [string](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#string)
 .

The ID of a layer that exists in the current style.

### featureNamespace[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#Selector-featureNamespace "Direct link to featureNamespace")

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [string](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#string)
 .

An optional field that represents the feature namespace defined by the selector within a featureset to which this feature belongs. If the underlying source is the same for multiple selectors within a featureset, the same featureNamespace should be used across those selectors.

### properties[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#Selector-properties "Direct link to properties")

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [selectorProperty](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#selectorProperty)
 .

Properties accessible to the end user through queried feautures. If properties are empty, no feature properties are exposed. If undefined, all original feature properties will be accessible.

SelectorProperty[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#selectorProperty "Direct link to SelectorProperty")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### \*[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/featuresets/#SelectorProperty-* "Direct link to *")

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional .

The value of the property. It can be an expression that generates the returned value from the feature, or a constant value specifying the returned value.

# Geography and geometry | Mapbox GL JS | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search GL JS API Reference

General utilities and types that relate to working with and manipulating geographic information or geometries.

[LngLat](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglat)

---------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/geo/lng\_lat.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/geo/lng_lat.ts#L70-L195)

A `LngLat` object represents a given longitude and latitude coordinate, measured in degrees. These coordinates use longitude, latitude coordinate order (as opposed to latitude, longitude) to match the [GeoJSON specification](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7946#section-4)
, which is equivalent to the OGC:CRS84 coordinate reference system.

Note that any Mapbox GL method that accepts a `LngLat` object as an argument or option can also accept an `Array` of two numbers and will perform an implicit conversion. This flexible type is documented as [LngLatLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike)
.

new LngLat(lng: [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number)
, lat: [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number)
)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglat-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [lng](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglat-parameters-lng)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) ` | Longitude, measured in degrees. |
| #### [lat](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglat-parameters-lat)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) ` | Latitude, measured in degrees. |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglat-example)

    const ll = new mapboxgl.LngLat(-123.9749, 40.7736);console.log(ll.lng); // = -123.9749

### [Static Members](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglat-static-members)

[LngLatBounds](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatbounds)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/geo/lng\_lat.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/geo/lng_lat.ts#L235-L517)

A `LngLatBounds` object represents a geographical bounding box, defined by its southwest and northeast points in [`longitude`](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/lat-lon/)
 and [`latitude`](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/lat-lon/)
. `Longitude` values are typically set between `-180` to `180`, but can exceed this range if `renderWorldCopies` is set to `true`. `Latitude` values must be within `-85.051129` to `85.051129`.

If no arguments are provided to the constructor, a `null` bounding box is created.

Note that any Mapbox GL method that accepts a `LngLatBounds` object as an argument or option can also accept an `Array` of two [LngLatLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike)
 constructs and will perform an implicit conversion. This flexible type is documented as [LngLatBoundsLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatboundslike)
.

new LngLatBounds(sw: [LngLatLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike)
?, ne: [LngLatLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike)
?)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatbounds-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [sw](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatbounds-parameters-sw)<br><br>`[LngLatLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike) ?` | The southwest corner of the bounding box. |
| #### [ne](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatbounds-parameters-ne)<br><br>`[LngLatLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike) ?` | The northeast corner of the bounding box. |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatbounds-example)

    const sw = new mapboxgl.LngLat(-73.9876, 40.7661);const ne = new mapboxgl.LngLat(-73.9397, 40.8002);const llb = new mapboxgl.LngLatBounds(sw, ne);

### [Static Members](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatbounds-static-members)
