[LngLatBoundsLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatboundslike)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/geo/lng\_lat.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/geo/lng_lat.ts#L519-L531)

A [LngLatBounds](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatbounds)
 object, an array of [LngLatLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike)
 objects in \[sw, ne\] order, or an array of numbers in \[west, south, east, north\] order.

### [Type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatboundslike-type)

`([LngLatBounds](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatbounds)  | [[LngLatLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike) , [LngLatLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike) ] | [[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) , [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) , [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) , [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) ])`

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatboundslike-example)

    const v1 = new mapboxgl.LngLatBounds(  new mapboxgl.LngLat(-73.9876, 40.7661),  new mapboxgl.LngLat(-73.9397, 40.8002));const v2 = new mapboxgl.LngLatBounds([-73.9876, 40.7661], [-73.9397, 40.8002]);const v3 = [[-73.9876, 40.7661], [-73.9397, 40.8002]];

Was this section on LngLatBoundsLike helpful?YesNo

[LngLatLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/geo/lng\_lat.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/geo/lng_lat.ts#L197-L206)

A [LngLat](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglat)
 object, an array of two numbers representing longitude and latitude, or an object with `lng` and `lat` or `lon` and `lat` properties.

### [Type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike-type)

`([LngLat](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglat)  | {lng: [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) , lat: [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) } | {lon: [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) , lat: [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) } | [[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) , [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) ])`

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#lnglatlike-example)

    const v1 = new mapboxgl.LngLat(-122.420679, 37.772537);const v2 = [-122.420679, 37.772537];const v3 = {lon: -122.420679, lat: 37.772537};

Was this section on LngLatLike helpful?YesNo

[MercatorCoordinate](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#mercatorcoordinate)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/geo/mercator\_coordinate.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/geo/mercator_coordinate.ts#L126-L201)

A `MercatorCoordinate` object represents a projected three dimensional position.

`MercatorCoordinate` uses the web mercator projection ([EPSG:3857](https://epsg.io/3857)
) with slightly different units:

*   the size of 1 unit is the width of the projected world instead of the "mercator meter"
*   the origin of the coordinate space is at the north-west corner instead of the middle.

For example, `MercatorCoordinate(0, 0, 0)` is the north-west corner of the mercator world and `MercatorCoordinate(1, 1, 0)` is the south-east corner. If you are familiar with [vector tiles](https://github.com/mapbox/vector-tile-spec)
 it may be helpful to think of the coordinate space as the `0/0/0` tile with an extent of `1`.

The `z` dimension of `MercatorCoordinate` is conformal. A cube in the mercator coordinate space would be rendered as a cube.

new MercatorCoordinate(x: [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number)
, y: [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number)
, z: [number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number)
)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#mercatorcoordinate-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [x](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#mercatorcoordinate-parameters-x)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) ` | The x component of the position. |
| #### [y](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#mercatorcoordinate-parameters-y)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) ` | The y component of the position. |
| #### [z](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#mercatorcoordinate-parameters-z)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) `(default `0`) | The z component of the position. |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#mercatorcoordinate-example)

    const nullIsland = new mapboxgl.MercatorCoordinate(0.5, 0.5, 0);

### [Static Members](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#mercatorcoordinate-static-members)

#### fromLngLat

()

caret-right

### [Instance Members](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#mercatorcoordinate-instance-members)

#### toLngLat

()

caret-right

#### toAltitude

()

caret-right

#### meterInMercatorCoordinateUnits

()

caret-right

*   [Example: Add a custom style layer](https://www.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/custom-style-layer/)

[Point](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#point)

-------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/map.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/map.ts#L5337-L5344)

A [`Point` geometry](https://github.com/mapbox/point-geometry)
 object, which has `x` and `y` screen coordinates in pixels, or other units.

`[Point](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#point) `

[PointLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#pointlike)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/map.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/map.ts#L5346-L5353)

A [Point](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#point)
 or an array of two numbers representing `x` and `y` screen coordinates in pixels, or other units.

### [Type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#pointlike-type)

`([Point](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#point)  | [Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array) <[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) >)`

    const p1 = new mapboxgl.Point(400, 525); // a PointLike which is a Pointconst p2 = [400, 525]; // a PointLike which is an array of two numbers

# Terrain | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/terrain/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

A style's `terrain` property is a global modifier that elevates layers and markers based on a DEM data source.

    "terrain": {    "source": "mapbox-raster-dem",    "exaggeration": 2}

Terrain is supported only in `globe` and `mercator` [projections](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/projection/)
.

Use the Mapbox Terrain-DEM v1 Tileset

The [`mapbox-terrain-dem-v1`](https://docs.mapbox.com/data/tilesets/reference/mapbox-terrain-dem-v1/)
 raster tileset is compatible with `terrain`. See the following code examples for your platform to learn how to enable terrain in your map style:

*   [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/add-terrain/)
    
*   [Maps SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/examples/sky-layer/)
    
*   [Maps SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/examples/compose/style-terrain/)
    

source[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/terrain/#source "Direct link to source")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Required [string](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#string)
 .

Name of a source of `raster_dem` type to be used for terrain elevation.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 2.0.0 | \>= 10.0.0 | \>= 10.0.0 |

exaggeration[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/terrain/#exaggeration "Direct link to exaggeration")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1000` inclusive. Defaults to `1`. _Requires_ source. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Exaggerates the elevation of the terrain by multiplying the data from the DEM with this value.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 2.0.0 | \>= 10.0.0 | \>= 10.0.0 |

# Rain | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/rain/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

A style's `rain` property is a global styling effect that can be used to add animated rain to the map. This property can be used to create a rainy scene or match a designer's aesthetic. Rain provides a visual cue to improve the appearance of a map and can be used to create a more true-to-life atmosphere.

[EXAMPLE\
\
Add a rain effect to a map\
\
See what a style using the `rain` property looks like in a web browser with Mapbox GL JS.](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/rain)
[PLAYGROUND\
\
Rain and Snow Playground\
\
The rain and snow playground allows you to test these style values in browser, offering UI to adjust the particle density, intensity, color, speed, etc.](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/rain)

    "rain": {  "density": 0.5,  "intensity": 1.0,  "color": "#a8adbc",  "opacity": 0.7,  "vignette": 1.0,  "vignette-color": "#464646",  "direction": [0, 80],  "droplet-size": [2.6, 18.2],  "distortion-strength": 0.7,  "center-thinning": 0 }

center-thinning[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/rain/#center-thinning "Direct link to center-thinning")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `0.57`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Thinning factor of rain particles from center. 0 - no thinning. 1 - maximal central area thinning.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

color[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/rain/#color "Direct link to color")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [color](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#color)
 . Defaults to `["interpolate",["linear"],["measure-light","brightness"],0,"#03113d",0.3,"#a8adbc"]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Individual rain particle dorplets color.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

density[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/rain/#density "Direct link to density")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `["interpolate",["linear"],["zoom"],11,0,13,0.5]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Rain particles density. Controls the overall screen density of the rain.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

direction[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/rain/#direction "Direct link to direction")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

 of [numbers](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number) between `0` and `360` inclusive. Defaults to `[0,80]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Main rain particles direction. Azimuth and polar angles.

    [  0,  45]

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `0.7`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

| --- | --- | --- | --- |

# User interaction handlers | Mapbox GL JS | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search GL JS API Reference

Items related to the ways in which the map responds to user input.

[BoxZoomHandler](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#boxzoomhandler)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/handler/box\_zoom.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/handler/box_zoom.ts#L15-L178)

The `BoxZoomHandler` allows the user to zoom the map to fit within a bounding box. The bounding box is defined by clicking and holding `shift` while dragging the cursor.

[FeaturesetDescriptor](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#featuresetdescriptor)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/util/vectortile\_to\_geojson.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/util/vectortile_to_geojson.ts#L82-L84)

`FeaturesetDescriptor` references a featureset in a style. If `importId` is not specified, the featureset is assumed to be in the root style.

### [Type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#featuresetdescriptor-type)

`{featuresetId: [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) , importId: [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ?}`

### [Properties](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#featuresetdescriptor-properties)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [featuresetId](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#featuresetdescriptor-properties-featuresetid)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ` |     |
| #### [importId](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#featuresetdescriptor-properties-importid)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ?` |     |

[Interaction](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#interaction)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/interactions.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/interactions.ts#L16-L19)

`Interaction` is a configuration object used with [Map#addInteraction](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#addinteraction)
 to handle user events, such as clicks and hovers. Interactions can be applied globally or to specific targets, such as layers or featuresets.

### [Type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#interaction-type)

`{type: MapInteractionEventType, target: [TargetDescriptor](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#targetdescriptor) ?, namespace: [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ?, filter: FilterSpecification?, handler: function (event: [InteractionEvent](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#interactionevent) ): ([boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean)  | void)}`

### [Properties](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#interaction-properties)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [filter](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#interaction-properties-filter)<br><br>`FilterSpecification?` |     |
| #### [handler](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#interaction-properties-handler)<br><br>`function (event: [InteractionEvent](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#interactionevent) ): ([boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean)  \| void)` |     |
| #### [namespace](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#interaction-properties-namespace)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ?` |     |
| #### [target](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#interaction-properties-target)<br><br>`[TargetDescriptor](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#targetdescriptor) ?` |     |
| #### [type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#interaction-properties-type)<br><br>`MapInteractionEventType` |     |

[KeyboardHandler](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#keyboardhandler)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/handler/keyboard.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/handler/keyboard.ts#L28-L209)

The `KeyboardHandler` allows the user to zoom, rotate, and pan the map using the following keyboard shortcuts:

*   `=` / `+`: Increase the zoom level by 1.
*   `Shift-=` / `Shift-+`: Increase the zoom level by 2.
*   `-`: Decrease the zoom level by 1.
*   `Shift--`: Decrease the zoom level by 2.
*   Arrow keys: Pan by 100 pixels.
*   `Shift+⇢`: Increase the rotation by 15 degrees.
*   `Shift+⇠`: Decrease the rotation by 15 degrees.
*   `Shift+⇡`: Increase the pitch by 10 degrees.
*   `Shift+⇣`: Decrease the pitch by 10 degrees.

### [Instance Members](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#keyboardhandler-instance-members)

#### enable

()

caret-right

#### disable

()

caret-right

#### isEnabled

()

caret-right

#### isActive

()

caret-right

#### disableRotation

()

caret-right

#### enableRotation

()

caret-right

[TargetDescriptor](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#targetdescriptor)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/util/vectortile\_to\_geojson.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/util/vectortile_to_geojson.ts#L87-L91)

`TargetDescriptor` defines the target for a [Map#queryRenderedFeatures](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#queryrenderedfeatures)
 query to inspect, referencing either a [style layer ID](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/style-spec/#layer-id)
 or a [FeaturesetDescriptor](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#featuresetdescriptor)
. It acts as a universal target for [Map#addInteraction](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#addinteraction)
 and [Map#queryRenderedFeatures](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#queryrenderedfeatures)
.

### [Type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#targetdescriptor-type)

`({layerId: [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) } | [FeaturesetDescriptor](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#featuresetdescriptor) )`

Was this section on TargetDescriptor helpful?YesNo

[TargetFeature](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#targetfeature)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/util/vectortile\_to\_geojson.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/util/vectortile_to_geojson.ts#L113-L152)

`TargetFeature` is a [GeoJSON](http://geojson.org/)
 [Feature object](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7946#section-3.2)
 representing a feature associated with a specific query target in [Map#queryRenderedFeatures](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#queryrenderedfeatures)
. For featuresets in imports, `TargetFeature` includes a `target` reference as a [TargetDescriptor](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#targetdescriptor)
 and may also include a `namespace` property to prevent feature ID collisions when layers defined in the query target reference multiple sources. Unlike features returned for root style featuresets, `TargetFeature` omits the `layer`, `source`, and `sourceLayer` properties if the feature belongs to import style.

Extends Feature.

### [Instance Members](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#targetfeature-instance-members)

#### target

caret-right

#### namespace

caret-right

Was this section on TargetFeature helpful?YesNo

[TouchPitchHandler](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#touchpitchhandler)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/handler/touch\_zoom\_rotate.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/handler/touch_zoom_rotate.ts#L210-L287)

The `TouchPitchHandler` allows the user to pitch the map by dragging up and down with two fingers.

Extends TwoTouchHandler.

new TouchPitchHandler(map: [Map](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map)
)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#touchpitchhandler-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [map](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#touchpitchhandler-parameters-map)<br><br>`[Map](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map) ` |     |

### [Related](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#touchpitchhandler-related)

*   [Example: Set pitch and bearing](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/set-perspective/)

[TouchZoomRotateHandler](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/handlers/#touchzoomrotatehandler)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/handler/shim/touch\_zoom\_rotate.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/handler/shim/touch_zoom_rotate.ts#L17-L115)

The `TouchZoomRotateHandler` allows the user to zoom and rotate the map by pinching on a touchscreen.

They can zoom with one finger by double tapping and dragging. On the second tap, hold the finger down and drag up or down to zoom in or out.

# Events and event types | Mapbox GL JS | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search GL JS API Reference

`Map` and other Mapbox GL JS classes emit events in response to user interactions or changes in state. `Evented` is the interface used to bind and unbind listeners for these events. This page describes the different types of events that Mapbox GL JS can raise.

You can learn more about the originating events here:

*   [`Map` events](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)
     fire when a user interacts with a `Map`.
*   [`Marker` events](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-events)
     fire when a user interacts with a `Marker`.
*   [`Popup` events](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-events)
     fire when a user interacts with a `Popup`.
*   [`GeolocationControl` events](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-events)
     fire when a user interacts with a `GeolocationControl`.

[Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)

--------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/util/evented.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/util/evented.ts#L73-L207)

`Evented` mixes methods into other classes for event capabilities.

Unless you are developing a plugin you will most likely use these methods through classes like `Map` or `Popup`.

For lists of events you can listen for, see API documentation for specific classes: [`Map`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)
, [`Marker`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)
, [`Popup`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)
, and [`GeolocationControl`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)
.

### [Instance Members](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented-instance-members)

#### on

()

caret-right

#### off

()

caret-right

#### once

()

caret-right

Was this section on Evented helpful?YesNo

[MapBoxZoomEvent](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapboxzoomevent)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/events.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/events.ts#L387-L411)

`MapBoxZoomEvent` is a class used to generate the events 'boxzoomstart', 'boxzoomend', and 'boxzoomcancel'. For a full list of available events, see [`Map` events](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)
.

### [Type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapboxzoomevent-type)

`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) `

### [Properties](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapboxzoomevent-properties)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [originalEvent](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapboxzoomevent-properties-originalevent)<br><br>`[MouseEvent](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/MouseEvent) ` | The DOM event that triggered the boxzoom event. Can be a `MouseEvent` or `KeyboardEvent` . |
| #### [target](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapboxzoomevent-properties-target)<br><br>`[Map](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map) ` | The `Map` instance that triggered the event. |
| #### [type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapboxzoomevent-properties-type)<br><br>``(`"boxzoomstart"` \| `"boxzoomend"` \| `"boxzoomcancel"`)`` | The type of originating event. For a full list of available events, see [`Map` events](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)<br> . |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapboxzoomevent-example)

    // Example trigger of a BoxZoomEvent of type "boxzoomstart"map.on('boxzoomstart', (e) => {    console.log('event type:', e.type);    // event type: boxzoomstart});

    // Example of a BoxZoomEvent of type "boxzoomstart"// {//   originalEvent: {...},//   type: "boxzoomstart",//   target: {...}// }

### [Related](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapboxzoomevent-related)

*   [Reference: `Map` events API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)
    
*   [Example: Highlight features within a bounding box](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/using-box-queryrenderedfeatures/)
    

Was this section on MapBoxZoomEvent helpful?YesNo

[MapDataEvent](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/events.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/events.ts#L435-L472)

`MapDataEvent` is a type of events related to loading data, styles, and sources. For a full list of available events, see [`Map` events](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)
.

### [Type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-type)

`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) `

### [Properties](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-properties)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [coord](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-properties-coord)<br><br>`OverscaledTileID?` | The coordinate of the tile if the event has a `dataType` of `source` and the event is related to loading of a tile. |
| #### [dataType](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-properties-datatype)<br><br>``(`"source"` \| `"style"`)`` | The type of data that has changed. One of `'source'` or `'style'` , where `'source'` refers to the data associated with any source, and `'style'` refers to the entire [style](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/style/)<br> used by the map. |
| #### [isSourceLoaded](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-properties-issourceloaded)<br><br>`[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean) ?` | True if the event has a `dataType` of `source` and the source has no outstanding network requests. |
| #### [source](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-properties-source)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) ?` | The [style spec representation of the source](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/style-spec/sources/)<br> if the event has a `dataType` of `source` . |
| #### [sourceDataType](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-properties-sourcedatatype)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ?` | Included if the event has a `dataType` of `source` and the event signals that internal data has been received or changed. Possible values are `metadata` , `content` and `visibility` , and `error` . |
| #### [sourceId](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-properties-sourceid)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ?` | The `id` of the [`source`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/style-spec/sources/)<br> that triggered the event, if the event has a `dataType` of `source` . Same as the `id` of the object in the `source` property. |
| #### [tile](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-properties-tile)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) ?` | The tile being loaded or changed, if the event has a `dataType` of `source` and the event is related to loading of a tile. |
| #### [type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-properties-type)<br><br>``(`"data"` \| `"dataloading"` \| `"styledata"` \| `"styledataloading"` \| `"sourcedata"` \| `"sourcedataloading"`)`` | The type of originating event. For a full list of available events, see [`Map` events](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)<br> . |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-example)

    // Example of a MapDataEvent of type "sourcedata"map.on('sourcedata', (e) => {    console.log(e);    // {    //   dataType: "source",    //   isSourceLoaded: false,    //   source: {    //     type: "vector",    //     url: "mapbox://mapbox.mapbox-streets-v8,mapbox.mapbox-terrain-v2"    //   },    //   sourceDataType: "visibility",    //   sourceId: "composite",    //   style: {...},    //   target: {...},    //   type: "sourcedata"    // }});

### [Related](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapdataevent-related)

*   [Reference: `Map` events API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)
    
*   [Example: Change a map's style](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/setstyle/)
    
*   [Example: Add a GeoJSON line](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/geojson-line/)
    

Was this section on MapDataEvent helpful?YesNo

[MapMouseEvent](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapmouseevent)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/events.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/events.ts#L65-L164)

`MapMouseEvent` is a class used by other classes to generate mouse events of specific types such as 'click' or 'hover'. For a full list of available events, see [`Map` events](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-events)
.

Extends [Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object)
.

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#mapmouseevent-example)

    // Example of a MapMouseEvent of type "click"map.on('click', (e) => {    console.log(e);    // {    //     lngLat: {    //         lng: 40.203,    //         lat: -74.451    //     },    //     originalEvent: {...},    //     point: {    //         x: 266,    //         y: 464    //     },    //      target: {...},    //      type: "click"    // }});

# Snow | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/snow/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

A style's `snow` property is a global styling effect that can be used to add animated snow to the map. This property can be used to create a winter scene or match a designer's aesthetic. Snow provides a visual cue to improve the appearance of a map and can be used to create a festive or more true-to-life atmosphere.

[EXAMPLE\
\
Add a snow effect to a map\
\
See what a style using the `snow` property looks like in a web browser with Mapbox GL JS.](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/snow)
[PLAYGROUND\
\
Rain and Snow Playground\
\
The rain and snow playground allows you to test these style values in browser, offering UI to adjust the particle density, intensity, color, speed, etc.](https://docs.mapbox.com/playground/rain-and-snow/)

    "snow": {  "density": 0.85,  "intensity": 1.0,  "center-thinning": 0.1,  "direction": [0, 50],  "opacity": 1.0,  "color": "#ffffff",  "flake-size": 0.71,  "vignette": 0.3,  "vignette-color": "#ffffff"}

center-thinning[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/snow/#center-thinning "Direct link to center-thinning")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `0.4`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Thinning factor of snow particles from center. 0 - no thinning. 1 - maximal central area thinning.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

color[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/snow/#color "Direct link to color")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [color](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#color)
 . Defaults to `"#ffffff"`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Snow particles color.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

density[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/snow/#density "Direct link to density")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `["interpolate",["linear"],["zoom"],11,0,13,0.85]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Snow particles density. Controls the overall particles number.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

direction[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/snow/#direction "Direct link to direction")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
 of [numbers](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number) between `0` and `360` inclusive. Defaults to `[0,50]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Main snow particles direction. Azimuth and polar angles

    [  0,  45]

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

flake-size[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/snow/#flake-size "Direct link to flake-size")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `5` inclusive. Defaults to `0.71`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Snow flake particle size. Correlates with individual particle screen size

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

intensity[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/snow/#intensity "Direct link to intensity")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `1`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Snow particles movement factor. Controls the overall particles movement speed.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

opacity[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/snow/#opacity "Direct link to opacity")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `1`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Snow particles opacity.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

vignette[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/snow/#vignette "Direct link to vignette")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EXPERIMENTAL

Optional [number](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number)
 between `0` and `1` inclusive. Defaults to `["interpolate",["linear"],["zoom"],11,0,13,0.3]`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)
expressions. opacityTransitionable.

Snow vignette screen-space effect. Adds snow tint to screen corners

| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 3.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 | \>= 11.9.0 |

 . Defaults to `"#ffffff"`. Supports [smooth-ramp`interpolate`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#interpolate)

# Projection | Mapbox Style Spec | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/projection/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search Style Specification

A style's `projection` property sets which [projection](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/projection/)
 a map is rendered in. Mapbox GL supports the following projections:

*   **Globe**: A 3D representation of the Earth.
    *   [`globe`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/projections/#globe)
        
*   **Equal-area** projections: Relative size of regions is accurate, but shapes are distorted.
    *   [`equalEarth`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/projections/#equal-earth)
        
    *   [`albers`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/projections/#albers)
        
*   **Conformal** projections: Shapes and angles are accurate, but sizes are distorted.
    *   [`mercator`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/projections/#mercator)
        
    *   [`lambertConformalConic`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/projections/#lambert-conformal-conic)
        
*   **Compromise** projections: A balance of size and shape distortion.
    *   [`winkelTripel`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/projections/#winkel-tripel)
        
    *   [`naturalEarth`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/projections/#natural-earth)
        
    *   [`equirectangular`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/projections/#equirectangular)
        

Learn more about supported projections in the [projection guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/guides/projections/)
.

name[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/projection/#name "Direct link to name")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Required [enum](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#enum)
 . One of `"albers"`, `"equalEarth"`, `"equirectangular"`, `"lambertConformalConic"`, `"mercator"`, `"naturalEarth"`, `"winkelTripel"`, `"globe"`. Defaults to `"mercator"`.

The name of the projection to be used for rendering the map.

`"albers"`:

An Albers equal-area projection centered on the continental United States. You can configure the projection for a different region by setting `center` and `parallels` properties. You may want to set max bounds to constrain the map to the relevant region.

`"equalEarth"`:

An Equal Earth projection.

`"equirectangular"`:

An Equirectangular projection. This projection is very similar to the Plate Carrée projection.

`"lambertConformalConic"`:

A Lambert conformal conic projection. You can configure the projection for a region by setting `center` and `parallels` properties. You may want to set max bounds to constrain the map to the relevant region.

`"mercator"`:

The Mercator projection is the default projection.

`"naturalEarth"`:

A Natural Earth projection.

`"winkelTripel"`:

A Winkel Tripel projection.

`"globe"`:

A globe projection.

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 2.6.0 | \>= 10.5.0 | \>= 10.5.0 |

center[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/projection/#center "Direct link to center")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Optional [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
 of [numbers](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number) \[ between `-180` and `180` inclusive, between `-90` and `90` inclusive \]. _Requires_ name to be `"albers"`, or `"lambertConformalConic"`.

The reference longitude and latitude of the projection. `center` takes the form of \[lng, lat\]. This property is only configurable for conic projections (Albers and Lambert Conformal Conic). All other projections are centered on \[0, 0\].

    [  -96,  37.5]

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 2.6.0 | Not yet supported | Not yet supported |

parallels[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/projection/#parallels "Direct link to parallels")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Optional [array](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#array)
 of [numbers](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/types/#number) \[ between `-90` and `90` inclusive, between `-90` and `90` inclusive \]. _Requires_ name to be `"albers"`, or `"lambertConformalConic"`.

The standard parallels of the projection, denoting the desired latitude range with minimal distortion. `parallels` takes the form of \[lat0, lat1\]. This property is only configurable for conic projections (Albers and Lambert Conformal Conic).

    [  29.5,  45.5]

| SDK Support | Mapbox GL JS | Android SDK | iOS SDK |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| basic functionality | \>= 2.6.0 | Not yet supported | Not yet supported |

Was this page helpful?YesNo

---

# Pricing | Mapbox Tiling Service | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Tileset Pricing

This pricing guide applies to both the [Mapbox Tiling Service (MTS)](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/)
 and the [Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/uploads/)
. Both APIs use the same pricing outlined in this guide. Mapbox Studio is powered by the Uploads API, but is subject to usage restrictions and alternative pricing. See the [Mapbox Studio pricing guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/studio-manual/ja/reference/tilesets/#pricing)
 for more details.

*   Billed by **tileset processing and tileset hosting**
*   See rates and discounts per tileset processing and tileset hosting in the pricing page's **[Tilesets](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#tilesets)
    ** section

Tilesets pricing is billed monthly based on the number of processing jobs and hosted [tilesets](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/tileset/)
 in your account. For processing jobs, you are billed for the processing resources each job consumes, including processed file size (in megabytes) and processed compute units (CUs).

Tilesets processing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#tilesets-processing "Tilesets processing への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Tileset processing is a one-time cost incurred when you [publish an MTS job](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#publish-a-tileset)
 or [create an upload with the Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#create-an-upload)
. You are only billed for successfully published [tilesets](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/tileset/)
 or successfully created uploads.

Each tilesets processing job is billed in two ways: file size and compute units (or CUs).

### File size[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#file-size "File size への直接リンク")

For each processing job, you are billed for the file size of your processed data, which is measured in megabytes (MB). Processed MB will appear on your invoice as **Tilesets Processing File Size**.

For MTS jobs, this is the file size of the [tileset source](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/tileset-sources/)
 of your job. For the [Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/uploads/)
, this is the file size of the uploaded source file.

For MTS, uploading data to a tileset source or [changeset](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/incremental-updates/#changesets)
 is free. You are not billed for processing until you publish a job for a tileset.

### Compute units (CUs)[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#compute-units-cus "Compute units (CUs) への直接リンク")

Compute units (also called CUs) represent the processing resources a tilesets processing job from MTS or the Uploads API consumes on Mapbox infrastructure. For example, a tileset that uses 4.50 CUs consumes roughly three times more compute than a 1.50 CU tileset. Processed CUs will appear on your invoice as **Tilesets Processing CU**.

Most vector tileset processing jobs range from 0.3 CUs to 5 CUs. Large complex tilesets require more CUs than small simple tilesets. For example, a [zoom level](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/zoom-level/)
 0-16 tileset covering a large country with thousands of large polygon features and a 50 GB tileset source uses more CUs than a [zoom level](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/zoom-level/)
 10-11 tileset covering a single city with a hundred point features and a 10 MB source file size.

Tilesets hosting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#tilesets-hosting "Tilesets hosting への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Unlike tileset processing, tileset hosting is a recurring charge. You are billed on a monthly basis for the number of tilesets hosted in your account. Each day that you host a tileset counts as a tileset hosting day. For example, 3 tilesets hosted for 15 days is 45 tileset hosting days (3 tilesets \* 15 days). Tileset hosting charges will appear on your invoice as **Tileset Hosting Days**.

The file size of hosted [tilesets](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/tileset/)
 does not contribute to any hosting costs. You are only billed for the number of tileset hosting days per month. Tilesets created before October 1, 2020, are exempt from tileset hosting billing.

The table below shows how average monthly hosted tilesets convert to tileset hosting days. This table assumes a 30-day month. It also assumes you did not create or delete any additional tilesets during the 30-day period.

| Average monthly hosted tilesets | Tileset hosting days |
| --- | --- |
| 5   | 150 |
| 10  | 300 |
| 50  | 1,500 |
| 100 | 3,000 |
| 1,000 | 30,000 |
| 5,000 | 150,000 |
| 10,000 | 300,000 |
| 100,000 | 3,000,000 |

Viewing usage in the Tileset Explorer[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#viewing-usage-in-the-tileset-explorer "Viewing usage in the Tileset Explorer への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can view the processing billing metrics (CUs and file size) for your successfully published or processed tilesets in the Tileset Explorer. To access the Tileset Explorer, go to your [Mapbox Studio Tilesets](https://console.mapbox.com/studio/tilesets/)
 page and click on a tileset's name.

Once the Explorer has opened, the _Billing metrics_ graph will appear in the bottom left corner of the page. This graph shows the CUs and file size of the latest processing event for the tileset.

For MTS jobs, you can also view the past CU and file size values for previous MTS jobs in the job history panel. To open the job history panel, click on _Job history_ in the top right-hand corner when viewing a tileset in the Tileset Explorer.

Tracking usage in Statistics[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#tracking-usage-in-statistics "Tracking usage in Statistics への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can track your tilesets usage in your account's [Statistics page](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/statistics/)
. There are three products for tilesets usage on the Statistics page: **Tileset Processing CU**, **Tileset Processing File Size**, and **Tileset Hosting Days**. For **Tileset Hosting Days**, the number of tileset hosting days for a given day is the same as the number of tilesets hosted in your account for that day. For example, this account hosted 21 tilesets on July 2.

Understanding your invoice[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#understanding-your-invoice "Understanding your invoice への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you submitted tileset processing jobs that month, you should see three line items for tilesets usage on your monthly invoice:

*   Tilesets Processing File Size
*   Tilesets Processing CU
*   Tileset Hosting Days

If you did not submit any tileset processing jobs that month but are still hosting tilesets, your invoice will only have a single line item for **Tileset Hosting Days**.

Estimating costs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#estimating-costs "Estimating costs への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Estimating processing costs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#estimating-processing-costs "Estimating processing costs への直接リンク")

Your number of monthly processing jobs (job frequency) has the largest impact on processing costs. To estimate your processing costs, first determine how many [tilesets](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/tileset/)
 you need to create each month and the number of monthly updates (job frequency) for each tileset. Second, multiply the number of monthly jobs by the estimated file size in MB per job and estimated compute units (CUs) per job.

For example, one tileset with 5,000 jobs per month at 0.5 MB and 1.5 CUs is 2,500 MB and 7,500 CUs per month. 5,000 jobs times 0.5 MB per job is 2,500 total MB. 5,000 jobs times 1.5 CUs per job is 7,500 CUs.

To calculate processing costs, enter the number of monthly MB and CUs into the calculator for [tileset processing](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#tilesets)
 on the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/)
.

#### Estimating job frequency[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#estimating-job-frequency "Estimating job frequency への直接リンク")

The table below converts common job frequencies (e.g., updates every 5 minutes, every hour, etc.) to monthly jobs. This table assumes a 30-day month.

| Job frequency | Monthly jobs |
| --- | --- |
| 1 minute | 43,200 |
| 5 minutes | 8,640 |
| 15 minutes | 2,880 |
| 30 minutes | 1,440 |
| 1 hour | 720 |
| 3 hours | 240 |
| 6 hours | 120 |
| 12 hours | 60  |
| 1 day | 30  |
| 1 week | 4   |
| 1 month | 1   |

#### Estimating file size[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#estimating-file-size "Estimating file size への直接リンク")

For MTS jobs, you can check the file size for a tileset source using the [`view-source` command](https://github.com/mapbox/tilesets-cli#view-source)
 of the Tilesets CLI or the [Retrieve tileset source information endpoint](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#retrieve-tileset-source-information)
 of the MTS API.

For the [Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/uploads/)
 jobs, use the file size of the uploaded source file.

#### Estimating compute units[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#estimating-compute-units "Estimating compute units への直接リンク")

The most accurate method to estimate CUs is to submit a single test job for a new tileset workload and review the CU values for that job. You can also review CUs for existing or previous similar jobs in your account. If you cannot submit a new test job or do not have previous tilesets jobs, you can use average CU values.

99% of tileset jobs, whether on MTS or the Uploads API, are 5 CUs or less. The average CU value for all tileset jobs on the Mapbox platform ranges from 0.90 to 1.00 CUs. Raster tileset job CUs vary based on the source data resolution, number of bands, and geographic extent. You can estimate the CUs for raster jobs using the [estimate-cu function of the Tilesets CLI](https://github.com/mapbox/tilesets-cli?tab=readme-ov-file#estimate-cu)
.

File size and [zoom levels](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/zoom-level/)
 have the largest impact on CUs, with MTS recipe features having only a small impact. The larger the file size and the more zoom levels you tile, the more CUs a job will use. Jobs that use high zoom levels (11+) will generally use more CUs than jobs that use low zoom levels (10 or less).

The tables below show how file size and maximum zoom level typically affect CU values for jobs. These tables do not guarantee CU values, and your results may differ.

##### Maximum zoom level 10 or less[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#maximum-zoom-level-10-or-less "Maximum zoom level 10 or less への直接リンク")

| File size | CU range |
| --- | --- |
| < 1 GB | < 5 |
| 1 - 5 GB | 5 - 30 |
| \> 5 GB | 30+ |

##### Maximum zoom level 11 or greater[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#maximum-zoom-level-11-or-greater "Maximum zoom level 11 or greater への直接リンク")

| File size | CU range |
| --- | --- |
| < 200 MB | < 5 |
| 200 MB - 5 GB | 5 - 30 |
| \> 5 GB | 30+ |

### Estimating hosting costs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#estimating-hosting-costs "Estimating hosting costs への直接リンク")

To estimate your hosting costs, determine how many tilesets you need to host per month and how many days per month you need to host each tileset. To calculate tileset hosting days, multiply the number of tilesets times the days per month you host each tileset. For example, if you create 10 tilesets every day and delete each tileset after 7 days, that is 70 tileset hosting days.

To calculate hosting costs, enter the number of monthly tileset hosting days into the calculator for [tileset hosting](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/#tilesets)
 on the [pricing page](https://www.mapbox.com/pricing/)
.

Managing costs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#managing-costs "Managing costs への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Managing processing costs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#managing-processing-costs "Managing processing costs への直接リンク")

Tileset processing is a one-time cost incurred when you [publish an MTS job](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#publish-a-tileset)
 or [create an upload with the Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#create-an-upload)
. You cannot lower processing costs associated with any tilesets you have already published with MTS or uploaded with the Uploads API.

In general, being efficient and intentional with your tilesets processing jobs will reduce your costs. Only submit the minimum number of processing jobs you need and the minimum amount of data for your use case.

The following tips can help you reduce processing costs for future tilesets:

*   Reduce job frequency
*   Clean up source data
*   Reduce [zoom levels](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/zoom-level/)
    
*   Use [MTS recipes](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/tileset-recipes/)
    
*   Use [incremental updates](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/incremental-updates/)
     (large tilesets only)

#### Reduce job frequency[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#reduce-job-frequency "Reduce job frequency への直接リンク")

Reducing job frequency will have the largest impact on decreasing your monthly processing costs. For example, switching from updates every 5 minutes (8,640 monthly jobs) to updates every 15 minutes (2,880 monthly jobs) will reduce your costs by 66%.

Another way to reduce job frequency is to only update your tileset when geometries change. If most of your updates affect your data's attributes rather than their geometries, you may be able to leave the volatile attributes out of the tileset and instead join them on the client side. This way, you can update your data's attributes without updating the whole tileset.

For example, consider a state-level unemployment dataset: the state geometries rarely change, while the unemployment numbers change often. A cost-effective strategy would be to create a static state boundaries tileset and then join the unemployment data dynamically on the client. See the [Data Joins with Mapbox Boundaries tutorial](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/tutorials/data-joins-with-mapbox-boundaries/)
 for more information on how to use this data-join technique.

#### Clean up source data[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#clean-up-source-data "Clean up source data への直接リンク")

The cleaner and more efficient your source data, the lower your processing costs. The amount of data you process roughly correlates with file size. File size has the second greatest impact on your processing costs after job frequency.

The following tips can help you clean up your source data:

*   Limit latitude and longitude coordinates in your source dataset to 6 decimal points (10cm precision) or less using a tool like [geojson-precision](https://github.com/perrygeo/geojson-precision)
    
*   Remove unnecessary layers from your tileset
*   Remove unnecessary features from your source dataset
*   Remove unnecessary feature attributes from your source dataset
*   Reduce feature attribute size (e.g., shorter strings)

#### Reduce zoom levels[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#reduce-zoom-levels "Reduce zoom levels への直接リンク")

The fewer [zoom levels](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/zoom-level/)
 your tileset uses, the lower the CUs for your job and the lower your processing costs. Only tile the zoom levels needed for your use case. For example, a building footprint use case likely does not need zoom levels 0-11, so you could tile your data from zoom levels 12 through 15, and rely on [overzooming](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/overzoom/)
 for zoom levels 16 and higher.

Overzooming and the maxzoom value

The `maxzoom` value does not determine which zoom level a map zooms to. Your map will still zoom past the `maxzoom` specified in your tileset recipe because of [overzooming](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/overzoom/)
.

The maximum zoom level of your tileset has a higher impact on CUs than the minimum zoom level. Maximum zoom levels of 11 or higher have a large impact on increasing CUs, with maximum zoom levels of 14 or higher having an even larger impact. To reduce costs, stick to zoom level 10 (10-meter precision) or less for your tileset processing jobs.

Uploads API and maximum zoom level

You cannot specify the maximum zoom level of a tileset when using the [Uploads API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/#uploads)
 or uploading data using [Mapbox Studio](https://console.mapbox.com/studio/)
. The Uploads API guesses what zoom levels your tileset needs, based on the data you upload. We recommend using MTS over the Uploads API so that you have more control over your tilesets, and, ultimately, your costs.

#### Use MTS recipes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#use-mts-recipes "Use MTS recipes への直接リンク")

Certain [MTS recipe](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/tileset-recipes/)
 configurations may increase or decrease CUs for your processing jobs. MTS recipe configurations have a smaller impact on CUs than cleaning up source data and reducing zoom levels. Certain MTS recipe configurations, like [`simplification`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#feature-simplification)
 may either increase or decrease CUs depending on how they are used.

See the [MTS recipe specification](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/)
 for detailed information on each recipe configuration.

| Increases CUs | Decreases CUs |
| --- | --- |
| [`buffer_size`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#buffer-size) | [`bbox`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#bounding-box) |
| [`distance`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#simplification-distance) | [`filter`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#feature-filters) |
| [`simplification`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#feature-simplification)<br> (lower values) | [`simplification`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#feature-simplification)<br> (higher values) |
| [`highest_where`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#limit) | [`limit`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#limit) |
| [`highest_where_in_distance`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#limit) | [`remove_filled`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#remove-filled) |
| [`lowest_where`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#limit) | [`union`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#feature-union) |
| [`lowest_where_in_distance`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#limit) | [`zoom_element`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#zoom-element) |
| [`order`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#order) |     |
| [`outward_only`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#outward-only-simplification) |     |
| [`union`](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/recipe-specification/vector/#feature-union) |     |

#### Use incremental updates[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#use-incremental-updates "Use incremental updates への直接リンク")

[Incremental updates](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/incremental-updates/)
 enable updating and re-processing part of an existing tileset instead of updating and re-processing the entire tileset. For example, if your tileset contains 1,000 points and only 1 point changed recently, you can use MTS incremental updates to update and re-process only that 1 updated point. Without incremental updates, MTS will re-process and re-tile all 1,000 points in the tileset, even the points that did not change.

Like regular MTS jobs, incremental update jobs are billed for the processing resources each job consumes, including processed file size (in megabytes) and processed compute units (CUs). Processed MB and CUs for incremental update jobs do not show up separately in the account statistics page or in your invoice. Instead, MB and CUs for all job types are combined into a single number in both account statistics and your invoice.

Certain large tilesets may see cost benefits from using incremental updates. These tilesets include datasets over 1 GB or a maximum zoom level of 11 or higher. Smaller tilesets may see little to no cost benefits from incremental updates.

Not all use cases are eligible for incremental updates. Incremental updates is only available for vector tilesets created with [Vector MTS](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/vector/)
. Additionally, stable unique [feature IDs](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/incremental-updates/#feature-ids)
 are required to use incremental updates.

### Managing hosting costs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#managing-hosting-costs "Managing hosting costs への直接リンク")

The following tips can reduce your tileset hosting costs:

*   Delete hosted tilesets after they are no longer needed.
*   Periodically delete inactive or unused hosted tilesets.
*   Where possible, reuse existing [tileset IDs](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/tileset-id/)
     instead of creating new unique tilesets for each processing job.

#### Identifying inactive hosted tilesets in Studio[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#identifying-inactive-hosted-tilesets-in-studio "Identifying inactive hosted tilesets in Studio への直接リンク")

The _Tileset hosting_ sidebar in [Mapbox Studio Tilesets](https://console.mapbox.com/studio/tilesets/)
 page can help you identify inactive tilesets for deletion. This sidebar shows the active and inactive tilesets for your account in the past 30 days. An active tileset had at least 1 API request in the past 30 days.

You can also view the number of API requests in the past 30 days for an individual tileset in the [Mapbox Studio Tilesets](https://console.mapbox.com/studio/tilesets/)
 page.

#### Deleting hosted tilesets[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/ja/guides/pricing/#deleting-hosted-tilesets "Deleting hosted tilesets への直接リンク")

Deleting tilesets

You cannot restore any deleted tilesets so exercise caution when deleting tilesets.

You can delete tilesets in your account using the [Mapbox Studio Tilesets](https://console.mapbox.com/studio/tilesets/)
 page, the [`delete` command](https://github.com/mapbox/tilesets-cli#delete)
 in the [Tilesets CLI](https://github.com/mapbox/tilesets-cli)
, or the [Delete tileset](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/maps/mapbox-tiling-service/#delete-tileset)
 endpoint in the MTS API.

You can delete up to 25 tilesets at a time in Mapbox Studio. You can only delete tilesets one at a time via the [Tilesets CLI](https://github.com/mapbox/tilesets-cli)
 or the delete tileset endpoint in the MTS API.

You will receive a warning before deleting any tilesets. You must confirm deletion before any tilesets are deleted. You can use the `--force` flag in the [Tilesets CLI](https://github.com/mapbox/tilesets-cli)
 to override the warning for scripting and automation.

このpageは役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# Sources | Mapbox GL JS | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search GL JS API Reference

A [`source`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/sources/)
 defines data the map should display. This reference lists the source types Mapbox GL JS can handle in addition to the ones described in the [Mapbox Style Specification](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/)
.

[CanvasSource](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#canvassource)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/source/canvas\_source.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/source/canvas_source.ts#L62-L238)

A data source containing the contents of an HTML canvas. See [CanvasSourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#canvassourceoptions)
 for detailed documentation of options.

Extends [ImageSource](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#imagesource)
.

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#canvassource-example)

    // add to mapmap.addSource('some id', {    type: 'canvas',    canvas: 'idOfMyHTMLCanvas',    animate: true,    coordinates: [        [-76.54, 39.18],        [-76.52, 39.18],        [-76.52, 39.17],        [-76.54, 39.17]    ]});// updateconst mySource = map.getSource('some id');mySource.setCoordinates([    [-76.54335737228394, 39.18579907229748],    [-76.52803659439087, 39.1838364847587],    [-76.5295386314392, 39.17683392507606],    [-76.54520273208618, 39.17876344106642]]);map.removeSource('some id');  // remove

### [Instance Members](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#canvassource-instance-members)

#### play

()

caret-right

#### pause

()

caret-right

#### getCanvas

()

caret-right

#### setCoordinates

()

caret-right

*   [Example: Add a canvas source](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/canvas-source/)
    

Was this section on CanvasSource helpful?YesNo

[CanvasSourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#canvassourceoptions)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/source/canvas\_source.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/source/canvas_source.ts#L17-L25)

Options to add a canvas source type to the map.

### [Type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#canvassourceoptions-type)

`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) `

### [Properties](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#canvassourceoptions-properties)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [animate](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#canvassourceoptions-properties-animate)<br><br>`[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean) ?` | Whether the canvas source is animated. If the canvas is static (pixels do not need to be re-read on every frame), `animate` should be set to `false` to improve performance. |
| #### [canvas](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#canvassourceoptions-properties-canvas)<br><br>`([string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String)  \| [HTMLCanvasElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/HTMLCanvasElement) )` | Canvas source from which to read pixels. Can be a string representing the ID of the canvas element, or the `HTMLCanvasElement` itself. |
| #### [coordinates](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#canvassourceoptions-properties-coordinates)<br><br>`[Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array) <[Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array) <[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) >>` | Four geographical coordinates denoting where to place the corners of the canvas, specified in `[longitude, latitude]` pairs. |
| #### [type](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#canvassourceoptions-properties-type)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ` | Source type. Must be `"canvas"` . |

Was this section on CanvasSourceOptions helpful?YesNo

[GeoJSONSource](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#geojsonsource)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/source/geojson\_source.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/source/geojson_source.ts#L73-L524)

A source containing GeoJSON. See the [Style Specification](https://www.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-style-spec/#sources-geojson)
 for detailed documentation of options.

Extends [Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)
.

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#geojsonsource-example)

    map.addSource('some id', {    type: 'geojson',    data: 'https://d2ad6b4ur7yvpq.cloudfront.net/naturalearth-3.3.0/ne_10m_ports.geojson'});

    map.addSource('some id', {    type: 'geojson',    data: {        "type": "FeatureCollection",        "features": [{            "type": "Feature",            "properties": {},            "geometry": {                "type": "Point",                "coordinates": [                    -76.53063297271729,                    39.18174077994108                ]            }        }]    }});

    map.getSource('some id').setData({    "type": "FeatureCollection",    "features": [{        "type": "Feature",        "properties": {"name": "Null Island"},        "geometry": {            "type": "Point",            "coordinates": [ 0, 0 ]        }    }]});

[ImageSource](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#imagesource)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/source/image\_source.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/source/image_source.ts#L218-L830)

A data source containing an image. See the [Style Specification](https://www.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-style-spec/#sources-image)
 for detailed documentation of options.

Extends [Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)
.

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#imagesource-example)

    // add to mapmap.addSource('some id', {    type: 'image',    url: 'https://www.mapbox.com/images/foo.png',    coordinates: [        [-76.54, 39.18],        [-76.52, 39.18],        [-76.52, 39.17],        [-76.54, 39.17]    ]});// update coordinatesconst mySource = map.getSource('some id');mySource.setCoordinates([    [-76.54335737228394, 39.18579907229748],    [-76.52803659439087, 39.1838364847587],    [-76.5295386314392, 39.17683392507606],    [-76.54520273208618, 39.17876344106642]]);// update url and coordinates simultaneouslymySource.updateImage({    url: 'https://www.mapbox.com/images/bar.png',    coordinates: [        [-76.54335737228394, 39.18579907229748],        [-76.52803659439087, 39.1838364847587],        [-76.5295386314392, 39.17683392507606],        [-76.54520273208618, 39.17876344106642]    ]});map.removeSource('some id');  // remove
