[ModelSource](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#modelsource)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[github3d-style/source/model\_source.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/3d-style/source/model_source.ts#L50-L306)

A source containing single models. See the [Style Specification](https://www.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-style-spec/#sources-model)
 for detailed documentation of options.

Extends [Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)
.

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#modelsource-example)

    map.addSource('some id', {  "type": "model",  "models": {    "ego-car" : {         "uri": "car.glb",         "position": [-74.0135, 40.7153],         "orientation": [0, 0, 0],         "materialOverrides": {           "body": {             "model-color": [0.00775, 0.03458, 0.43854],             "model-color-mix-intensity": 1.0           }         },         "nodeOverrides": {           "doors_front-left": {             "orientation": [0.0, -45.0, 0.0]           }         }     }  }});

[RasterArrayTileSource](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rasterarraytilesource)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/source/raster\_array\_tile\_source.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/source/raster_array_tile_source.ts#L54-L434)

A data source containing raster-array tiles created with [Mapbox Tiling Service](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-tiling-service/guides/)
. See the [Style Specification](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/sources/#raster-array)
 for detailed documentation of options.

Extends [RasterTileSource](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rastertilesource)
.

new RasterArrayTileSource(id: [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String)
, options: [RasterArraySourceSpecification](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rasterarraysourcespecification)
, dispatcher: Dispatcher, eventedParent: [Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)
)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rasterarraytilesource-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [id](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rasterarraytilesource-parameters-id)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ` |     |
| #### [options](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rasterarraytilesource-parameters-options)<br><br>`[RasterArraySourceSpecification](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rasterarraysourcespecification) ` |     |
| #### [dispatcher](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rasterarraytilesource-parameters-dispatcher)<br><br>`Dispatcher` |     |
| #### [eventedParent](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rasterarraytilesource-parameters-eventedparent)<br><br>`[Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented) ` |     |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rasterarraytilesource-example)

    // add to mapmap.addSource('some id', {    type: 'raster-array',    url: 'mapbox://rasterarrayexamples.gfs-winds',    tileSize: 512});

[RasterTileSource](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rastertilesource)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/source/raster\_tile\_source.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/source/raster_tile_source.ts#L46-L270)

A source containing raster tiles. See the [Style Specification](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/style-spec/sources/#raster)
 for detailed documentation of options.

Extends [Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)
.

new RasterTileSource(id: [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String)
, options: (RasterSourceSpecification | RasterDEMSourceSpecification | [RasterArraySourceSpecification](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rasterarraysourcespecification)
), dispatcher: Dispatcher, eventedParent: [Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)
)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rastertilesource-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [id](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rastertilesource-parameters-id)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ` |     |
| #### [options](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rastertilesource-parameters-options)<br><br>`(RasterSourceSpecification \| RasterDEMSourceSpecification \| [RasterArraySourceSpecification](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rasterarraysourcespecification) )` |     |
| #### [dispatcher](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rastertilesource-parameters-dispatcher)<br><br>`Dispatcher` |     |
| #### [eventedParent](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rastertilesource-parameters-eventedparent)<br><br>`[Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented) ` |     |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#rastertilesource-example)

    map.addSource('some id', {    type: 'raster',    url: 'mapbox://mapbox.satellite',    tileSize: 256});

    map.addSource('some id', {    type: 'raster',    tiles: ['https://img.nj.gov/imagerywms/Natural2015?bbox={bbox-epsg-3857}&format=image/png&service=WMS&version=1.1.1&request=GetMap&srs=EPSG:3857&transparent=true&width=256&height=256&layers=Natural2015'],    tileSize: 256});

[VectorTileSource](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#vectortilesource)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/source/vector\_tile\_source.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/source/vector_tile_source.ts#L51-L383)

A source containing vector tiles in [Mapbox Vector Tile format](https://docs.mapbox.com/vector-tiles/reference/)
. See the [Style Specification](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/style-spec/sources/#vector)
 for detailed documentation of options.

Extends [Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)
.

new VectorTileSource(id: [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String)
, options: any, dispatcher: Dispatcher, eventedParent: [Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)
)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#vectortilesource-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [id](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#vectortilesource-parameters-id)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ` |     |
| #### [options](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#vectortilesource-parameters-options)<br><br>`any` |     |
| #### [dispatcher](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#vectortilesource-parameters-dispatcher)<br><br>`Dispatcher` |     |
| #### [eventedParent](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#vectortilesource-parameters-eventedparent)<br><br>`[Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented) ` |     |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/sources/#vectortilesource-example)

    map.addSource('some id', {    type: 'vector',    url: 'mapbox://mapbox.mapbox-streets-v8'});

    map.addSource('some id', {    type: 'vector',    tiles: ['https://d25uarhxywzl1j.cloudfront.net/v0.1/{z}/{x}/{y}.mvt'],    minzoom: 6,    maxzoom: 14});

    map.getSource('some id').setUrl("mapbox://mapbox.mapbox-streets-v8");

    map.getSource('some id').setTiles(['https://d25uarhxywzl1j.cloudfront.net/v0.1/{z}/{x}/{y}.mvt']);

# Maps SDK v9 | Android Docs | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/legacy/maps/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Maps SDK for Android v9
=======================

現在のバージョン：`v9.7.1`[変更履歴を表示](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-android/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md)

*   check
    
    Map styles
    
*   check
    
    Run-time styling
    
*   check
    
    Data clusters
    
*   check
    
    Camera manipulation
    
*   check
    
    Querying the map
    
*   check
    
    Gestures
    
*   check
    
    Map images
    

[githubGithubで貢献](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-android)

A newer version of the Maps SDK is available

This page uses v9.7.1 of the Mapbox Maps SDK. A newer version of the SDK is available and is recommended for all users. SDK versions before v10 are no longer receiving updates, including compatibility and bug fixes. Learn about the latest version, v11.17.1, in the [Maps SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/maps/guides/)
.

The Mapbox Maps SDK for Android is a toolset for displaying maps inside of your Android application.

[](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.mapbox.mapboxandroiddemo&pcampaignid=MKT-Other-global-all-co-prtnr-py-PartBadge-Mar2515-1)

[Mapbox's demo app on the Google Play Store](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.mapbox.mapboxandroiddemo&hl=en)
 includes many examples of how to use the Mapbox Maps SDK for Android. The demo app and [examples page](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/legacy/maps/examples/)
 will illustrate the power of the Mapbox Maps SDK for Android.

Upgrade to the most recent version

For information about migrating from older versions of the Mapbox Maps SDK for Android to newer ones, see:- **v8.x.x to v9.x.x**: Applications implementing this version of the Maps SDK must support [AndroidX](https://developer.android.com/jetpack/androidx)
.- **v7.x.x to v8.x.x**: This version change improved the method for counting monthly active users. No migration guide is required, but you can read more about these changes in the [changelog](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native/releases/tag/android-v8.0.0)
 and the [Pricing by products](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/account/pricing/#maps-sdk-for-ios-and-android)
 guide.- [**v6.x.x to v7.x.x**](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native/wiki/Android-6.x-to-7.x-migration-guide)
\- [**v5.x.x to v6.x.x**](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native/wiki/Android-5.x-to-6.x-migration-guide)
\- [**v4.x.x to v5.x.x**](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native/wiki/Android-4.x-to-5.x-migration-guide)

Attribution[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/legacy/maps/guides/#attribution "Attribution への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You must include the Mapbox wordmark and attribution notice on any map that uses the Mapbox Maps SDK for Android. The SDK provides an attribution layout that includes all required information and can be customized either in xml or using the `UiSettings` object.

[You may adjust the position of the Mapbox wordmark and attribution notice](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/legacy/maps/guides/ui-settings/#logo-and-attribution)
, but they must stay visible on the map. You may also change the background and text color of the attribution notice to match your design aesthetics, but all information must be legible.

You may not otherwise alter the Mapbox wordmark or text attribution notice.

[GUIDE\
\
Attribution guidelines\
\
Learn more about what kinds of attribution Mapbox requires and why.](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/how-mapbox-works/attribution/)

Telemetry opt out[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/legacy/maps/guides/#telemetry-opt-out "Telemetry opt out への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mapbox Telemetry is a [powerful location analytics platform](https://www.mapbox.com/telemetry/)
 included in this SDK. By default, the SDK sends de-identified location and usage data to Mapbox whenever the host app causes it to be gathered. The [Mapbox Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/tos/)
 require your app to provide users with a way to individually opt out of Mapbox Telemetry, which is provided automatically as part of the [attribution](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/legacy/maps/guides/#attribution)
 control. If you hide the attribution control, you must provide an alternative opt out for your users to use.

MapView XML attributes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/legacy/maps/guides/#mapview-xml-attributes "MapView XML attributes への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

XML attributes can be added inside of the XML `MapView` to further customize map behavior, such as setting the starting camera position, enabling tilt, or adjusting the compass' location on the screen. All `MapView` XML attributes start with `mapbox_` for identification and for removing any potential conflicts with other libraries. Due to the current implementation of Android Studio, you can't generate `MapView` attributes by typing. [View the full list of `MapView` attributes here](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-android/blob/master/MapboxGLAndroidSDK/src/main/res-public/values/public.xml)
.

Some examples of `MapView` attributes are:

    <com.mapbox.mapboxsdk.maps.MapView  mapbox:mapbox_cameraTargetLat="-36.84"  mapbox:mapbox_cameraTargetLng="174.76"  mapbox:mapbox_cameraZoom="10"  mapbox:mapbox_cameraBearing="34.33"  mapbox:mapbox_cameraTilt="50.25"  mapbox:mapbox_cameraZoomMax="12.41"  mapbox:mapbox_cameraZoomMin="6"  mapbox:mapbox_uiRotateGestures="false"/>

Add `xmlns:mapbox="http://schemas.android.com/tools"` to the XML file's root `View` or `ViewGroup` element if you use any `MapView` XML attribute.

Understanding GeoJSON[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/legacy/maps/guides/#understanding-geojson "Understanding GeoJSON への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Many parts of the Mapbox Maps SDK for Android are based on GeoJSON, an open standard file format for representing map data. Many Maps SDK methods take GeoJSON geometries as a parameter or return GeoJSON features. For example, the Maps SDK has [a `GeoJSONSource` class](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/legacy/maps/guides/data-driven-styling/#geojson)
 for adding data to a map style, and one helpful Maps SDK's [querying method](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/legacy/maps/guides/query/#query-rendered-features)
 returns a list of GeoJSON `Feature`s.

The [Mapbox Java SDK's GeoJSON module](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/java/guides/geojson/)
 provides GeoJSON support to the Maps SDK. Although it's not required to use the Maps SDK, becoming more familiar with GeoJSON will greatly enhance your understanding and usage of the Maps SDK. Make sure to read [how to use the Java SDK's GeoJSON module with the Maps SDK](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/java/guides/geojson/#use-with-the-maps-sdk)
.

---

# Markers and controls | Mapbox GL JS | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Search GL JS API Reference

User interface elements that can be added to the map. The items in this section exist outside of the map's `canvas` element.

[AttributionControl](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#attributioncontrol)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/control/attribution\_control.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/control/attribution_control.ts#L30-L208)

An `AttributionControl` control presents the map's [attribution information](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/how-mapbox-works/attribution/)
. Add this control to a map using [Map#addControl](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#addcontrol)
.

new AttributionControl(options: [Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object)
?)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#attributioncontrol-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [options](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#attributioncontrol-parameters-options)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) ?`(default `{}`) |     |
| #### [options.compact](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#attributioncontrol-parameters-options-compact)<br><br>`[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean) ?` | If `true` , force a compact attribution that shows the full attribution on mouse hover. If `false` , force the full attribution control. The default is a responsive attribution that collapses when the map is less than 640 pixels wide. **Attribution should not be collapsed if it can comfortably fit on the map. `compact` should only be used to modify default attribution when map size makes it impossible to fit [default attribution](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/how-mapbox-works/attribution/)<br> and when the automatic compact resizing for default settings are not sufficient** . |
| #### [options.customAttribution](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#attributioncontrol-parameters-options-customattribution)<br><br>`([string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String)  \| [Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array) <[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) >)?` | String or strings to show in addition to any other attributions. You can also set a custom attribution when initializing your map with [the customAttribution option](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map-parameters)<br> . |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#attributioncontrol-example)

    const map = new mapboxgl.Map({attributionControl: false})    .addControl(new mapboxgl.AttributionControl({        customAttribution: 'Map design by me'    }));

Was this section on AttributionControl helpful?YesNo

[FullscreenControl](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#fullscreencontrol)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/control/fullscreen\_control.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/control/fullscreen_control.ts#L32-L137)

A `FullscreenControl` control contains a button for toggling the map in and out of fullscreen mode. See the `requestFullScreen` [compatibility table](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/requestFullScreen#browser_compatibility)
 for supported browsers. Add this control to a map using [Map#addControl](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#addcontrol)
.

new FullscreenControl(options: [Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object)
?)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#fullscreencontrol-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [options](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#fullscreencontrol-parameters-options)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) ?`(default `{}`) |     |
| #### [options.container](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#fullscreencontrol-parameters-options-container)<br><br>`[HTMLElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTML/Element) ?` | `container` is the [compatible DOM element](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/requestFullScreen#Compatible_elements)<br> which should be made full screen. By default, the map container element will be made full screen. |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#fullscreencontrol-example)

    map.addControl(new mapboxgl.FullscreenControl({container: document.querySelector('body')}));

### [Related](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#fullscreencontrol-related)

*   [Example: View a fullscreen map](https://www.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/fullscreen/)
    

Was this section on FullscreenControl helpful?YesNo

[GeolocateControl](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/control/geolocate\_control.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/control/geolocate_control.ts#L96-L703)

A `GeolocateControl` control provides a button that uses the browser's geolocation API to locate the user on the map. Add this control to a map using [Map#addControl](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#addcontrol)
.

Not all browsers support geolocation, and some users may disable the feature. Geolocation support for modern browsers including Chrome requires sites to be served over HTTPS. If geolocation support is not available, the `GeolocateControl` will show as disabled.

The [zoom level](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/zoom-level/)
 applied depends on the accuracy of the geolocation provided by the device.

The GeolocateControl has two modes. If `trackUserLocation` is `false` (default) the control acts as a button, which when pressed will set the map's camera to target the user location. If the user moves, the map won't update. This is most suited for the desktop. If `trackUserLocation` is `true` the control acts as a toggle button that when active the user's location is actively monitored for changes. In this mode the `GeolocateControl` has three interaction states:

*   active - The map's camera automatically updates as the user's location changes, keeping the location dot in the center. This is the initial state, and the state upon clicking the `GeolocateControl` button.
*   passive - The user's location dot automatically updates, but the map's camera does not. Occurs upon the user initiating a map movement.
*   disabled - Occurs if geolocation is not available, disabled, or denied.

These interaction states can't be controlled programmatically. Instead, they are set based on user interactions.

Extends [Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)
.

new GeolocateControl(options: [Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object)
?)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [options](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-parameters-options)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) ?`(default `{}`) |     |
| #### [options.fitBoundsOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-parameters-options-fitboundsoptions)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) `(default `{maxZoom:15}`) | A [Map#fitBounds](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#fitbounds)<br> options object to use when the map is panned and zoomed to the user's location. The default is to use a `maxZoom` of 15 to limit how far the map will zoom in for very accurate locations. |
| #### [options.geolocation](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-parameters-options-geolocation)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) `(default `window.navigator.geolocation`) | `window.navigator.geolocation` by default; you can provide an object with the same shape to customize geolocation handling. |
| #### [options.positionOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-parameters-options-positionoptions)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) `(default `{enableHighAccuracy:false,timeout:6000}`) | A Geolocation API [PositionOptions](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/PositionOptions)<br> object. |
| #### [options.showAccuracyCircle](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-parameters-options-showaccuracycircle)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) `(default `true`) | By default, if `showUserLocation` is `true` , a transparent circle will be drawn around the user location indicating the accuracy (95% confidence level) of the user's location. Set to `false` to disable. Always disabled when `showUserLocation` is `false` . |
| #### [options.showUserHeading](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-parameters-options-showuserheading)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) `(default `false`) | If `true` an arrow will be drawn next to the user location dot indicating the device's heading. This only has affect when `trackUserLocation` is `true` . |
| #### [options.showUserLocation](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-parameters-options-showuserlocation)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) `(default `true`) | By default a dot will be shown on the map at the user's location. Set to `false` to disable. |
| #### [options.trackUserLocation](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-parameters-options-trackuserlocation)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) `(default `false`) | If `true` the `GeolocateControl` becomes a toggle button and when active the map will receive updates to the user's location as it changes. |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-example)

    map.addControl(new mapboxgl.GeolocateControl({    positionOptions: {        enableHighAccuracy: true    },    trackUserLocation: true,    showUserHeading: true}));

### [Instance Members](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#geolocatecontrol-instance-members)

()

caret-right

[IControl](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#icontrol)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/map.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/map.ts#L438-L5247)

Interface for interactive controls added to the map. This is a specification for implementers to model: it is not an exported method or class.

Controls must implement `onAdd` and `onRemove`, and must own an element, which is often a `div` element. To use Mapbox GL JS's default control styling, add the `mapboxgl-ctrl` class to your control's node.

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#icontrol-example)

    // Control implemented as ES6 classclass HelloWorldControl {    onAdd(map) {        this._map = map;        this._container = document.createElement('div');        this._container.className = 'mapboxgl-ctrl';        this._container.textContent = 'Hello, world';        return this._container;    }    onRemove() {        this._container.parentNode.removeChild(this._container);        this._map = undefined;    }}

    // Control implemented as ES5 prototypical classfunction HelloWorldControl() { }HelloWorldControl.prototype.onAdd = function(map) {    this._map = map;    this._container = document.createElement('div');    this._container.className = 'mapboxgl-ctrl';    this._container.textContent = 'Hello, world';    return this._container;};HelloWorldControl.prototype.onRemove = function () {    this._container.parentNode.removeChild(this._container);    this._map = undefined;};

### [Instance Members](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#icontrol-instance-members)

#### onAdd

()

caret-right

#### onRemove

()

caret-right

#### getDefaultPosition

()

caret-right

[Marker](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker)

-------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/marker.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/marker.ts#L82-L993)

Creates a marker component.

Extends [Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)
.

new Marker(options: [Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object)
?, legacyOptions: MarkerOptions?)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [options](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) ?` |     |
| #### [options.altitude](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-altitude)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) `(default `0`) | Elevation in meters above the map surface. If terrain is enabled, the marker will be elevated relative to the terrain. |
| #### [options.anchor](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-anchor)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) `(default `'center'`) | A string indicating the part of the Marker that should be positioned closest to the coordinate set via [Marker#setLngLat](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker#setlnglat)<br> . Options are `'center'` , `'top'` , `'bottom'` , `'left'` , `'right'` , `'top-left'` , `'top-right'` , `'bottom-left'` , and `'bottom-right'` . |
| #### [options.className](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-classname)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ?` | Space-separated CSS class names to add to marker element. |
| #### [options.clickTolerance](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-clicktolerance)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) `(default `0`) | The max number of pixels a user can shift the mouse pointer during a click on the marker for it to be considered a valid click (as opposed to a marker drag). The default is to inherit map's `clickTolerance` . |
| #### [options.color](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-color)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) `(default `'#3FB1CE'`) | The color to use for the default marker if `options.element` is not provided. The default is light blue. |
| #### [options.draggable](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-draggable)<br><br>`[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean) `(default `false`) | A boolean indicating whether or not a marker is able to be dragged to a new position on the map. |
| #### [options.element](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-element)<br><br>`[HTMLElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTML/Element) ?` | DOM element to use as a marker. The default is a light blue, droplet-shaped SVG marker. |
| #### [options.occludedOpacity](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-occludedopacity)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) `(default `0.2`) | The opacity of a marker that's occluded by 3D terrain. |
| #### [options.offset](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-offset)<br><br>`[PointLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#pointlike) ?` | The offset in pixels as a [PointLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#pointlike)<br> object to apply relative to the element's center. Negatives indicate left and up. |
| #### [options.pitchAlignment](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-pitchalignment)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) `(default `'auto'`) | `'map'` aligns the `Marker` to the plane of the map. `'viewport'` aligns the `Marker` to the plane of the viewport. `'auto'` automatically matches the value of `rotationAlignment` . |
| #### [options.rotation](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-rotation)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) `(default `0`) | The rotation angle of the marker in degrees, relative to its respective `rotationAlignment` setting. A positive value will rotate the marker clockwise. |
| #### [options.rotationAlignment](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-rotationalignment)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) `(default `'auto'`) | The alignment of the marker's rotation. `'map'` is aligned with the map plane, consistent with the cardinal directions as the map rotates. `'viewport'` is screenspace-aligned. `'horizon'` is aligned according to the nearest horizon, on non-globe projections it is equivalent to `'viewport'` . `'auto'` is equivalent to `'viewport'` . |
| #### [options.scale](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-options-scale)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) `(default `1`) | The scale to use for the default marker if `options.element` is not provided. The default scale corresponds to a height of `41px` and a width of `27px` . |
| #### [legacyOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-parameters-legacyoptions)<br><br>`MarkerOptions?` |     |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#marker-example)

    // Create a new marker.const marker = new mapboxgl.Marker()    .setLngLat([30.5, 50.5])    .addTo(map);

    // Set marker options.const marker = new mapboxgl.Marker({    color: "#FFFFFF",    draggable: true}).setLngLat([30.5, 50.5])    .addTo(map);

[NavigationControl](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#navigationcontrol)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/control/navigation\_control.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/control/navigation_control.ts#L41-L164)

A `NavigationControl` control contains zoom buttons and a compass. Add this control to a map using [Map#addControl](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#addcontrol)
.

new NavigationControl(options: [Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object)
?)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#navigationcontrol-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [options](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#navigationcontrol-parameters-options)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) ?`(default `{}`) |     |
| #### [options.showCompass](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#navigationcontrol-parameters-options-showcompass)<br><br>`[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean) `(default `true`) | If `true` the compass button is included. |
| #### [options.showZoom](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#navigationcontrol-parameters-options-showzoom)<br><br>`[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean) `(default `true`) | If `true` the zoom-in and zoom-out buttons are included. |
| #### [options.visualizePitch](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#navigationcontrol-parameters-options-visualizepitch)<br><br>`[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean) `(default `false`) | If `true` the pitch is visualized by rotating X-axis of compass. |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#navigationcontrol-example)

    const nav = new mapboxgl.NavigationControl();map.addControl(nav, 'top-left');

    const nav = new mapboxgl.NavigationControl({    visualizePitch: true});map.addControl(nav, 'bottom-right');

### [Related](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#navigationcontrol-related)

*   [Example: Display map navigation controls](https://www.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/navigation/)
    
*   [Example: Add a third party vector tile source](https://www.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/example/third-party/)

[Popup](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup)

-----------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/popup.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/popup.ts#L110-L697)

A popup component.

Extends [Evented](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/events/#evented)
.

new Popup(options: [Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object)
?)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [options](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-parameters-options)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) ?` |     |
| #### [options.altitude](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-parameters-options-altitude)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) `(default `0`) | Elevation in meters above the map surface. If terrain is enabled, the popup will be elevated relative to the terrain. |
| #### [options.anchor](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-parameters-options-anchor)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ?` | A string indicating the part of the popup that should be positioned closest to the coordinate, set via [Popup#setLngLat](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup#setlnglat)<br> . Options are `'center'` , `'top'` , `'bottom'` , `'left'` , `'right'` , `'top-left'` , `'top-right'` , `'bottom-left'` , and `'bottom-right'` . If unset, the anchor will be dynamically set to ensure the popup falls within the map container with a preference for `'bottom'` . |
| #### [options.className](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-parameters-options-classname)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) ?` | Space-separated CSS class names to add to popup container. |
| #### [options.closeButton](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-parameters-options-closebutton)<br><br>`[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean) `(default `true`) | If `true` , a close button will appear in the top right corner of the popup. |
| #### [options.closeOnClick](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-parameters-options-closeonclick)<br><br>`[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean) `(default `true`) | If `true` , the popup will close when the map is clicked. |
| #### [options.closeOnMove](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-parameters-options-closeonmove)<br><br>`[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean) `(default `false`) | If `true` , the popup will close when the map moves. |
| #### [options.focusAfterOpen](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-parameters-options-focusafteropen)<br><br>`[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Boolean) `(default `true`) | If `true` , the popup will try to focus the first focusable element inside the popup. |
| #### [options.maxWidth](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-parameters-options-maxwidth)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) `(default `'240px'`) | A string that sets the CSS property of the popup's maximum width (for example, `'300px'` ). To ensure the popup resizes to fit its content, set this property to `'none'` . See the MDN documentation for the list of [available values](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/max-width)<br> . |
| #### [options.offset](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-parameters-options-offset)<br><br>`([number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number)  \| [PointLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#pointlike)  \| [Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) )?` | A pixel offset applied to the popup's location specified as:<br><br>*   a single number specifying a distance from the popup's location<br>*   a [PointLike](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#pointlike)<br>     specifying a constant offset<br>*   an object of [Point](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/geography/#point)<br>    s specifing an offset for each anchor position.<br><br>Negative offsets indicate left and up. |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#popup-example)

    const markerHeight = 50;const markerRadius = 10;const linearOffset = 25;const popupOffsets = {    'top': [0, 0],    'top-left': [0, 0],    'top-right': [0, 0],    'bottom': [0, -markerHeight],    'bottom-left': [linearOffset, (markerHeight - markerRadius + linearOffset) * -1],    'bottom-right': [-linearOffset, (markerHeight - markerRadius + linearOffset) * -1],    'left': [markerRadius, (markerHeight - markerRadius) * -1],    'right': [-markerRadius, (markerHeight - markerRadius) * -1]};const popup = new mapboxgl.Popup({offset: popupOffsets, className: 'my-class'})    .setLngLat(e.lngLat)    .setHTML("<h1>Hello World!</h1>")    .setMaxWidth("300px")    .addTo(map);

[ScaleControl](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#scalecontrol)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[githubsrc/ui/control/scale\_control.ts](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js/blob/9fa8566c845bef0bea237c18d0f5386917bb1450/src/ui/control/scale_control.ts#L43-L144)

A `ScaleControl` control displays the ratio of a distance on the map to the corresponding distance on the ground. Add this control to a map using [Map#addControl](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/map/#map#addcontrol)
.

new ScaleControl(options: [Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object)
?)

### [Parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#scalecontrol-parameters)

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| #### [options](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#scalecontrol-parameters-options)<br><br>`[Object](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object) ?`(default `{}`) |     |
| #### [options.maxWidth](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#scalecontrol-parameters-options-maxwidth)<br><br>`[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number) `(default `'100'`) | The maximum length of the scale control in pixels. |
| #### [options.unit](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#scalecontrol-parameters-options-unit)<br><br>`[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String) `(default `'metric'`) | Unit of the distance ( `'imperial'` , `'metric'` or `'nautical'` ). |

### [Example](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#scalecontrol-example)

    const scale = new mapboxgl.ScaleControl({    maxWidth: 80,    unit: 'imperial'});map.addControl(scale);scale.setUnit('metric');

### [Instance Members](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/markers/#scalecontrol-instance-members)

#### setUnit

()

caret-right
